### ✅ `view`

**view the file at D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\project\projectIndexer.js.**

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\project\projectIndexer.js (lines 1-260)

<details>
<summary>81 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. const includeScanner = require('../parser/includeScanner');
7. const keywordScanner = require('../parser/keywordScanner');
8. const { ProjectGraph } = require('./projectGraph');
9. 
10. function resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, searchPaths) {
11.     for (const searchPath of searchPaths) {
12.         const fullPath = path.resolve(searchPath, fileName);
13.         if (fs.existsSync(fullPath)) return fullPath;
14.     }
15.     return null;
16. }
17. 
18. function addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, keywords) {
19.     for (const keywordEntry of keywords) {
20.         if (!keywordMap.has(keywordEntry.keyword)) keywordMap.set(keywordEntry.keyword, []);
21.         keywordMap.get(keywordEntry.keyword).push(keywordEntry);
22.     }
23. }
24. 
25. async function buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
26.     const files = [];
27.     const keywordMap = new Map();
28.     const graph = new ProjectGraph();
29.     const visited = new Set();
30. 
31.     async function visit(filePath, ancestry = []) {
32.         if (visited.has(filePath)) return;
33.         visited.add(filePath);
34.         files.push(filePath);
35.         graph.addFile(filePath);
36. 
37.         const keywords = await keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile(filePath);
38.         addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, keywords);
39. 
40.         const { includeEntries, searchPaths } = await includeScanner.collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(filePath);
41.         for (const { fileName } of includeEntries) {
42.             const resolvedPath = resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, searchPaths);
43.             if (!resolvedPath) {
44.                 graph.addMissingFile({
45.                     fromFile: filePath,
46.                     fileName,
47.                     filePath: path.resolve(searchPaths[0] || path.dirname(filePath), fileName),
48.                 });
49.                 continue;
50.             }
51. 
52.             if (ancestry.includes(resolvedPath) || resolvedPath === filePath) {
53.                 graph.addCycle({
54.                     fromFile: filePath,
55.                     toFile: resolvedPath,
56.                     path: [...ancestry, filePath, resolvedPath],
57.                 });
58.                 continue;
59.             }
60. 
61.             graph.addIncludeEdge(filePath, resolvedPath);
62.             await visit(resolvedPath, [...ancestry, filePath]);
63.         }
64.     }
65. 
66.     await visit(rootFile);
67. 
68.     return {
69.         rootFile,
70.         files,
71.         graph,
72.         keywordMap,
73.         missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
74.         cycles: graph.cycles,
75.     };
76. }
77. 
78. module.exports = {
79.     buildProjectIndex,
80. };
81.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 172m 33s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\client\services\indexClient.js.**

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\client\services\indexClient.js (lines 1-260)

<details>
<summary>157 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. function resolveRootFile(rootFile) {
7.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
8.         throw new TypeError('loadProjectSnapshot requires a rootFile path');
9.     }
10.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
11. }
12. 
13. function getRootCacheKey(rootFile) {
14.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
15.     return process.platform === 'win32'
16.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
17.         : resolvedRootFile;
18. }
19. 
20. function getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot) {
21.     if (Array.isArray(snapshot.files) && snapshot.files.length > 0) {
22.         return snapshot.files;
23.     }
24.     return [snapshot.rootFile];
25. }
26. 
27. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
28.     return left
29.         && right
30.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
31.         && left.size === right.size;
32. }
33. 
34. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
35.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
36.     return {
37.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
38.         size: stat.size,
39.     };
40. }
41. 
42. async function captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature) {
43.     const trackedFiles = [];
44.     const seen = new Set();
45. 
46.     for (const filePath of getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot)) {
47.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveRootFile(filePath);
48.         const fileCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
49.         if (seen.has(fileCacheKey)) continue;
50.         seen.add(fileCacheKey);
51.         trackedFiles.push({
52.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
53.             signature: await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath),
54.         });
55.     }
56. 
57.     return trackedFiles;
58. }
59. 
60. async function isSnapshotValid(entry, getFileSignature) {
61.     if (!Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles) || entry.trackedFiles.length === 0) {
62.         return false;
63.     }
64. 
65.     for (const trackedFile of entry.trackedFiles) {
66.         try {
67.             const currentSignature = await getFileSignature(trackedFile.filePath);
68.             if (!areFileSignaturesEqual(currentSignature, trackedFile.signature)) {
69.                 return false;
70.             }
71.         } catch (_error) {
72.             return false;
73.         }
74.     }
75. 
76.     return true;
77. }
78. 
79. function createIndexClient({ buildProjectIndex, getFileSignature = readFileSignature } = {}) {
80.     if (typeof buildProjectIndex !== 'function') {
81.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires a buildProjectIndex function');
82.     }
83.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
84.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires getFileSignature to be a function');
85.     }
86. 
87.     const snapshots = new Map();
88.     const generations = new Map();
89. 
90.     function getGeneration(rootCacheKey) {
91.         return generations.get(rootCacheKey) || 0;
92.     }
93. 
94.     async function loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile) {
95.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
96.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
97. 
98.         while (snapshots.has(rootCacheKey)) {
99.             const cachedEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
100.             if (cachedEntry.promise) return cachedEntry.promise;
101. 
102.             const valid = await isSnapshotValid(cachedEntry, getFileSignature);
103.             const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
104.             if (currentEntry !== cachedEntry) continue;
105.             if (valid) return cachedEntry.snapshot;
106. 
107.             invalidate(resolvedRootFile);
108.         }
109. 
110.         const generation = getGeneration(rootCacheKey);
111.         const promise = Promise.resolve(buildProjectIndex(resolvedRootFile))
112.             .then(async snapshot => {
113.                 let trackedFiles = null;
114.                 try {
115.                     trackedFiles = await captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature);
116.                 } catch (_error) {
117.                     trackedFiles = null;
118.                 }
119. 
120.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
121.                 if (getGeneration(rootCacheKey) === generation && currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
122.                     if (trackedFiles) {
123.                         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { snapshot, trackedFiles });
124.                     } else {
125.                         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
126.                     }
127.                 }
128.                 return snapshot;
129.             })
130.             .catch(error => {
131.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
132.                 if (currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
133.                     snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
134.                 }
135.                 throw error;
136.             });
137. 
138.         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise });
139.         return promise;
140.     }
141. 
142.     function invalidate(rootFile) {
143.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
144.         generations.set(rootCacheKey, getGeneration(rootCacheKey) + 1);
145.         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
146.     }
147. 
148.     return {
149.         invalidate,
150.         loadProjectSnapshot,
151.     };
152. }
153. 
154. module.exports = {
155.     createIndexClient,
156. };
157.
```

</details>

14.     }

22.     }
23. }
24. 
25. async function buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
26.     const files = [];
27.     const keywordMap = new Map();
28.     const graph = new ProjectGraph();
29.     const visited = new Set();
30. 
31.     async function visit(filePath, ancestry = []) {
32.         if (visited.has(filePath)) return;
33.         visited.add(filePath);
34.         files.push(filePath);
35.         graph.addFile(filePath);
36. 
37.         const keywords = await keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile(filePath);
38.         addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, keywords);
39. 
40.         const { includeEntries, searchPaths } = await includeScanner.collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(filePath);
41.         for (const { fileName } of includeEntries) {
42.             const resolvedPath = resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, searchPaths);
43.             if (!resolvedPath) {
44.                 graph.addMissingFile({
45.                     fromFile: filePath,
46.                     fileName,
47.                     filePath: path.resolve(searchPaths[0] || path.dirname(filePath), fileName),
48.                 });
49.                 continue;
50.             }
51. 
52.             if (ancestry.includes(resolvedPath) || resolvedPath === filePath) {
53.                 graph.addCycle({
54.                     fromFile: filePath,
55.                     toFile: resolvedPath,
56.                     path: [...ancestry, filePath, resolvedPath],
57.                 });
58.                 continue;
59.             }
60. 
61.             graph.addIncludeEdge(filePath, resolvedPath);
62.             await visit(resolvedPath, [...ancestry, filePath]);
63.         }
64.     }
65. 
66.     await visit(rootFile);
67. 
68.     return {
69.         rootFile,
70.         files,
71.         graph,
72.         keywordMap,
73.         missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
74.         cycles: graph.cycles,
75.     };
76. }
77. 
78. module.exports = {
79.     buildProjectIndex,
80. };
81.
```

</details>

1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. function resolveRootFile(rootFile) {
7.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
8.         throw new TypeError('loadProjectSnapshot requires a rootFile path');
9.     }
10.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
11. }
12. 
13. function getRootCacheKey(rootFile) {
14.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
15.     return process.platform === 'win32'
16.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
17.         : resolvedRootFile;
18. }
19. 
20. function getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot) {
21.     if (Array.isArray(snapshot.files) && snapshot.files.length > 0) {
22.         return snapshot.files;
23.     }
24.     return [snapshot.rootFile];
25. }
26. 
27. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
28.     return left
29.         && right
30.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
31.         && left.size === right.size;
32. }
33. 
34. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
35.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
36.     return {
37.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
38.         size: stat.size,
39.     };
40. }
41. 
42. async function captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature) {
43.     const trackedFiles = [];
44.     const seen = new Set();
45. 
46.     for (const filePath of getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot)) {
47.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveRootFile(filePath);
48.         const fileCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
49.         if (seen.has(fileCacheKey)) continue;
50.         seen.add(fileCacheKey);
51.         trackedFiles.push({
52.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
53.             signature: await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath),
54.         });
55.     }
56. 
57.     return trackedFiles;
58. }
59. 
60. async function isSnapshotValid(entry, getFileSignature) {
61.     if (!Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles) || entry.trackedFiles.length === 0) {
62.         return false;
63.     }
64. 
65.     for (const trackedFile of entry.trackedFiles) {
66.         try {
67.             const currentSignature = await getFileSignature(trackedFile.filePath);
68.             if (!areFileSignaturesEqual(currentSignature, trackedFile.signature)) {
69.                 return false;
70.             }
71.         } catch (_error) {
72.             return false;
73.         }
74.     }
75. 
76.     return true;
77. }
78. 
79. function createIndexClient({ buildProjectIndex, getFileSignature = readFileSignature } = {}) {
80.     if (typeof buildProjectIndex !== 'function') {
81.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires a buildProjectIndex function');
82.     }
83.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
84.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires getFileSignature to be a function');
85.     }
86. 
87.     const snapshots = new Map();
88.     const generations = new Map();
89. 
90.     function getGeneration(rootCacheKey) {
91.         return generations.get(rootCacheKey) || 0;
92.     }
93. 
94.     async function loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile) {
95.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
96.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
97. 
98.         while (snapshots.has(rootCacheKey)) {
99.             const cachedEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
100.             if (cachedEntry.promise) return cachedEntry.promise;
101. 
102.             const valid = await isSnapshotValid(cachedEntry, getFileSignature);
103.             const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
104.             if (currentEntry !== cachedEntry) continue;
105.             if (valid) return cachedEntry.snapshot;
106. 
107.             invalidate(resolvedRootFile);
108.         }
109. 
110.         const generation = getGeneration(rootCacheKey);
111.         const promise = Promise.resolve(buildProjectIndex(resolvedRootFile))
112.             .then(async snapshot => {
113.                 let trackedFiles = null;
114.                 try {
115.                     trackedFiles = await captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature);
116.                 } catch (_error) {
117.                     trackedFiles = null;
118.                 }
119. 
120.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
121.                 if (getGeneration(rootCacheKey) === generation && currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
122.                     if (trackedFiles) {
123.                         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { snapshot, trackedFiles });
124.                     } else {
125.                         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
126.                     }
127.                 }
128.                 return snapshot;
129.             })
130.             .catch(error => {
131.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
132.                 if (currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
133.                     snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
134.                 }
135.                 throw error;
136.             });
137. 
138.         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise });
139.         return promise;
140.     }
141. 
142.     function invalidate(rootFile) {
143.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
144.         generations.set(rootCacheKey, getGeneration(rootCacheKey) + 1);
145.         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
146.     }
147. 
148.     return {
149.         invalidate,
150.         loadProjectSnapshot,
151.     };
152. }
153. 
154. module.exports = {
155.     createIndexClient,
156. };
157.
```

</details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. class ProjectGraph {
4.     constructor() {
5.         this.children = new Map();
6.         this.includeEntries = new Map();
7.         this.parents = new Map();
8.         this.missingFiles = [];
9.         this.cycles = [];
10.     }
11. 
12.     addFile(filePath) {
13.         if (!this.children.has(filePath)) this.children.set(filePath, []);
14.         if (!this.includeEntries.has(filePath)) this.includeEntries.set(filePath, []);
15.         if (!this.parents.has(filePath)) this.parents.set(filePath, []);
16.     }
17. 
18.     addIncludeEntry(fromFile, entry) {
19.         this.addFile(fromFile);
20.         const entries = this.includeEntries.get(fromFile);
21.         if (entry.missing) {
22.             entries.push(entry);
23.             return;
24.         }
25.         const exists = entries.some(candidate =>
26.             candidate.filePath === entry.filePath && Boolean(candidate.missing) === Boolean(entry.missing)
27.         );
28.         if (!exists) {
29.             entries.push(entry);
30.         }
31.     }
32. 
33.     addIncludeEdge(fromFile, toFile) {
34.         this.addFile(fromFile);
35.         this.addFile(toFile);
36. 
37.         if (!this.children.get(fromFile).includes(toFile)) {
38.             this.children.get(fromFile).push(toFile);
39.         }
40.         if (!this.parents.get(toFile).includes(fromFile)) {
41.             this.parents.get(toFile).push(fromFile);
42.         }
43. 
44.         this.addIncludeEntry(fromFile, { filePath: toFile });
45.     }
46. 
47.     addMissingFile(record) {
48.         this.missingFiles.push(record);
49.         if (record.fromFile && record.filePath) {
50.             this.addIncludeEntry(record.fromFile, {
51.                 filePath: record.filePath,
52.                 fileName: record.fileName,
53.                 missing: true,
54.             });
55.         }
56.     }
57. 
58.     addCycle(record) {
59.         this.cycles.push(record);
60.     }
61. 
62.     getChildren(filePath) {
63.         return [...(this.children.get(filePath) || [])];
64.     }
65. 
66.     getParents(filePath) {
67.         return [...(this.parents.get(filePath) || [])];
68.     }
69. 
70.     getIncludeEntries(filePath) {
71.         return [...(this.includeEntries.get(filePath) || [])];
72.     }
73. 
74.     toTree(rootFile, ancestry = []) {
75.         if (ancestry.includes(rootFile)) {
76.             return {
77.                 filePath: rootFile,
78.                 children: [],
79.             };
80.         }
81. 
82.         return {
83.             filePath: rootFile,
84.             children: this.getIncludeEntries(rootFile).map(entry => (
85.                 entry.missing
86.                     ? { ...entry, children: [] }
87.                     : this.toTree(entry.filePath, [...ancestry, rootFile])
88.             )),
89.         };
90.     }
91. }
92. 
93. module.exports = {
94.     ProjectGraph,
95. };
96.
```

</details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. const readline = require('readline');
6. 
7. const STREAM_SCAN_YIELD_INTERVAL = 50000;
8. 
9. function createIncludeDirectiveState(basePath) {
10.     return {
11.         basePath,
12.         keyword: '',
13.         cardCount: 0,
14.         includeEntries: [],
15.         searchPaths: [basePath],
16.         pendingInclude: null,
17.     };
18. }
19. 
20. function getIncludeDirectiveRule(keyword) {
21.     if (keyword === '*INCLUDE') {
22.         return { repeatable: true, filenameCard: 1 };
23.     }
24.     if (keyword.startsWith('*INCLUDE_MULTISCALE_SPOTWELD')) {
25.         return { repeatable: false, filenameCard: 2 };
26.     }
27.     if (keyword.startsWith('*INCLUDE') && !keyword.startsWith('*INCLUDE_PATH')) {
28.         return { repeatable: false, filenameCard: 1 };
29.     }
30.     return null;
31. }
32. 
33. function createIncludeSegment(line, lineIndex) {
34.     const trimmed = line.trim();
35.     return {
36.         lineIndex,
37.         startChar: line.indexOf(trimmed),
38.         endChar: line.trimEnd().length,
39.     };
40. }
41. 
42. function startIncludeEntry(line, lineIndex) {
43.     const trimmed = line.trim();
44.     const segment = createIncludeSegment(line, lineIndex);
45.     return {
46.         lineIndex: segment.lineIndex,
47.         startChar: segment.startChar,
48.         endLineIndex: segment.lineIndex,
49.         endChar: segment.endChar,
50.         parts: [trimmed.endsWith(' +') ? trimmed.slice(0, -2) : trimmed],
51.         segments: [segment],
52.         awaitingContinuation: trimmed.endsWith(' +'),
53.     };
54. }
55. 
56. function appendIncludeEntry(entry, line, lineIndex) {
57.     const trimmed = line.trim();
58.     const segment = createIncludeSegment(line, lineIndex);
59.     entry.parts.push(trimmed.endsWith(' +') ? trimmed.slice(0, -2) : trimmed);
60.     entry.segments.push(segment);
61.     entry.endLineIndex = segment.lineIndex;
62.     entry.endChar = segment.endChar;
63.     entry.awaitingContinuation = trimmed.endsWith(' +');
64. }
65. 
66. function includeEntryContainsLine(entry, lineIndex) {
67.     return (entry.segments || []).some(segment => segment.lineIndex === lineIndex);
68. }
69. 
70. function getIncludeEntryRanges(entry) {
71.     if (!entry.segments || entry.segments.length === 0) {
72.         return [{
73.             lineIndex: entry.lineIndex,
74.             startChar: entry.startChar,
75.             endLineIndex: entry.endLineIndex,
76.             endChar: entry.endChar,
77.         }];
78.     }
79. 
80.     const [firstSegment, ...remainingSegments] = entry.segments;
81.     const ranges = [{
82.         lineIndex: firstSegment.lineIndex,
83.         startChar: firstSegment.startChar,
84.         endLineIndex: firstSegment.lineIndex,
85.         endChar: firstSegment.endChar,
86.     }];
87. 
88.     for (const segment of remainingSegments) {
89.         const currentRange = ranges[ranges.length - 1];
90.         if (segment.lineIndex === currentRange.endLineIndex + 1) {
91.             currentRange.endLineIndex = segment.lineIndex;
92.             currentRange.endChar = segment.endChar;
93.             continue;
94.         }
95. 
96.         ranges.push({
97.             lineIndex: segment.lineIndex,
98.             startChar: segment.startChar,
99.             endLineIndex: segment.lineIndex,
100.             endChar: segment.endChar,
101.         });
102.     }
103. 
104.     return ranges;
105. }
106. 
107. function flushIncludeEntry(state) {
108.     if (!state.pendingInclude) return;
109.     const fileName = state.pendingInclude.parts.join('').trim();
110.     if (fileName) {
111.         const { lineIndex, startChar, endLineIndex, endChar, segments } = state.pendingInclude;
112.         state.includeEntries.push({ lineIndex, startChar, endLineIndex, endChar, fileName, segments });
113.     }
114.     state.pendingInclude = null;
115. }
116. 
117. function processIncludeDirectiveLine(state, line, lineIndex) {
118.     const trimmed = line.trim();
119. 
120.     if (line.startsWith('*')) {
121.         flushIncludeEntry(state);
122.         state.keyword = trimmed;
123.         state.cardCount = 0;
124.         return;
125.     }
126. 
127.     if (state.pendingInclude) {
128.         if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('$')) return;
129.         appendIncludeEntry(state.pendingInclude, line, lineIndex);
130.         if (!state.pendingInclude.awaitingContinuation) {
131.             flushIncludeEntry(state);
132.         }
133.         return;
134.     }
135. 
136.     if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('$')) return;
137. 
138.     if (state.keyword === '*INCLUDE_PATH') {
139.         state.searchPaths.push(trimmed);
140.         return;
141.     }
142.     if (state.keyword === '*INCLUDE_PATH_RELATIVE') {
143.         state.searchPaths.push(path.resolve(state.basePath, trimmed));
144.         return;
145.     }
146. 
147.     const includeRule = getIncludeDirectiveRule(state.keyword);
148.     if (!includeRule) return;
149. 
150.     state.cardCount++;
151.     if (includeRule.repeatable || state.cardCount === includeRule.filenameCard) {
152.         state.pendingInclude = startIncludeEntry(line, lineIndex);
153.         if (!state.pendingInclude.awaitingContinuation) {
154.             flushIncludeEntry(state);
155.         }
156.     }
157. }
158. 
159. function finalizeIncludeDirectiveState(state) {
160.     flushIncludeEntry(state);
161.     return { includeEntries: state.includeEntries, searchPaths: state.searchPaths };
162. }
163. 
164. function collectIncludeDirectivesFromLineReader(lineCount, getLine, basePath) {
165.     const state = createIncludeDirectiveState(basePath);
166.     for (let i = 0; i < lineCount; i++) {
167.         processIncludeDirectiveLine(state, getLine(i), i);
168.     }
169.     return finalizeIncludeDirectiveState(state);
170. }
171. 
172. async function collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(filePath) {
173.     const state = createIncludeDirectiveState(path.dirname(filePath));
174.     const stream = fs.createReadStream(filePath, { encoding: 'utf8' });
175.     const lines = readline.createInterface({ input: stream, crlfDelay: Infinity });
176.     let lineIndex = 0;
177. 
178.     try {
179.         for await (const line of lines) {
180.             processIncludeDirectiveLine(state, line, lineIndex);
181.             lineIndex++;
182.             if (lineIndex % STREAM_SCAN_YIELD_INTERVAL === 0) await new Promise(r => setImmediate(r));
183.         }
184.     } finally {
185.         lines.close();
186.     }
187. 
188.     return finalizeIncludeDirectiveState(state);
189. }
190. 
191. module.exports = {
192.     collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile,
193.     collectIncludeDirectivesFromLineReader,
194.     getIncludeEntryRanges,
195.     includeEntryContainsLine,
196. };
197.
```

</details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const readline = require('readline');
5. 
6. const STREAM_SCAN_YIELD_INTERVAL = 50000;
7. 
8. function collectKeywordsFromLineReader(lineCount, getLine, filePath) {
9.     const keywords = [];
10.     for (let i = 0; i < lineCount; i++) {
11.         const trimmed = getLine(i).trim();
12.         if (!trimmed.startsWith('*')) continue;
13.         const keyword = trimmed.slice(1);
14.         if (!keyword) continue;
15.         keywords.push({ keyword, filePath, lineIndex: i });
16.     }
17.     return keywords;
18. }
19. 
20. async function collectKeywordsFromFile(filePath) {
21.     const stream = fs.createReadStream(filePath, { encoding: 'utf8' });
22.     const rl = readline.createInterface({ input: stream, crlfDelay: Infinity });
23.     const keywords = [];
24.     let lineIndex = 0;
25. 
26.     try {
27.         for await (const line of rl) {
28.             const trimmed = line.trim();
29.             if (trimmed.startsWith('*')) {
30.                 const keyword = trimmed.slice(1);
31.                 if (keyword) keywords.push({ keyword, filePath, lineIndex });
32.             }
33.             lineIndex++;
34.             if (lineIndex % STREAM_SCAN_YIELD_INTERVAL === 0) await new Promise(r => setImmediate(r));
35.         }
36.     } finally {
37.         rl.close();
38.     }
39. 
40.     await new Promise(r => setImmediate(r));
41.     return keywords;
42. }
43. 
44. module.exports = {
45.     collectKeywordsFromFile,
46.     collectKeywordsFromLineReader,
47. };
48.
```

</details>

1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const assert = require('assert');
4. const fs = require('fs');
5. const os = require('os');
6. const path = require('path');
7. 
8. const { buildProjectIndex } = require('../../../src/core/project/projectIndexer');
9. 
10. describe('projectIndexer', () => {
11.     it('recursively aggregates included files and keyword usages into one project snapshot', async () => {
12.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
13.         const submodelsDir = path.join(tempRoot, 'submodels');
14.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
15.         const aFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'a.key');
16.         const bFile = path.join(submodelsDir, 'b.key');
17. 
18.         fs.mkdirSync(submodelsDir);
19.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE_PATH_RELATIVE\nsubmodels\n*INCLUDE\na.key\nb.key\n', 'utf8');
20.         fs.writeFileSync(aFile, '*PART\npart line\n', 'utf8');
21.         fs.writeFileSync(bFile, '*MAT_ELASTIC\nmat line\n', 'utf8');
22. 
23.         try {
24.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
25. 
26.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.files.sort(), [rootFile, aFile, bFile].sort());
27.             assert.deepEqual(
28.                 snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath),
29.                 [aFile]
30.             );
31.             assert.deepEqual(
32.                 snapshot.keywordMap.get('MAT_ELASTIC').map(entry => entry.filePath),
33.                 [bFile]
34.             );
35.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.missingFiles, []);
36.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.cycles, []);
37.         } finally {
38.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
39.         }
40.     });
41. 
42.     it('records missing includes without aborting the rest of the project scan', async () => {
43.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
44.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
45. 
46.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nmissing.key\n*PART\n', 'utf8');
47. 
48.         try {
49.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
50. 
51.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.files, [rootFile]);
52.             assert.equal(snapshot.missingFiles.length, 1);
53.             assert.equal(snapshot.missingFiles[0].fromFile, rootFile);
54.             assert.equal(snapshot.missingFiles[0].fileName, 'missing.key');
55.             assert.deepEqual(
56.                 snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath),
57.                 [rootFile]
58.             );
59.         } finally {
60.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
61.         }
62.     });
63. 
64.     it('builds a project graph with forward and reverse include edges', async () => {
65.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
66.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
67.         const childFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'child.key');
68. 
69.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nchild.key\n', 'utf8');
70.         fs.writeFileSync(childFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
71. 
72.         try {
73.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
74. 
75.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.getChildren(rootFile), [childFile]);
76.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.getParents(childFile), [rootFile]);
77.         } finally {
78.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
79.         }
80.     });
81. 
82.     it('materializes a nested include tree from the project graph in include order', async () => {
83.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
84.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
85.         const aFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'a.key');
86.         const bFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'b.key');
87.         const cFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'c.key');
88. 
89.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\na.key\nb.key\n', 'utf8');
90.         fs.writeFileSync(aFile, '*INCLUDE\nc.key\n', 'utf8');
91.         fs.writeFileSync(bFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
92.         fs.writeFileSync(cFile, '*MAT_ELASTIC\n', 'utf8');
93. 
94.         try {
95.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
96. 
97.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
98.                 filePath: rootFile,
99.                 children: [
100.                     {
101.                         filePath: aFile,
102.                         children: [
103.                             {
104.                                 filePath: cFile,
105.                                 children: [],
106.                             },
107.                         ],
108.                     },
109.                     {
110.                         filePath: bFile,
111.                         children: [],
112.                     },
113.                 ],
114.             });
115.         } finally {
116.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
117.         }
118.     });
119. 
120.     it('preserves missing includes as tree nodes in include order', async () => {

150.                     },

152.             });

6. describe('createIndexClient', () => {

150.             },

154. 
155.         signatures.delete(childFile);
156.

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. const vscode = require('vscode');
6. 
7. const keywordScanner = require('../../core/parser/keywordScanner');
8. 
9. class KeywordItem extends vscode.TreeItem {
10.     constructor(keyword) {
11.         super(keyword, vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed);
12.         this.children = [];
13.         this.iconPath = new vscode.ThemeIcon('symbol-keyword');
14.     }
15. }
16. 
17. class KeywordUsageItem extends vscode.TreeItem {
18.     constructor(filePath, lineIndex, rootDir) {
19.         const rel = path.relative(rootDir, filePath);
20.         super(`${rel}  :${lineIndex + 1}`, vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.None);
21.         this.iconPath = new vscode.ThemeIcon('file');
22.         this.tooltip = `${filePath}:${lineIndex + 1}`;
23.         this.command = {
24.             command: 'extension.goToKeywordUsage',
25.             title: 'Go to keyword',
26.             arguments: [filePath, lineIndex],
27.         };
28.     }
29. }
30. 
31. class LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider {
32.     constructor({ collectIncludeFiles, loadProjectSnapshot, shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan } = {}) {
33.         this.collectIncludeFiles = collectIncludeFiles;
34.         this.loadProjectSnapshot = loadProjectSnapshot;
35.         this.shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan = shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan;
36.         this._onDidChangeTreeData = new vscode.EventEmitter();
37.         this.onDidChangeTreeData = this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
38.         this.roots = [];
39.         this._mode = 'local'; // 'local' | 'recursive'
40.     }
41. 
42.     _setMode(mode) {
43.         this._mode = mode;
44.         vscode.commands.executeCommand('setContext', 'lsdyna.keywordIndexMode', mode);
45.     }
46. 
47.     _buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir) {
48.         return [...keywordMap.entries()]
49.             .sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b))
50.             .map(([keyword, usages]) => {
51.                 const item = new KeywordItem(keyword);
52.                 item.children = usages.map(({ filePath, lineIndex }) =>
53.                     new KeywordUsageItem(filePath, lineIndex, rootDir)
54.                 );
55.                 return item;
56.             });
57.     }
58. 
59.     async _buildRootsAsync(filePaths, rootDir) {
60.         const keywordMap = new Map();
61.         for (const filePath of filePaths) {
62.             if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) continue;
63.             const keywords = await keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile(filePath);
64.             for (const { keyword, lineIndex } of keywords) {
65.                 if (!keywordMap.has(keyword)) keywordMap.set(keyword, []);
66.                 keywordMap.get(keyword).push({ filePath, lineIndex });
67.             }
68.         }
69.         return this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir);
70.     }
71. 
72.     _buildRootsFromSnapshot(snapshot, rootDir) {
73.         return this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(snapshot.keywordMap, rootDir);
74.     }
75. 
76.     refreshFromDocument(document) {
77.         if (this._mode !== 'local') return;
78.         if (!document || document.languageId !== 'lsdyna') return;
79.         if (this.shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) {
80.             this.roots = [];
81.             this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
82.             return;
83.         }
84.         const filePath = document.uri.fsPath;
85.         const rootDir = path.dirname(filePath);
86.         const keywordMap = new Map();
87.         for (const { keyword, lineIndex } of keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromLineReader(
88.             document.lineCount,
89.             i => document.lineAt(i).text,
90.             filePath
91.         )) {
92.             if (!keywordMap.has(keyword)) keywordMap.set(keyword, []);
93.             keywordMap.get(keyword).push({ filePath, lineIndex });
94.         }
95.         this.roots = this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir);
96.         this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
97.     }
98. 
99.     async scan() {
100.         const editor = vscode.window.activeTextEditor;
101.         if (!editor || editor.document.languageId !== 'lsdyna') {
102.             vscode.window.showWarningMessage('Open an LS-DYNA file first.');
103.             return;
104.         }
105.         const rootFile = editor.document.uri.fsPath;
106.         const rootDir = path.dirname(rootFile);
107.         await vscode.window.withProgress(
108.             { location: vscode.ProgressLocation.Notification, title: 'Scanning keywords…', cancellable: false },
109.             async (progress) => {
110.                 if (this.loadProjectSnapshot) {
111.                     const snapshot = await this.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
112.                     this.roots = this._buildRootsFromSnapshot(snapshot, rootDir);
113.                 } else {
114.                     const files = await this.collectIncludeFiles(rootFile, (count) => {
115.                         progress.report({ message: `${count} file${count === 1 ? '' : 's'} found` });
116.                     });
117.                     this.roots = await this._buildRootsAsync(files, rootDir);
118.                 }
119.                 this._setMode('recursive');
120.                 this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
121.             }
122.         );
123.     }
124. 
125.     setLocal() {
126.         this._setMode('local');
127.         if (vscode.window.activeTextEditor) {
128.             this.refreshFromDocument(vscode.window.activeTextEditor.document);
129.         } else {
130.             this.roots = [];
131.             this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
132.         }
133.     }
134. 
135.     getTreeItem(element) { return element; }
136. 
137.     getChildren(element) {
138.         if (element) return element.children;
139.         return this.roots;
140.     }
141. }
142. 
143. module.exports = {
144.     LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider,
145. };
146.
```

</details>

1. const vscode = require('vscode');
2. const path = require('path');
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const readline = require('readline');
5. const { LsdynaIncludeTreeProvider } = require('./client/providers/includeTreeProvider');
6. const { LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider } = require('./client/providers/keywordIndexProvider');
7. const { createIndexClient } = require('./client/services/indexClient');
8. const includeScanner = require('./core/parser/includeScanner');
9. const keywordScanner = require('./core/parser/keywordScanner');
10. const { buildProjectIndex } = require('./core/project/projectIndexer');
11. 
12. const LARGE_DOCUMENT_LINE_THRESHOLD = 100000;
13. const STREAM_SCAN_YIELD_INTERVAL = 50000;
14. const includeDirectiveCache = new WeakMap();
15. 
16. // --- Folding ---
17. 
18. class LsDynaFoldingProvider {
19.     provideFoldingRanges(document) {
20.         if (shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) return [];
21. 
22.         const ranges = [];
23.         let foldStart = -1;
24. 
25.         for (let i = 0; i < document.lineCount; i++) {
26.             if (/^\*/.test(document.lineAt(i).text)) {
27.                 if (foldStart !== -1 && i - 1 > foldStart) {
28.                     ranges.push(new vscode.FoldingRange(foldStart, i - 1));
29.                 }
30.                 foldStart = i;
31.             }
32.         }
33. 
34.         if (foldStart !== -1 && document.lineCount - 1 > foldStart) {
35.             ranges.push(new vscode.FoldingRange(foldStart, document.lineCount - 1));
36.         }
37. 
38.         return ranges;
39.     }
40. }
41. 
42. // --- Symbol Provider ---
43. 
44. class LsdynaKeywordSymbolProvider {
45.     provideDocumentSymbols(document) {
46.         if (shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) return [];
47. 
48.         const symbols = [];
49.         for (let i = 0; i < document.lineCount; i++) {
50.             const line = document.lineAt(i);
51.             if (line.text.startsWith('*')) {
52.                 symbols.push(new vscode.DocumentSymbol(
53.                     line.text.trim(),
54.                     '',
55.                     vscode.SymbolKind.Property,
56.                     line.range,
57.                     line.range
58.                 ));
59.             }
60.         }
61.         return symbols;
62.     }
63. }
64. 
65. // --- Document Link Provider ---
66. 
67. class LsdynaDocumentLinkProvider {
68.     provideDocumentLinks(document) {
69.         return collectIncludeDocumentLinks(document);
70.     }
71. }
72. 
73. // --- Helpers ---
74. 
75. function getIncludeDirectiveData(document) {
76.     const version = document.version ?? null;
77.     const cached = includeDirectiveCache.get(document);
78.     if (cached && cached.version === version) {
79.         return cached.value;
80.     }
```

1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. // Intercept require('vscode') before any module tries to load it
4. const Module = require('module');
5. const vscodeMock = require('./vscode-mock');
6. const _load = Module._load;
7. Module._load = function (request, parent, isMain) {
8.     if (request === 'vscode') return vscodeMock;
9.     return _load.call(this, request, parent, isMain);
10. };
11. 
12. const { Range, Position } = vscodeMock;
13. 
14. /**
15.  * Creates a minimal fake TextDocument from a string.
16.  * Supports getText(), lineAt(), lineCount, getWordRangeAtPosition(), and uri.fsPath.
17.  */
18. function fakeDoc(text, fsPath = '/test/file.k') {
19.     const lines = text.split('\n');
20.     return {
21.         getText(range) {
22.             if (!range) return text;
23.             const { start, end } = range;
24.             if (start.line === end.line) return lines[start.line].slice(start.character, end.character);
25.             return lines.slice(start.line, end.line + 1)
26.                 .map((l, i, a) => i === 0 ? l.slice(start.character) : i === a.length - 1 ? l.slice(0, end.character) : l)
27.                 .join('\n');
28.         },
29.         lineCount: lines.length,
30.         uri: { fsPath },
31.         lineAt: i => ({ text: lines[i], range: new Range(i, 0, i, lines[i].length) }),
32.         getWordRangeAtPosition(position, regex) {
33.             const line = lines[position.line];
34.             if (!line) return undefined;
35.             const re = new RegExp(regex.source, 'g');
36.             let match;
37.             while ((match = re.exec(line)) !== null) {
38.                 const start = match.index;
39.                 const end = start + match[0].length;
40.                 if (position.character >= start && position.character <= end) {
41.                     return new Range(position.line, start, position.line, end);
42.                 }
43.             }
44.             return undefined;
45.         },
46.     };
47. }
48. 
49. module.exports = { fakeDoc, vscodeMock };
50.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 177m 3s</sub>

### ℹ️ Notification

Background agent "Critique file reuse plan" (rubber-duck) completed

<details>
<summary>Detail</summary>

Critique this planned next batch for D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna on branch dev.

Current state:
- Phase 2 is largely complete: parser -> projectIndexer/projectGraph -> indexClient -> providers.
- Phase 3 now has:
  1. project snapshot L1 cache in `src/client/services/indexClient.js`
  2. explicit `invalidate(rootFile)`
  3. stale in-flight protection
  4. automatic snapshot invalidation by tracked file signature (`mtimeMs` + `size`) using `snapshot.files`
- Remaining Phase 3 gap: when any tracked file changes, the snapshot is correctly invalidated, but the rebuild still rescans every file in the project. The rollout plan explicitly wants reuse of unchanged files and fewer rescans after a child file changes.

Proposed implementation batch:
1. Evolve `src/core/project/projectIndexer.js` from a stateless function into a small stateful factory that can cache per-file scan results across builds.
2. Cache a minimal `FileScanResult` keyed by normalized `filePath + mtimeMs + size` (at least includes + keyword summary), so unchanged files can be reused on later builds.
3. Keep project graph aggregation per-build, but source its inputs from reused file scan results when signatures match.
4. Expose minimal snapshot stats such as `scannedFileCount` and `reusedFileCount` so tests and docs can prove the behavior.
5. Keep `indexClient` API stable; just inject/use a long-lived `buildProjectIndex` implementation that now has internal file reuse.
6. Validate with the user's real project at `D:\temp\LSDYNA\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\combine.key`, especially touching a small included file and confirming reuse counts on reload.

Constraints:
- Use strict TDD.
- Prefer minimal scope; do not introduce worker_threads, manifest, or eviction yet.
- Must preserve existing snapshot shape/consumers, only additive changes are okay.
- Enterprise robustness matters more than maximizing reuse.

Please critique for correctness risks, architecture blind spots, race/consistency issues, and test strategy. Tell me the best minimal shape for this batch and what to avoid.

</details>

11.     it('recursively aggregates included files and keyword usages into one project snapshot', async () => {
12.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
13.         const submodelsDir = path.join(tempRoot, 'submodels');

18.         fs.mkdirSync(submodelsDir);

25.

34.             );

process.platform === 'win32'\n+        ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()\n+        : resolvedFilePath;\n+}\n+\n+function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {\n+    return left\n+        && right\n+ for (const keywordEntry of keywords) {\n+        if (!keywordMap.has(keywordEntry.keyword)) keywordMap.set(keywordEntry.keyword, []);\n+        keywordMap.get(keywordEntry.keyword).push(keywordEntry);\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+function createProjectIndexer({\n+    collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile = includeScanner.collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile,\n+ requires getFileSignature to be a function');\n+    }\n+\n+    const fileScanCache = new Map();\n+\n+    async function loadFileScan(filePath, stats) {\n+        const resolvedFilePath = cachedEntry.scanResult;\n+        }\n+\n+        const keywords = await collectKeywordsFromFile(resolvedFilePath);\n+        const { includeEntries, searchPaths } = await collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(resolvedFilePath);\n+        const scanResult = {\n+            filePath: resolvedFilePath,\n+            keywords,\n+            includeEntries,\n+            searchPaths,\n+        };\n+\n+        fileScanCache.set(fileCacheKey, {\n+            signature,\n+            scanResult,\n+        });\n+        stats.scannedFileCount += 1;\n+        return scanResult;\n+    }\n+\n+    async function buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {\n+        const resolvedRootFile = resolveProjectFile(rootFile);\n+        const files = [];\n+        const keywordMap = new Map();\n+        const graph = new ProjectGraph();\n+        const visited = new Set();\n+        const stats = {\n+            scannedFileCount: 0,\n+            reusedFileCount: 0,\n+        };\n+\n+        async function visit(filePath, ancestry = []) {\n+            const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);\n+            if (visited.has(resolvedFilePath)) return;\n+            visited.add(resolvedFilePath);\n+            files.push(resolvedFilePath);\n+            graph.addFile(resolvedFilePath);\n+\n+            const scanResult = await loadFileScan(resolvedFilePath, stats);\n+            addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, scanResult.keywords);\n+\n+            for (const { fileName } of scanResult.includeEntries) {\n+                const resolvedPath = resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, scanResult.searchPaths);\n+                if (!resolvedPath) {\n+                    graph.addMissingFile({\n+                        fromFile: resolvedFilePath,\n+                        fileName,\n+                        filePath: path.resolve(scanResult.searchPaths[0] || path.dirname(resolvedFilePath), fileName),\n+                    });\n+                    continue;\n+                }\n+\n+                if (ancestry.includes(resolvedPath) || resolvedPath === resolvedFilePath) {\n+                    graph.addCycle({\n+                        fromFile: resolvedFilePath,\n+                        toFile: resolvedPath,\n+                        path: [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath],\n+                    });\n+                    continue;\n+                }\n+\n+                graph.addIncludeEdge(resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath);\n+                await visit(resolvedPath, [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath]);\n+            }\n+        }\n+\n+        await visit(resolvedRootFile);\n+\n+        return {\n+            rootFile: resolvedRootFile,\n+            files,\n+            graph,\n+            keywordMap,\n+            missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,\n+            cycles: graph.cycles,\n+            stats,\n+        };\n+    }\n+\n+    return {\n+        buildProjectIndex,\n+    };\n+}\n+\n+const defaultProjectIndexer = createProjectIndexer();\n+\n+module.exports = {\n+    buildProjectIndex: defaultProjectIndexer.buildProjectIndex,\n+    createProjectIndexer,\n+};\n*** End Patch\n"
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 616m 50s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase3_project_indexer_file_reuse.md\n+# vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 3：projectIndexer 单文件扫描复用）\n+\n+## 1. 当前进度定位\n+\n+截至本轮开始前，整体进度已经推进到：\n+\n+- **Phase 2 已基本打通**\n+  - parser 抽取完成\n+  - `projectIndexer` / `ProjectGraph` 已落地\n+  - Include Tree / Keyword Index 已接入统一项目快照\n+  - `indexClient` 已成为共享 client 边界\n+\n+- **Phase 3 已完成前两步**\n+  1. `indexClient` L1 项目快照缓存\n+  2. `invalidate(rootFile)` + stale in-flight 防污染\n+  3. 基于 `snapshot.files` 的自动过期校验\n+\n+但还有一个明显未完成点：\n+\n+> **快照虽然会在文件变化后正确失效，但失效后的重建仍然会全工程重扫。**\n+\n+这与总体计划里“修改一个 include 文件后，不会触发全工程所有文件重扫”的目标还有差距。\n+\n+因此本轮聚焦到 Phase 3 的下一块核心能力：\n+\n+**让 `projectIndexer` 在跨次构建之间复用未变化文件的扫描结果。**\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. 本轮目标\n+\n+本轮只做最小但高价值的复用能力：\n+\n+1. 把 `projectIndexer` 从纯无状态函数演进为一个可持有内存缓存的 factory\n+2. 为每个文件缓存最小 `FileScanResult`\n+   - `keywords`\n+   - `includeEntries`\n+   - `searchPaths`\n+3. 用 `filePath + mtimeMs + size` 判断单文件扫描结果是否仍可复用\n+4. 保持 `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 对外 API 兼容\n+5. 给快照增加最小统计：\n+   - `stats.scannedFileCount`\n+   - `stats.reusedFileCount`\n+\n+本轮**不做**：\n+\n+- manifest\n+- LRU / 配额逐出\n+- watcher\n+- worker_threads\n+- 块级增量\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. 设计选择\n+\n+## 3.1 让“文件扫描缓存”下沉到 `projectIndexer`\n+\n+前两轮缓存都放在 `indexClient`：\n+\n+- 项目快照缓存\n+- 项目快照自动失效\n+\n+但“未变化文件的扫描结果复用”更适合放到 `projectIndexer`，因为它最了解：\n+\n+1. 单文件扫描结果包含哪些字段\n+2. 哪些字段可复用、哪些字段需要重新聚合\n+3. 项目图和关键字聚合应如何基于这些结果重建\n+\n+因此本轮做法是：\n+\n+- `indexClient` 继续负责**项目快照级**缓存边界\n+- `projectIndexer` 新增**单文件扫描级**缓存边界\n+\n+两层职责分离，不把文件级缓存逻辑继续堆到 client 层。\n+\n+## 3.2 保持图聚合仍然是“每次重建”\n+\n+本轮没有尝试直接复用：\n+\n+- `ProjectGraph`\n+- `keywordMap`\n+- 最终 `ProjectIndexSnapshot`\n+\n+而是保持：\n+\n+1. 每次 `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 仍然重新聚合工程图\n+2. 只是它读取输入时，优先复用未变化文件的扫描结果\n+\n+这样更稳健，因为：\n+\n+- 图聚合逻辑仍然是一次性、确定性的\n+- 不需要处理复杂的“图局部补丁”\n+- 先把收益最大的重复文件扫描收掉即可\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. TDD 过程\n+\n+### 4.1 先写失败测试\n+\n+本轮先在 `test/core/project/projectIndexer.test.js` 增加一条新约束：\n+\n+**当只有一个子文件发生变化时，第二次构建应只重扫该子文件，其余文件应从缓存复用。**\n+\n+测试样本使用：\n+\n+- `main.k` include `a.key` / `b.key`\n+- 首次构建后缓存三份文件扫描结果\n+- 第二次仅修改 `b.key` 的签名与关键字结果\n+\n+期望：\n+\n+1. 第二次构建的 `stats` 为：\n+   - `scannedFileCount: 1`\n+   - `reusedFileCount: 2`\n+2. `PART` 仍来自 `a.key`\n+3. `MAT_ELASTIC` 被新的 `SECTION` 替代\n+4. `main.k` 和 `a.key` 的 include / keyword 扫描函数不再重复调用\n+\n+先执行聚焦测试：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\core\\project\\projectIndexer.test.js --grep \"reuses unchanged file scans\"\n+```\n+\n+第一次先红，失败原因正确：\n+\n+- `createProjectIndexer` 还不存在\n+\n+随后把测试样本修正为“真实存在的临时文件路径”，避免 include 解析被文件不存在提前短路后，再次执行聚焦测试，仍然要求新能力本身来决定是否转绿。\n+\n+### 4.2 再写最小实现\n+\n+`src/core/project/projectIndexer.js` 本轮改为：\n+\n+1. 导出 `createProjectIndexer(...)`\n+2. 模块内仍保留一个默认 singleton：\n+   - `buildProjectIndex = defaultProjectIndexer.buildProjectIndex`\n+3. factory 内维护 `fileScanCache`\n+4. 每次访问文件时先取：\n+   - 当前文件签名\n+   - 缓存中的上次签名\n+5. 若签名一致，则直接复用缓存里的：\n+   - `keywords`\n+   - `includeEntries`\n+   - `searchPaths`\n+6. 若签名变化，则重新调用：\n+   - `collectKeywordsFromFile`\n+   - `collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile`\n+\n+并把统计结果累加到：\n+\n+- `stats.scannedFileCount`\n+- `stats.reusedFileCount`\n+\n+### 4.3 验证转绿\n+\n+聚焦测试转绿后，再执行整组 `projectIndexer` 测试，全部通过。\n+\n+这说明：\n+\n+1. 原有图聚合、缺失 include、循环保护行为未退化\n+2. 新增的文件级扫描复用已成立\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. 实际代码改动\n+\n+## 5.1 `src/core/project/projectIndexer.js`\n+\n+本轮新增：\n+\n+1. `createProjectIndexer(...)`\n+2. `readFileSignature(filePath)`\n+3. `fileScanCache`\n+4. 单文件签名比较与缓存复用逻辑\n+\n+并保持：\n+\n+1. `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 仍然可直接导入使用\n+2. `ProjectGraph` 聚合方式不变\n+3. `snapshot.files / graph / keywordMap / missingFiles / cycles` 结构不变\n+\n+仅额外增加：\n+\n+```js\n+snapshot.stats = {\n+  scannedFileCount,\n+  reusedFileCount,\n+};\n+```\n+\n+## 5.2 `test/core/project/projectIndexer.test.js`\n+\n+新增针对“子文件变化后局部重扫”的行为测试，用真实的临时文件路径驱动 include 解析，以防测试只验证 stub 而不验证真实访问路径。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 6. 真实工程样本验证\n+\n+本轮继续使用用户提供的工程：\n+\n+- 工程目录：`D:\\temp\\LSDYNA\\2020-nissan-rogue-v3`\n+- 主文件：`combine.key`\n+- 修改验证文件：`wall.key`\n+\n+验证方式：\n+\n+1. 第一次冷加载 `combine.key`\n+2. 手动 `invalidate(rootFile)` 后再次加载，验证**全量文件扫描结果是否都来自复用**\n+3. 临时修改 `wall.key`（验证后恢复）\n+4. 不手动 `invalidate()`，再次加载 `combine.key`\n+5. 直接读取 `snapshot.stats`\n+\n+实测结果：\n+\n+| 场景 | durationMs | scannedFileCount | reusedFileCount | fileCount | keywordGroupCount |\n+| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |\n+| 冷加载 | 2547 | 4 | 0 | 4 | 88 |\n+| 手动失效后热重建 | 4 | 0 | 4 | 4 | 88 |\n+| 子文件变化后自动重建 | 5 | 1 | 3 | 4 | 88 |\n+\n+这组数据说明三件事已经同时成立：\n+\n+1. `indexClient` 的项目快照失效边界仍然有效\n+2. `projectIndexer` 已能在重建时完全复用未变化文件\n+3. 当只改一个 include 子文件时，已经不再需要全工程重扫\n+\n+并且验证结束后已将 `wall.key` 恢复原始内容。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 7. 设计取舍\n+\n+## 7.1 先缓存最小扫描摘要，不缓存聚合图\n+\n+本轮缓存的是“单文件扫描结果”，而不是更高层的图结构。这样做的原因是：\n+\n+1. 单文件扫描结果天然以文件为边界，失效判断最简单\n+2. 图与关键字聚合保持确定性重建，更稳\n+3. 未来要接入 worker / manifest / watcher 时，更容易复用同一层缓存\n+\n+## 7.2 先用 singleton `buildProjectIndex` 保持现有注入方式不变\n+\n+当前扩展和 `indexClient` 仍然直接依赖：\n+\n+- `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)`\n+\n+为了不在这一轮同时改动调用层，本轮保留默认 singleton 导出，同时额外开放：\n+\n+- `createProjectIndexer()`\n+\n+这样：\n+\n+1. 生产代码不用跟着改一圈\n+2. 新能力已经能在默认导出路径生效\n+3. 单测仍可用 factory 做隔离测试\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 8. 验证结果\n+\n+执行：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\core\\project\\projectIndexer.test.js --grep \"reuses unchanged file scans\"\n+npx mocha test\\core\\project\\projectIndexer.test.js\n+npm test\n+git --no-pager diff --check\n+```\n+\n+本轮提交前以上检查均通过。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 9. 对总体计划的推进意义\n+\n+做到这一步后，Phase 3 已经不只是“项目快照可复用”，而是进一步具备了：\n+\n+1. **项目快照级复用**：同一工程未变时直接命中快照\n+2. **自动正确性校验**：文件变了会自动放弃旧快照\n+3. **文件扫描级复用**：重建时只重扫变化文件，其余文件继续复用\n+\n+这使得 Phase 3 的核心目标基本开始落地：\n+\n+> 修改一个 include 文件后，不再触发全工程所有文件重扫。\n+\n+后续下一批更自然的方向就是：\n+\n+1. manifest / 大小统计 / 逐出\n+2. watcher 驱动失效\n+3. worker 后端执行\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 10. 一句话结论\n+\n+**本轮把 `projectIndexer` 从“每次全量扫描”推进到了“重建时复用未变化文件扫描结果”，并已在真实 LS-DYNA 工程中验证：手动失效后可 0 扫描重建，单个子文件变化后只需重扫 1 个文件。**\n*** End Patch\n"
````

</details>

Now let me check for a critical issue: what happens when a file signature changes but `getFileSignature` throws an error (e.g., permission denied)?Good - errors propagate as expected. Now let me examine a critical scenario: what if a file is deleted from the include graph but still cached? Let me trace through the logic more carefully:Good - the cache correctly doesn't include removed files. Now let me verify a critical edge case: cache key normalization on Windows vs Unix:Excellent - case-insensitive caching works correctly on Windows. Now let me check a critical scenario: what if the same file is visited multiple times in a single build (through different paths or symlinks)?Good - the `visited` set correctly prevents duplicate traversal. Now let me verify the most critical scenario: stale data after file content changes but signature remains the same:This is expected behavior - the cache relies on `mtimeMs` and `size` as the signature. This is a design choice, not a bug. Let me verify that this is documented and understood:Good - the documentation explicitly states that `mtimeMs + size` is the cache key. This is an intentional design choice. Now let me run the full test suite to ensure no regressions:Perfect. Now let me examine one final crl critical edge case: what happens if `resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths` returns a different resolved path for the same `fileName` due to search path changes?Good - when the parent file changes (main.k), it rescans and correctly resolves to a different child file. This is correct behavior. Now let me check one more critical scenario: what if the search paths themselves don't change, but a new file appears earlier in the search path?Wait - that's interesting! The cache reused main.k's scan result (which includes searchPaths), but `resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths` runs fresh each time and correctly found the new dir1/child.k. Let me verify this is actually correct behavior by looking at the code more carefully:I see - line 118 calls `resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, scanResult.searchPaths)` on every build. The cached `scanResult` contains the `searchPaths` from the scan, but the *resolution* happens fresh each time. This means if a new file appears in the filesystem that matches an include, it will be picked up even if the parent file's scan is cached. This is actually correct behavior!

Let me verify one last critical issue: **the singleton cache never gets cleared**. This could be a memory leak in long-running processes:Now I understand the full picture. There are two layers of caching:

1. **indexClient** (at line 87, 111): Caches project **snapshots** by rootFile, with automatic invalidation when files change
2. **projectIndexer singleton** (line 160 in projectIndexer.js): Caches individual **file scans** across all builds

The singleton indexer's cache (`fileScanCache` at line 66 in projectIndexer.js) will grow unbounded as it sees more files. Let me check if this is a known limitation:Perfect - line 48 of the doc explicitly states "本轮**不做**: LRU / 配额逐出". This is a known limitation that's documented as future work. Let me do a final comprehensive check of the test coverage:Let me check if there are any tests for cycles:Let me exLet me examine the cycle detection logic more carefully to ensure the caching doesn't break it:Excellent - cycle detection still works correctly with caching. Now let me run one final check: verify that the implementation handles the case where `collectKeywordsFromFile` or `collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile` throws an error:This shows that errors propagate correctly and don't pollute the cache. Perfect.

Now let me do a final review of the overall structure and check for any remaining issues:Perfectfect - no whitespace issues. Let me do one final comprehensive review of the implementation for any logic errors I might have missed:Wait - I found a **critical issue**! Look at lines 71-77:

1. Line 71: `getFileSignature` is called **before** checking the cache
2. Line 72: Only then we get the cached entry
3. Line 74: We compare signatures to decide whether to reuse the cache

**The problem**: If `getFileSignature` throws an error (e.g., file deleted, permission denied), the entire function fails **even if we have a valid cached entry**.

This means:
- If a file was cached but then becomes temporarily inaccessible (permissions changed, network drive disconnected, etc.), the build will fail instead of potentially using the cached data
- This violates the principle of "fail gracefully" for cached data

However, on deeper thought: this might be **intentiontional** because:
- If we can't stat the file, we can't know if it's changed
- Using stale cached data when we can't verify freshness is dangerous
- It's better to fail than to return incorrect results

Let me verify if this is the intended behavior by checking similar code patterns:Interesting - `indexClient.isSnapshotValid` wraps `getFileSignature` in a try-catch and returns `false` on error (lines 66-73). This invalidates s invalidates the cache gracefully.

But in `projectIndexer.loadFileScan`, there's no try-catch around `getFileSignatgnature` (line 71), so errors propagate up and fail the entire build.

This is **inconsistent** and potentially a bug. Let me verify the actual behavior:This cThis confirms the issue. However, let me think about whether this is actually a **bug** or **intended behavior**:

**Arguments for this being intended:**
1. We **must** verify file freshness before using cached data
2. If we can't access the file, we can't build the project correctly anyway (the file is part of the project)
3. Failing fast is better than using potentially stale data

**Arguments for this being a bug:**
1. Inconsistent with `indexClient` which gracefully invalidates on stat errors
2. If the file truly exists in the project, a build would need to read it anyway (lines 79-80), so the stat check is redundant
3. Temporary permissions issues shouldn't fail builds if we have valid cached data

Actually, on reflection: **this is likely intended behavior** because:
- The caching is an optimization, not a fallback mechanism
- If we can't access a project file, the project is fundamentally broken
- We need to stat the file to know if our cache is valid; without that, we're guessing

However, I should document this as a finding for the user to confirm the design intent.

Let me summarize my findings:Perfect. All tests pass. Now let me compile my review findings.

---

## Review Result: **PASS**

All tests pass (98 passing), and the implementation correctly delivers the intended functionality: file-level scan result caching with `mtimeMs + size` invalidation.

### Findings

No critical bugs found. One design question for awareness:

#### Design Question: Error handling in `loadFileScan`

**File:** `src/core/project/projectIndexer.js:71`

**Observation:** `getFileSignature` is called before checking the cache, and errors propagate without try-catch. If a file becomes temporarily inaccessible (permissions change, network drive disconnects), the build fails even when valid cached scan results exist.

**Comparison:** `indexClient.isSnapshotValid` (lines 66-73 in indexClient.js) wraps `getFileSignature` in try-catch and gracefully invalidates the cache on stat errors.

**Current behavior:**
```javascript
const signature = await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath);  // ← throws on permission error
const cachedEntry = fileScanCache.get(fileCacheKey);
if (cachedEntry && areFileSignaturesEqual(...)) { ... }
```

**Question:** Is this intentional? Arguments for current design:
- Must verify file freshness before using cache (can't trust stale data)
- If file is inaccessible, project build would fail anyway when trying to read content
- Fail-fast is clearer than returning potentially incorrect results

Arguments for try-catch:
- Consistency with `indexClient` 
- Temporary access issues (especially on network drives) wouldn't fail builds if cache exists

**My assessment:** This appears to be **intentional design** - the cache is an optimization, not a fallback. If you can't stat the file, you can't verify cache validity, so failing is correct. Just flagging for awareness since it differs from `indexClient`'s approach.

---

### Test Sufficiency Assessment

**Current test coverage:** ✅ **Sufficient for the stated scope**

The tests correctly verify:
1. ✅ Cache reuse when file unchanged (`mtimeMs` + `size` match)
2. ✅ Cache invalidation when file changes (signature change)
3. ✅ Correct stats tracking (`scannedFileCount`, `reusedFileCount`)
4. ✅ Scan function call counts (verifies actual reuse, not just stats)
5. ✅ Keyword updates propagate correctly after file change
6. ✅ Existing behavior preserved (includes, cycles, missing files)

**Additional scenarios verified through manual testing (not regression risks):**
- Case-insensitive caching on Windows ✅
- Cycle detection with caching ✅  
- Include resolution runs fresh each build ✅
- Error propagation (stat failures, scanner failures) ✅
- Multi-parent includes (visited set prevents duplication) ✅

**Intentionally not tested (per design doc, Phase 3 scope):**
- LRU eviction (documented as future work)
- Memory limits (documented as future work)
- File watcher integration (future)

### Verified Correctness

✅ **Caching invalidation:** Correctly keyed by `filePath + mtimeMs + size`  
✅ **Stale data prevention:** Changed files trigger rescan  
✅ **Graph reconstruction:** Runs fresh each build, no graph-level caching bugs  
✅ **Include resolution:** Runs fresh using cached `searchPaths`, correctly picks up new files  
✅ **Cycle detection:** Unaffected by caching (relies on per-build `visited` set)  
✅ **Missing include handling:** Preserved correctly  
✅ **API compatibility:** `buildProjectIndex` signature unchanged, `stats` field added (non-breaking)  
✅ **Singleton vs factory:** Default export uses singleton for production, `createProjectIndexer` available for isolated tests

### Summary

The implementation is **correct and complete** for Phase 3's stated scope. No substantive issues found. The singleton cache has unbounded growth, but this is explicitly documented as acceptable for this phase (LRU eviction deferred to future work). Test cove

diff --git a/src/core/project/projectIndexer.js b/src/core/project/projectIndexer.js
index b275676..30de7fc 100644
--- a/src/core/project/projectIndexer.js
+++ b/src/core/project/projectIndexer.js
@@ -7,6 +7,32 @@ const includeScanner = require('../parser/includeScanner');
 const keywordScanner = require('../parser/keywordScanner');
 const { ProjectGraph } = require('./projectGraph');
 
+function resolveProjectFile(filePath) {
+    return path.resolve(filePath);
+}
+
+function getProjectFileCacheKey(filePath) {
+    const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
+    return process.platform === 'win32'
+        ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()
+        : resolvedFilePath;
+}
+
+function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
+    return left
+        && right
+        && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
+        && left.size === right.size;
+}
+
+async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
+    const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
+    return {
+        mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
+        size: stat.size,
+    };
+}
+
 function resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, searchPaths) {
     for (const searchPath of searchPaths) {
         const fullPath = path.resolve(searchPath, fileName);
@@ -22,59 +48,118 @@ function addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, keywords) {
     }
 }

diff --git a/test/core/project/projectIndexer.test.js b/test/core/project/projectIndexer.test.js

 describe('projectIndexer', () => {
     it('recursively aggregates included files and keyword usages into one project snapshot', async () => {
@@ -192,4 +192,80 @@ describe('projectIndexer', () => {
             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
         }
     });
+
+    it('reuses unchanged file scans when rebuilding after a child file change', async () => {
+        const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
+        const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
+        const aFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'a.key');
+        const bFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'b.key');
+        const projectDir = path.dirname(rootFile);
+        const scanCounts = new Map();
+        const includeFixtures = new Map([
+            [rootFile, {
+                includeEntries: [{ fileName: 'a.key' }, { fileName: 'b.key' }],
+                searchPaths: [projectDir],
+            }],
+            [aFile, {
+                includeEntries: [],
+                searchPaths: [projectDir],
+            }],
+            [bFile, {
+                includeEntries: [],
+                searchPaths: [projectDir],
+            }],
+        ]);
+        const keywordFixtures = new Map([
+            [rootFile, []],
+            [aFile, [{ keyword: 'PART', filePath: aFile, line: 0 }]],
+            [bFile, [{ keyword: 'MAT_ELASTIC', filePath: bFile, line: 0 }]],
+        ]);
+        const signatures = new Map([
+            [rootFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }],
+            [aFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 200 }],
+            [bFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 300 }],
+        ]);
+        const indexer = createProjectIndexer({
+            getFileSignature: async (filePath) => signatures.get(filePath),
+            collectKeywordsFromFile: async (filePath) => {
+                scanCounts.set(`keywords:${filePath}`, (scanCounts.get(`keywords:${filePath}`) || 0) + 1);
+                return keywordFixtures.get(filePath);
+            },
+            collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile: async (filePath) => {
+                scanCounts.set(`includes:${filePath}`, (scanCounts.get(`includes:${filePath}`) || 0) + 1);
+                return includeFixtures.get(filePath);
+            },
+        });
+
+        fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*KEYWORD\n', 'utf8');
+        fs.writeFileSync(aFile, '*KEYWORD\n', 'utf8');
+        fs.writeFileSync(bFile, '*KEYWORD\n', 'utf8');
+
+        try {
+            const initialSnapshot = await indexer.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
+            assert.deepEqual(initialSnapshot.stats, {
+                scannedFileCount: 3,
+                reusedFileCount: 0,
+            });
+
+            signatures.set(bFile, { mtimeMs: 20, size: 300 });
+            keywordFixtures.set(bFile, [{ keyword: 'SECTION', filePath: bFile, line: 0 }]);
+
+            const updatedSnapshot = await indexer.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
+            assert.deepEqual(updatedSnapshot.stats, {
+                scannedFileCount: 1,
+                reusedFileCount: 2,
+            });
+            assert.deepEqual(updatedSnapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath), [aFile]);
+            assert.deepEqual(updatedSnapshot.keywordMap.get('SECTION').map(entry => entry.filePath), [bFile]);
+            assert.equal(updatedSnapshot.keywordMap.has('MAT_ELASTIC'), false);
+            assert.equal(scanCounts.get(`keywords:${rootFile}`), 1);
+            assert.equal(scanCounts.get(`includes:${rootFile}`), 1);
+            assert.equal(scanCounts.get(`keywords:${aFile}`), 1);
+            assert.equal(scanCounts.get(`includes:${aFile}`), 1);
+            assert.equal(scanCounts.get(`keywords:${bFile}`), 2);
+            assert.equal(scanCounts.get(`includes:${bFile}`), 2);
+        } finally {
+            fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
+        }
+    });
 });

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_execution_record_phase3_project_indexer_file_reuse.md

<details>
<summary>300 lines</summary>

````
1. # vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 3：projectIndexer 单文件扫描复用）
2. 
3. ## 1. 当前进度定位
4. 
5. 截至本轮开始前，整体进度已经推进到：
6. 
7. - **Phase 2 已基本打通**
8.   - parser 抽取完成
9.   - `projectIndexer` / `ProjectGraph` 已落地
10.   - Include Tree / Keyword Index 已接入统一项目快照
11.   - `indexClient` 已成为共享 client 边界
12. 
13. - **Phase 3 已完成前两步**
14.   1. `indexClient` L1 项目快照缓存
15.   2. `invalidate(rootFile)` + stale in-flight 防污染
16.   3. 基于 `snapshot.files` 的自动过期校验
17. 
18. 但还有一个明显未完成点：
19. 
20. > **快照虽然会在文件变化后正确失效，但失效后的重建仍然会全工程重扫。**
21. 
22. 这与总体计划里“修改一个 include 文件后，不会触发全工程所有文件重扫”的目标还有差距。
23. 
24. 因此本轮聚焦到 Phase 3 的下一块核心能力：
25. 
26. **让 `projectIndexer` 在跨次构建之间复用未变化文件的扫描结果。**
27. 
28. ---
29. 
30. ## 2. 本轮目标
31. 
32. 本轮只做最小但高价值的复用能力：
33. 
34. 1. 把 `projectIndexer` 从纯无状态函数演进为一个可持有内存缓存的 factory
35. 2. 为每个文件缓存最小 `FileScanResult`
36.    - `keywords`
37.    - `includeEntries`
38.    - `searchPaths`
39. 3. 用 `filePath + mtimeMs + size` 判断单文件扫描结果是否仍可复用
40. 4. 保持 `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 对外 API 兼容
41. 5. 给快照增加最小统计：
42.    - `stats.scannedFileCount`
43.    - `stats.reusedFileCount`
44. 
45. 本轮**不做**：
46. 
47. - manifest
48. - LRU / 配额逐出
49. - watcher
50. - worker_threads
51. - 块级增量
52. 
53. ---
54. 
55. ## 3. 设计选择
56. 
57. ## 3.1 让“文件扫描缓存”下沉到 `projectIndexer`
58. 
59. 前两轮缓存都放在 `indexClient`：
60. 
61. - 项目快照缓存
62. - 项目快照自动失效
63. 
64. 但“未变化文件的扫描结果复用”更适合放到 `projectIndexer`，因为它最了解：
65. 
66. 1. 单文件扫描结果包含哪些字段
67. 2. 哪些字段可复用、哪些字段需要重新聚合
68. 3. 项目图和关键字聚合应如何基于这些结果重建
69. 
70. 因此本轮做法是：
71. 
72. - `indexClient` 继续负责**项目快照级**缓存边界
73. - `projectIndexer` 新增**单文件扫描级**缓存边界
74. 
75. 两层职责分离，不把文件级缓存逻辑继续堆到 client 层。
76. 
77. ## 3.2 保持图聚合仍然是“每次重建”
78. 
79. 本轮没有尝试直接复用：
80. 
81. - `ProjectGraph`
82. - `keywordMap`
83. - 最终 `ProjectIndexSnapshot`
84. 
85. 而是保持：
86. 
87. 1. 每次 `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 仍然重新聚合工程图
88. 2. 只是它读取输入时，优先复用未变化文件的扫描结果
89. 
90. 这样更稳健，因为：
91. 
92. - 图聚合逻辑仍然是一次性、确定性的
93. - 不需要处理复杂的“图局部补丁”
94. - 先把收益最大的重复文件扫描收掉即可
95. 
96. ---
97. 
98. ## 4. TDD 过程
99. 
100. ### 4.1 先写失败测试
101. 
102. 本轮先在 `test/core/project/projectIndexer.test.js` 增加一条新约束：
103. 
104. **当只有一个子文件发生变化时，第二次构建应只重扫该子文件，其余文件应从缓存复用。**
105. 
106. 测试样本使用：
107. 
108. - `main.k` include `a.key` / `b.key`
109. - 首次构建后缓存三份文件扫描结果
110. - 第二次仅修改 `b.key` 的签名与关键字结果
111. 
112. 期望：
113. 
114. 1. 第二次构建的 `stats` 为：
115.    - `scannedFileCount: 1`
116.    - `reusedFileCount: 2`
117. 2. `PART` 仍来自 `a.key`
118. 3. `MAT_ELASTIC` 被新的 `SECTION` 替代
119. 4. `main.k` 和 `a.key` 的 include / keyword 扫描函数不再重复调用
120. 
121. 先执行聚焦测试：
122. 
123. ```powershell
124. npx mocha test\core\project\projectIndexer.test.js --grep "reuses unchanged file scans"
125. ```
126. 
127. 第一次先红，失败原因正确：
128. 
129. - `createProjectIndexer` 还不存在
130. 
131. 随后把测试样本修正为“真实存在的临时文件路径”，避免 include 解析被文件不存在提前短路后，再次执行聚焦测试，仍然要求新能力本身来决定是否转绿。
132. 
133. ### 4.2 再写最小实现
134. 
135. `src/core/project/projectIndexer.js` 本轮改为：
136. 
137. 1. 导出 `createProjectIndexer(...)`
138. 2. 模块内仍保留一个默认 singleton：
139.    - `buildProjectIndex = defaultProjectIndexer.buildProjectIndex`
140. 3. factory 内维护 `fileScanCache`
141. 4. 每次访问文件时先取：
142.    - 当前文件签名
143.    - 缓存中的上次签名
144. 5. 若签名一致，则直接复用缓存里的：
145.    - `keywords`
146.    - `includeEntries`
147.    - `searchPaths`
148. 6. 若签名变化，则重新调用：
149.    - `collectKeywordsFromFile`
150.    - `collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile`
151. 
152. 并把统计结果累加到：
153. 
154. - `stats.scannedFileCount`
155. - `stats.reusedFileCount`
156. 
157. ### 4.3 验证转绿
158. 
159. 聚焦测试转绿后，再执行整组 `projectIndexer` 测试，全部通过。
160. 
161. 这说明：
162. 
163. 1. 原有图聚合、缺失 include、循环保护行为未退化
164. 2. 新增的文件级扫描复用已成立
165. 
166. ---
167. 
168. ## 5. 实际代码改动
169. 
170. ## 5.1 `src/core/project/projectIndexer.js`
171. 
172. 本轮新增：
173. 
174. 1. `createProjectIndexer(...)`
175. 2. `readFileSignature(filePath)`
176. 3. `fileScanCache`
177. 4. 单文件签名比较与缓存复用逻辑
178. 
179. 并保持：
180. 
181. 1. `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 仍然可直接导入使用
182. 2. `ProjectGraph` 聚合方式不变
183. 3. `snapshot.files / graph / keywordMap / missingFiles / cycles` 结构不变
184. 
185. 仅额外增加：
186. 
187. ```js
188. snapshot.stats = {
189.   scannedFileCount,
190.   reusedFileCount,
191. };
192. ```
193. 
194. ## 5.2 `test/core/project/projectIndexer.test.js`
195. 
196. 新增针对“子文件变化后局部重扫”的行为测试，用真实的临时文件路径驱动 include 解析，以防测试只验证 stub 而不验证真实访问路径。
197. 
198. ---
199. 
200. ## 6. 真实工程样本验证
201. 
202. 本轮继续使用用户提供的工程：
203. 
204. - 工程目录：`D:\temp\LSDYNA\2020-nissan-rogue-v3`
205. - 主文件：`combine.key`
206. - 修改验证文件：`wall.key`
207. 
208. 验证方式：
209. 
210. 1. 第一次冷加载 `combine.key`
211. 2. 手动 `invalidate(rootFile)` 后再次加载，验证**全量文件扫描结果是否都来自复用**
212. 3. 临时修改 `wall.key`（验证后恢复）
213. 4. 不手动 `invalidate()`，再次加载 `combine.key`
214. 5. 直接读取 `snapshot.stats`
215. 
216. 实测结果：
217. 
218. | 场景 | durationMs | scannedFileCount | reusedFileCount | fileCount | keywordGroupCount |
219. | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
220. | 冷加载 | 2547 | 4 | 0 | 4 | 88 |
221. | 手动失效后热重建 | 4 | 0 | 4 | 4 | 88 |
222. | 子文件变化后自动重建 | 5 | 1 | 3 | 4 | 88 |
223. 
224. 这组数据说明三件事已经同时成立：
225. 
226. 1. `indexClient` 的项目快照失效边界仍然有效
227. 2. `projectIndexer` 已能在重建时完全复用未变化文件
228. 3. 当只改一个 include 子文件时，已经不再需要全工程重扫
229. 
230. 并且验证结束后已将 `wall.key` 恢复原始内容。
231. 
232. ---
233. 
234. ## 7. 设计取舍
235. 
236. ## 7.1 先缓存最小扫描摘要，不缓存聚合图
237. 
238. 本轮缓存的是“单文件扫描结果”，而不是更高层的图结构。这样做的原因是：
239. 
240. 1. 单文件扫描结果天然以文件为边界，失效判断最简单
241. 2. 图与关键字聚合保持确定性重建，更稳
242. 3. 未来要接入 worker / manifest / watcher 时，更容易复用同一层缓存
243. 
244. ## 7.2 先用 singleton `buildProjectIndex` 保持现有注入方式不变
245. 
246. 当前扩展和 `indexClient` 仍然直接依赖：
247. 
248. - `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)`
249. 
250. 为了不在这一轮同时改动调用层，本轮保留默认 singleton 导出，同时额外开放：
251. 
252. - `createProjectIndexer()`
253. 
254. 这样：
255. 
256. 1. 生产代码不用跟着改一圈
257. 2. 新能力已经能在默认导出路径生效
258. 3. 单测仍可用 factory 做隔离测试
259. 
260. ---
261. 
262. ## 8. 验证结果
263. 
264. 执行：
265. 
266. ```powershell
267. npx mocha test\core\project\projectIndexer.test.js --grep "reuses unchanged file scans"
268. npx mocha test\core\project\projectIndexer.test.js
269. npm test
270. git --no-pager diff --check
271. ```
272. 
273. 本轮提交前以上检查均通过。
274. 
275. ---
276. 
277. ## 9. 对总体计划的推进意义
278. 
279. 做到这一步后，Phase 3 已经不只是“项目快照可复用”，而是进一步具备了：
280. 
281. 1. **项目快照级复用**：同一工程未变时直接命中快照
282. 2. **自动正确性校验**：文件变了会自动放弃旧快照
283. 3. **文件扫描级复用**：重建时只重扫变化文件，其余文件继续复用
284. 
285. 这使得 Phase 3 的核心目标基本开始落地：
286. 
287. > 修改一个 include 文件后，不再触发全工程所有文件重扫。
288. 
289. 后续下一批更自然的方向就是：
290. 
291. 1. manifest / 大小统计 / 逐出
292. 2. watcher 驱动失效
293. 3. worker 后端执行
294. 
295. ---
296. 
297. ## 10. 一句话结论
298. 
299. **本轮把 `projectIndexer` 从“每次全量扫描”推进到了“重建时复用未变化文件扫描结果”，并已在真实 LS-DYNA 工程中验证：手动失效后可 0 扫描重建，单个子文件变化后只需重扫 1 个文件。**
300.
````
