<sub>⏱️ 643m 46s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase5_workspace_invalidation.md\n+# vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 5：workspace watcher 驱动缓存失效）\n+\n+## 1. 当前进度定位\n+\n+到本轮开始时，Phase 3 的缓存主干已经基本完成：\n+\n+1. 项目快照缓存\n+2. 自动/手动失效\n+3. 单文件扫描复用\n+4. LRU 逐出\n+5. cache manifest store\n+\n+但总体计划里的下一大块 —— **“文件系统变更可自动使缓存失效”** —— 还没有落地。\n+\n+也就是说，即使当前缓存已经能自动校验工程文件是否变化，扩展层仍然没有一个**工作区文件变化入口**，去在文件发生 create/change/delete 时主动让受影响工程根失效。\n+\n+因此本轮推进两件事：\n+\n+1. 新增纯函数模块，负责把“变化文件”映射到“受影响工程根”\n+2. 在 `activate()` 里接入 `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher(...)`\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. 本轮目标\n+\n+本轮只做第一版、企业级稳健优先的 watcher 失效路径：\n+\n+1. 新增 `src/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.js`\n+2. 提供：\n+   - `findAffectedProjectRoots(changedFilePath, manifestEntries)`\n+3. 新增扩展层辅助：\n+   - `createManifestDrivenInvalidator({ indexClient, findAffectedRoots })`\n+4. 在 `activate()` 中注册：\n+   - `**/*.{k,key,dyna}` watcher\n+5. watcher 在以下事件触发时使受影响根缓存失效：\n+   - change\n+   - create\n+   - delete\n+\n+本轮**不做**：\n+\n+- 自动重扫并刷新 UI\n+- watcher debounce / 批处理\n+- 文档内实时编辑增量\n+- worker 后台刷新\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. 设计选择\n+\n+## 3.1 先“失效”，不直接“自动重建”\n+\n+本轮 watcher 收到文件变化后，只做一件事：\n+\n+> 找到受影响工程根并调用 `indexClient.invalidate(rootFile)`\n+\n+而不立即：\n+\n+- 重建项目快照\n+- 刷新 Include Tree\n+- 刷新 Keyword Index\n+\n+原因是当前阶段先保证**缓存正确性**更重要：\n+\n+1. 文件变了，旧缓存不能继续被命中\n+2. 至于何时重建，可以继续由用户触发扫描或后续 worker/watcher 批处理机制决定\n+\n+这样更稳健，也避免把 Phase 5 一次性做得过重。\n+\n+## 3.2 用 manifest 做“变化文件 -> 工程根”反查\n+\n+既然上一轮已经把 `cacheManifestStore` 建成了正式边界，本轮就直接复用 manifest：\n+\n+1. watcher 拿到变化文件路径\n+2. `indexClient.getManifestEntries()` 提供当前工程清单\n+3. `findAffectedProjectRoots(...)` 根据 `trackedFiles` 找出所有受影响根\n+4. 对这些根逐个 `invalidate()`\n+\n+这让 watcher 不需要自己维护反向依赖表，逻辑更聚焦，也更容易测试。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. TDD 过程\n+\n+### 4.1 先写失败测试\n+\n+本轮先新增三组约束：\n+\n+#### A. `test/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.test.js`\n+\n+验证：\n+\n+1. 同一个变化文件可匹配多个工程根\n+2. Windows 风格路径别名 / 大小写差异能够正确归一化命中\n+\n+#### B. `test/extension.test.js`\n+\n+验证：\n+\n+1. `activate()` 必须注册 `**/*.{k,key,dyna}` watcher\n+2. `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)` 必须对受影响根逐个 `invalidate()`\n+3. 未被 manifest 跟踪的文件变化必须被忽略\n+\n+先执行聚焦测试：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\core\\incremental\\fileInvalidation.test.js test\\extension.test.js --grep \"findAffectedProjectRoots|workspace watcher|createManifestDrivenInvalidator\"\n+```\n+\n+结果先红，失败原因符合预期：\n+\n+- `fileInvalidation` 模块不存在\n+- `createManifestDrivenInvalidator` 不存在\n+- `activate()` 还没有注册 watcher\n+\n+### 4.2 再写最小实现\n+\n+本轮实现顺序：\n+\n+1. 新增 `src/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.js`\n+2. 新增 `findAffectedProjectRoots(...)`\n+3. 给 `indexClient` 增加：\n+   - `getManifestEntries()`\n+4. 在 `src/extension.js` 中新增：\n+   - `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)`\n+5. 在 `activate()` 中注册 workspace watcher，并把 change/create/delete 都接到该 invalidator\n+\n+### 4.3 验证转绿\n+\n+聚焦测试转绿后，再跑全量测试，确保 watcher 接线没有破坏现有激活、快照、provider 等路径。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. 实际代码改动\n+\n+## 5.1 `src/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.js`\n+\n+本轮新增了第一版文件失效辅助模块。\n+\n+职责非常明确：\n+\n+1. 输入：变化文件路径 + 当前 manifest 条目\n+2. 输出：受影响工程根列表\n+\n+模块内部做了：\n+\n+- 路径规范化\n+- Windows 下大小写归一化\n+- 重复根去重\n+\n+## 5.2 `src/client/services/indexClient.js`\n+\n+新增：\n+\n+- `getManifestEntries()`\n+\n+使扩展层可以读取当前 manifest 条目，而不用直接依赖 manifest store 内部实现。\n+\n+## 5.3 `src/extension.js`\n+\n+新增：\n+\n+- `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)`\n+\n+并在 `activate()` 中正式注册：\n+\n+- `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher('**/*.{k,key,dyna}')`\n+\n+让工作区文件变化能够自动把相关工程缓存置为失效状态。\n+\n+## 5.4 测试桩\n+\n+`test/vscode-mock.js` 补了最小 watcher mock，避免激活路径在测试环境下缺失 API。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 6. 设计取舍\n+\n+## 6.1 先支持多根失效，而不是只失效当前活动工程\n+\n+同一个子文件可能被多个工程根复用。因此本轮设计不是“只失效活动文档对应工程”，而是：\n+\n+- manifest 中凡是跟踪了该文件的根，全部失效\n+\n+这更符合企业级稳健性，也能避免交叉工程污染缓存。\n+\n+## 6.2 先让 watcher 成为缓存边界的一部分\n+\n+本轮 watcher 只做缓存边界治理，不直接触发 UI 行为变化。这样后续要加：\n+\n+- debounce\n+- batching\n+- worker refresh\n+\n+都可以在现有边界上逐步演进。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 7. 验证结果\n+\n+执行：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\core\\incremental\\fileInvalidation.test.js test\\extension.test.js --grep \"findAffectedProjectRoots|workspace watcher|createManifestDrivenInvalidator\"\n+npm test\n+git --no-pager diff --check\n+```\n+\n+本轮提交前以上检查均通过。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 8. 对总体计划的推进意义\n+\n+做到这一步后，缓存层已经不只是“等下次访问时才发现过期”，而是开始具备：\n+\n+1. **工作区文件变化入口**\n+2. **基于 manifest 的受影响工程根反查**\n+3. **主动失效路径**\n+\n+这意味着总体计划里的：\n+\n+> 文件系统变更可自动使缓存失效\n+\n+已经开始进入可运行状态。后续下一层更自然的能力就是：\n+\n+1. watcher debounce / batching\n+2. 自动后台重建\n+3. worker / server 侧调度\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 9. 一句话结论\n+\n+**本轮把工作区文件 watcher 正式接到了缓存失效链路上：文件一旦发生 create/change/delete，扩展就能基于 manifest 找到受影响工程根并主动使其快照缓存失效。**\n*** End Patch\n"
````

</details>

## Review Summary

I have completed a thorough review of the uncommitted changes. Here are my findings:

### **PASS**

No significant issues found in the reviewed changes.

---

### **Verification Performed:**

1. ✅ **All tests pass** (109 passing tests, including 5 new tests for this feature)

2. ✅ **Path normalization is consistent** across all modules:
   - `fileInvalidation.js` uses `getTrackedFileKey()` 
   - `cacheManifestStore.js` uses `getManifestRootKey()`
   - Both apply the same logic: `path.resolve()` + lowercase on Windows
   - Tested cross-module compatibility - normalization keys match

3. ✅ **Edge cases are handled correctly:**
   - Empty manifest → returns empty array, no error
   - Malformed manifest entries → skipped gracefully
   - Null/undefined input to invalidator → early return, no crash
   - Duplicate roots in manifest → deduplicated correctly
   - File URI vs string path → handled transparently via `uri.fsPath`

4. ✅ **Watcher pattern matches language definition:**
   - Watcher: `**/*.{k,key,dyna}`
   - Language extensions in package.json: `[".k", ".key", ".dyna"]`
   - Complete coverage

5. ✅ **Initialization order is safe:**
   - `indexClient` created first
   - `invalidateChangedProjectRoots` created with initialized `indexClient`
   - Watcher registered last with all dependencies ready

6. ✅ **Invalidation is synchronous and fast** (just Map operations, no I/O)

7. ✅ **Integration path is verified by existing tests:**
   - Test: "rebuilds cached snapshots when a tracked project file changes"
   - Tests verify cache invalidation → rebuild flow

---

### **Test Coverage Assessment:**

The current tests are **sufficient** for the intended scope. Coverage includes:

**Core logic (`fileInvalidation.js`):**
- ✅ Multi-root invalidation when same file is tracked by multiple projects
- ✅ Case-insensitive path matching on Windows

**Integration (`extension.js`):**
- ✅ Watcher registration with correct glob pattern
- ✅ `createManifestDrivenInvalidator` invalidates all affected roots
- ✅ Untracked files are ignored

**Missing but acceptable given Phase 5 scope:**
- ❌ No test for rapid successive file changes (acceptable - debouncing explicitly deferred to future phase per documentation)
- ❌ No end-to-end integration test from watcher event → cache rebuild (acceptable - existing indexClient tests verify the rebuild path separately)

The test coverage aligns with the documented Phase 5 goals: **invalidation only, no auto-rebuild**.

---

### **Conclusion:**

**PASS** - The implementation is correct, safe, and well-tested for its intended scope. The changes:
- Maintain backward compatibility (all existing tests pass)
- Handle edge cases gracefully
- Use consistent path normalization across modules
- Follow the documented design (invalidate only, defer auto-rebuild)
- Have approp

+    const workspaceWatcher = vscode.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher('**/*.{k,key,dyna}');
+    context.subscriptions.push(workspaceWatcher);
+    context.subscriptions.push(workspaceWatcher.onDidChange(uri => invalidateChangedProjectRoots(uri)));
+    context.subscriptions.push(workspaceWatcher.onDidCreate(uri => invalidateChangedProjectRoots(uri)));
+    context.subscriptions.push(workspaceWatcher.onDidDelete(uri => invalidateChangedProjectRoots(uri)));

@@ -878,6 +885,27 @@ function activate(context) {
 
 function deactivate() {}
 
+function createManifestDrivenInvalidator({ indexClient, findAffectedRoots = findAffectedProjectRoots } = {}) {
+    if (!indexClient || typeof indexClient.invalidate !== 'function' || typeof indexClient.getManifestEntries !== 'function') {
+        throw new TypeError('createManifestDrivenInvalidator requires indexClient.invalidate and indexClient.getManifestEntries');
+    }
+    if (typeof findAffectedRoots !== 'function') {
+        throw new TypeError('createManifestDrivenInvalidator requires a findAffectedRoots function');
+    }
+
+    return function invalidateChangedFile(fileUriOrPath) {
+        const changedFilePath = typeof fileUriOrPath === 'string'
+            ? fileUriOrPath
+            : fileUriOrPath && fileUriOrPath.fsPath;
+        if (!changedFilePath) return;
+
+        const affectedRoots = findAffectedRoots(changedFilePath, indexClient.getManifestEntries());
+        for (const rootFile of affectedRoots) {
+            indexClient.invalidate(rootFile);
+        }
+    };
+}
+
 module.exports = { activate, deactivate };

             vscodeMock.languages.registerCodeLensProvider = originalRegisterCodeLensProvider;
         }
     });
+
+    it('registers a workspace watcher for LS-DYNA project files', () => {
+        const context = { subscriptions: [] };
+        const disposable = { dispose() {} };
+        let watcherGlob;
+        const originalCreateFileSystemWatcher = vscodeMock.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher;
+        const originalRegisterTreeDataProvider = vscodeMock.window.registerTreeDataProvider;
+        const originalOnDidChangeActiveTextEditor = vscodeMock.window.onDidChangeActiveTextEditor;
+        const originalOnDidChangeTextEditorSelection = vscodeMock.window.onDidChangeTextEditorSelection;
+        const originalCreateTextEditorDecorationType = vscodeMock.window.createTextEditorDecorationType;
+        const originalRegisterHoverProvider = vscodeMock.languages.registerHoverProvider;
+        const originalRegisterCodeLensProvider = vscodeMock.languages.registerCodeLensProvider;
+
+        vscodeMock.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher = (glob) => {
+            watcherGlob = glob;
+            return {
+                onDidChange: () => disposable,
+                onDidCreate: () => disposable,
+                onDidDelete: () => disposable,
+                dispose() {},
+            };
+        };
+        vscodeMock.window.registerTreeDataProvider = () => disposable;
+        vscodeMock.window.onDidChangeActiveTextEditor = () => disposable;
+        vscodeMock.window.onDidChangeTextEditorSelection = () => disposable;
+        vscodeMock.window.createTextEditorDecorationType = () => disposable;
+        vscodeMock.languages.registerHoverProvider = () => disposable;
+        vscodeMock.languages.registerCodeLensProvider = () => disposable;
+
+        try {
+            extensionModule.activate(context);
+            assert.equal(watcherGlob, '**/*.{k,key,dyna}');
+        } finally {
+            vscodeMock.workspace.createFileSystemWatcher = originalCreateFileSystemWatcher;
+            vscodeMock.window.registerTreeDataProvider = originalRegisterTreeDataProvider;
+            vscodeMock.window.onDidChangeActiveTextEditor = originalOnDidChangeActiveTextEditor;
+            vscodeMock.window.onDidChangeTextEditorSelection = originalOnDidChangeTextEditorSelection;
+            vscodeMock.window.createTextEditorDecorationType = originalCreateTextEditorDecorationType;
+            vscodeMock.languages.registerHoverProvider = originalRegisterHoverProvider;
+            vscodeMock.languages.registerCodeLensProvider = originalRegisterCodeLensProvider;
+        }
+    });
+});
+
+describe('createManifestDrivenInvalidator', () => {
+    it('invalidates every affected project root for a changed tracked file', () => {
+        const rootA = path.resolve('project', 'root-a.k');
+        const rootB = path.resolve('project', 'root-b.k');
+        const changedFile = path.resolve('project', 'shared.key');
+        const invalidatedRoots = [];
+        const invalidateChangedFile = createManifestDrivenInvalidator({
+            indexClient: {
+                getManifestEntries() {
+                    return [
+                        { rootFile: rootA, trackedFiles: [rootA, changedFile] },
+                        { rootFile: rootB, trackedFiles: [rootB, changedFile] },
+                    ];
+                },
+                invalidate(rootFile) {
+                    invalidatedRoots.push(rootFile);
+                },
+            },
+        });
+
+        invalidateChangedFile({ fsPath: changedFile });
+
+        assert.deepEqual(invalidatedRoots, [rootA, rootB]);
+    });
+
+    it('ignores untracked files', () => {
+        const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'root.k');
+        const invalidatedRoots = [];
+        const invalidateChangedFile = createManifestDrivenInvalidator({
+            indexClient: {
+                getManifestEntries() {
+                    return [
+                        { rootFile, trackedFiles: [rootFile] },
+                    ];
+                },
+                invalidate(root) {
+                    invalidatedRoots.push(root);
+                },
+            },
+        });
+
+        invalidateChangedFile({ fsPath: path.resolve('project', 'other.key') });
+
+        assert.deepEqual(invalidatedRoots, []);
+    });
 });

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\incremental\fileInvalidation.js

<details>
<summary>43 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const path = require('path');
4. 
5. function resolveTrackedFile(filePath) {
6.     if (typeof filePath !== 'string' || filePath.trim() === '') {
7.         throw new TypeError('file invalidation requires a file path');
8.     }
9.     return path.resolve(filePath);
10. }
11. 
12. function getTrackedFileKey(filePath) {
13.     const resolvedFilePath = resolveTrackedFile(filePath);
14.     return process.platform === 'win32'
15.         ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()
16.         : resolvedFilePath;
17. }
18. 
19. function findAffectedProjectRoots(changedFilePath, manifestEntries = []) {
20.     const changedFileKey = getTrackedFileKey(changedFilePath);
21.     const affectedRoots = [];
22.     const seenRoots = new Set();
23. 
24.     for (const entry of manifestEntries) {
25.         if (!entry || typeof entry.rootFile !== 'string' || !Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles)) continue;
26.         const rootFile = path.resolve(entry.rootFile);
27.         const rootFileKey = getTrackedFileKey(rootFile);
28.         if (seenRoots.has(rootFileKey)) continue;
29. 
30.         const matchesChangedFile = entry.trackedFiles.some(trackedFile => getTrackedFileKey(trackedFile) === changedFileKey);
31.         if (!matchesChangedFile) continue;
32. 
33.         seenRoots.add(rootFileKey);
34.         affectedRoots.push(rootFile);
35.     }
36. 
37.     return affectedRoots;
38. }
39. 
40. module.exports = {
41.     findAffectedProjectRoots,
42. };
43.
```

</details>

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_execution_record_phase5_workspace_invalidation.md

<details>
<summary>236 lines</summary>

````
1. # vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 5：workspace watcher 驱动缓存失效）
2. 
3. ## 1. 当前进度定位
4. 
5. 到本轮开始时，Phase 3 的缓存主干已经基本完成：
6. 
7. 1. 项目快照缓存
8. 2. 自动/手动失效
9. 3. 单文件扫描复用
10. 4. LRU 逐出
11. 5. cache manifest store
12. 
13. 但总体计划里的下一大块 —— **“文件系统变更可自动使缓存失效”** —— 还没有落地。
14. 
15. 也就是说，即使当前缓存已经能自动校验工程文件是否变化，扩展层仍然没有一个**工作区文件变化入口**，去在文件发生 create/change/delete 时主动让受影响工程根失效。
16. 
17. 因此本轮推进两件事：
18. 
19. 1. 新增纯函数模块，负责把“变化文件”映射到“受影响工程根”
20. 2. 在 `activate()` 里接入 `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher(...)`
21. 
22. ---
23. 
24. ## 2. 本轮目标
25. 
26. 本轮只做第一版、企业级稳健优先的 watcher 失效路径：
27. 
28. 1. 新增 `src/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.js`
29. 2. 提供：
30.    - `findAffectedProjectRoots(changedFilePath, manifestEntries)`
31. 3. 新增扩展层辅助：
32.    - `createManifestDrivenInvalidator({ indexClient, findAffectedRoots })`
33. 4. 在 `activate()` 中注册：
34.    - `**/*.{k,key,dyna}` watcher
35. 5. watcher 在以下事件触发时使受影响根缓存失效：
36.    - change
37.    - create
38.    - delete
39. 
40. 本轮**不做**：
41. 
42. - 自动重扫并刷新 UI
43. - watcher debounce / 批处理
44. - 文档内实时编辑增量
45. - worker 后台刷新
46. 
47. ---
48. 
49. ## 3. 设计选择
50. 
51. ## 3.1 先“失效”，不直接“自动重建”
52. 
53. 本轮 watcher 收到文件变化后，只做一件事：
54. 
55. > 找到受影响工程根并调用 `indexClient.invalidate(rootFile)`
56. 
57. 而不立即：
58. 
59. - 重建项目快照
60. - 刷新 Include Tree
61. - 刷新 Keyword Index
62. 
63. 原因是当前阶段先保证**缓存正确性**更重要：
64. 
65. 1. 文件变了，旧缓存不能继续被命中
66. 2. 至于何时重建，可以继续由用户触发扫描或后续 worker/watcher 批处理机制决定
67. 
68. 这样更稳健，也避免把 Phase 5 一次性做得过重。
69. 
70. ## 3.2 用 manifest 做“变化文件 -> 工程根”反查
71. 
72. 既然上一轮已经把 `cacheManifestStore` 建成了正式边界，本轮就直接复用 manifest：
73. 
74. 1. watcher 拿到变化文件路径
75. 2. `indexClient.getManifestEntries()` 提供当前工程清单
76. 3. `findAffectedProjectRoots(...)` 根据 `trackedFiles` 找出所有受影响根
77. 4. 对这些根逐个 `invalidate()`
78. 
79. 这让 watcher 不需要自己维护反向依赖表，逻辑更聚焦，也更容易测试。
80. 
81. ---
82. 
83. ## 4. TDD 过程
84. 
85. ### 4.1 先写失败测试
86. 
87. 本轮先新增三组约束：
88. 
89. #### A. `test/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.test.js`
90. 
91. 验证：
92. 
93. 1. 同一个变化文件可匹配多个工程根
94. 2. Windows 风格路径别名 / 大小写差异能够正确归一化命中
95. 
96. #### B. `test/extension.test.js`
97. 
98. 验证：
99. 
100. 1. `activate()` 必须注册 `**/*.{k,key,dyna}` watcher
101. 2. `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)` 必须对受影响根逐个 `invalidate()`
102. 3. 未被 manifest 跟踪的文件变化必须被忽略
103. 
104. 先执行聚焦测试：
105. 
106. ```powershell
107. npx mocha test\core\incremental\fileInvalidation.test.js test\extension.test.js --grep "findAffectedProjectRoots|workspace watcher|createManifestDrivenInvalidator"
108. ```
109. 
110. 结果先红，失败原因符合预期：
111. 
112. - `fileInvalidation` 模块不存在
113. - `createManifestDrivenInvalidator` 不存在
114. - `activate()` 还没有注册 watcher
115. 
116. ### 4.2 再写最小实现
117. 
118. 本轮实现顺序：
119. 
120. 1. 新增 `src/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.js`
121. 2. 新增 `findAffectedProjectRoots(...)`
122. 3. 给 `indexClient` 增加：
123.    - `getManifestEntries()`
124. 4. 在 `src/extension.js` 中新增：
125.    - `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)`
126. 5. 在 `activate()` 中注册 workspace watcher，并把 change/create/delete 都接到该 invalidator
127. 
128. ### 4.3 验证转绿
129. 
130. 聚焦测试转绿后，再跑全量测试，确保 watcher 接线没有破坏现有激活、快照、provider 等路径。
131. 
132. ---
133. 
134. ## 5. 实际代码改动
135. 
136. ## 5.1 `src/core/incremental/fileInvalidation.js`
137. 
138. 本轮新增了第一版文件失效辅助模块。
139. 
140. 职责非常明确：
141. 
142. 1. 输入：变化文件路径 + 当前 manifest 条目
143. 2. 输出：受影响工程根列表
144. 
145. 模块内部做了：
146. 
147. - 路径规范化
148. - Windows 下大小写归一化
149. - 重复根去重
150. 
151. ## 5.2 `src/client/services/indexClient.js`
152. 
153. 新增：
154. 
155. - `getManifestEntries()`
156. 
157. 使扩展层可以读取当前 manifest 条目，而不用直接依赖 manifest store 内部实现。
158. 
159. ## 5.3 `src/extension.js`
160. 
161. 新增：
162. 
163. - `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)`
164. 
165. 并在 `activate()` 中正式注册：
166. 
167. - `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher('**/*.{k,key,dyna}')`
168. 
169. 让工作区文件变化能够自动把相关工程缓存置为失效状态。
170. 
171. ## 5.4 测试桩
172. 
173. `test/vscode-mock.js` 补了最小 watcher mock，避免激活路径在测试环境下缺失 API。
174. 
175. ---
176. 
177. ## 6. 设计取舍
178. 
179. ## 6.1 先支持多根失效，而不是只失效当前活动工程
180. 
181. 同一个子文件可能被多个工程根复用。因此本轮设计不是“只失效活动文档对应工程”，而是：
182. 
183. - manifest 中凡是跟踪了该文件的根，全部失效
184. 
185. 这更符合企业级稳健性，也能避免交叉工程污染缓存。
186. 
187. ## 6.2 先让 watcher 成为缓存边界的一部分
188. 
189. 本轮 watcher 只做缓存边界治理，不直接触发 UI 行为变化。这样后续要加：
190. 
191. - debounce
192. - batching
193. - worker refresh
194. 
195. 都可以在现有边界上逐步演进。
196. 
197. ---
198. 
199. ## 7. 验证结果
200. 
201. 执行：
202. 
203. ```powershell
204. npx mocha test\core\incremental\fileInvalidation.test.js test\extension.test.js --grep "findAffectedProjectRoots|workspace watcher|createManifestDrivenInvalidator"
205. npm test
206. git --no-pager diff --check
207. ```
208. 
209. 本轮提交前以上检查均通过。
210. 
211. ---
212. 
213. ## 8. 对总体计划的推进意义
214. 
215. 做到这一步后，缓存层已经不只是“等下次访问时才发现过期”，而是开始具备：
216. 
217. 1. **工作区文件变化入口**
218. 2. **基于 manifest 的受影响工程根反查**
219. 3. **主动失效路径**
220. 
221. 这意味着总体计划里的：
222. 
223. > 文件系统变更可自动使缓存失效
224. 
225. 已经开始进入可运行状态。后续下一层更自然的能力就是：
226. 
227. 1. watcher debounce / batching
228. 2. 自动后台重建
229. 3. worker / server 侧调度
230. 
231. ---
232. 
233. ## 9. 一句话结论
234. 
235. **本轮把工作区文件 watcher 正式接到了缓存失效链路上：文件一旦发生 create/change/delete，扩展就能基于 manifest 找到受影响工程根并主动使其快照缓存失效。**
236.
````

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\cache\cacheManifestStore.js.**

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\cache\cacheManifestStore.js

<details>
<summary>113 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const path = require('path');
4. 
5. function resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile) {
6.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
7.         throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a rootFile path');
8.     }
9.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
10. }
11. 
12. function getManifestRootKey(rootFile) {
13.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile);
14.     return process.platform === 'win32'
15.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
16.         : resolvedRootFile;
17. }
18. 
19. function normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles = []) {
20.     const normalizedFiles = [];
21.     const seen = new Set();
22. 
23.     for (const filePath of trackedFiles) {
24.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveManifestRootFile(filePath);
25.         const fileKey = getManifestRootKey(resolvedFilePath);
26.         if (seen.has(fileKey)) continue;
27.         seen.add(fileKey);
28.         normalizedFiles.push(resolvedFilePath);
29.     }
30. 
31.     return normalizedFiles;
32. }
33. 
34. function cloneManifestEntry(entry) {
35.     if (!entry) return null;
36.     return {
37.         rootFile: entry.rootFile,
38.         trackedFiles: [...entry.trackedFiles],
39.         trackedFileCount: entry.trackedFileCount,
40.         byteSize: entry.byteSize,
41.         lastAccessedAt: entry.lastAccessedAt,
42.     };
43. }
44. 
45. function createCacheManifestStore() {
46.     const entries = new Map();
47. 
48.     function upsert({ rootFile, trackedFiles, byteSize, lastAccessedAt }) {
49.         if (typeof byteSize !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(byteSize) || byteSize < 0) {
50.             throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a non-negative byteSize');
51.         }
52.         if (typeof lastAccessedAt !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(lastAccessedAt) || lastAccessedAt < 0) {
53.             throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a non-negative lastAccessedAt');
54.         }
55. 
56.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile);
57.         const normalizedTrackedFiles = normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles && trackedFiles.length > 0
58.             ? trackedFiles
59.             : [resolvedRootFile]);
60.         const entry = {
61.             rootFile: resolvedRootFile,
62.             trackedFiles: normalizedTrackedFiles,
63.             trackedFileCount: normalizedTrackedFiles.length,
64.             byteSize,
65.             lastAccessedAt,
66.         };
67. 
68.         entries.set(getManifestRootKey(resolvedRootFile), entry);
69.         return cloneManifestEntry(entry);
70.     }
71. 
72.     function get(rootFile) {
73.         return cloneManifestEntry(entries.get(getManifestRootKey(rootFile)));
74.     }
75. 
76.     function remove(rootFile) {
77.         return entries.delete(getManifestRootKey(rootFile));
78.     }
79. 
80.     function list() {
81.         return [...entries.values()]
82.             .sort((left, right) => right.lastAccessedAt - left.lastAccessedAt)
83.             .map(cloneManifestEntry);
84.     }
85. 
86.     function getStats() {
87.         let entryCount = 0;
88.         let totalBytes = 0;
89. 
90.         for (const entry of entries.values()) {
91.             entryCount += 1;
92.             totalBytes += entry.byteSize;
93.         }
94. 
95.         return {
96.             entryCount,
97.             totalBytes,
98.         };
99.     }
100. 
101.     return {
102.         get,
103.         getStats,
104.         list,
105.         remove,
106.         upsert,
107.     };
108. }
109. 
110. module.exports = {
111.     createCacheManifestStore,
112. };
113.
```

</details>

```
pm test
> lsdyna@2.0.7 test
> mocha test/**/*.test.js:\WINDOWS\system32\cmd.exe 

  createIndexClient
    ✔ exposes loadProjectSnapshot as the shared snapshot entry point
    ✔ reuses cached snapshots across normalized root aliases
    ✔ rebuilds cached snapshots after invalidation across normalized root aliases
    ✔ bypasses stale in-flight loads after invalidation
    ✔ rebuilds cached snapshots when a tracked project file changes
    ✔ rebuilds cached snapshots when a tracked project file disappears
    ✔ evicts the least recently used snapshot when the cache size limit is exceeded
    ✔ updates snapshot cache stats after invalidation
    ✔ updates the manifest store when caching, touching, and invalidating snapshots
    ✔ rebuilds instead of restoring stale manifest entries when invalidated during cache validation  createCacheManifestStore
    ✔ normalizes root aliases when storing and reading manifest entries
    ✔ lists manifest entries in most-recently-used order with aggregate stats  findAffectedProjectRoots
    ✔ finds all project roots whose manifest tracks the changed file

    ✔ records missing includes without aborting the rest of the project scan

    ✔ preserves missing includes as tree nodes in include order
    ✔ preserves duplicate missing includes as separate tree nodes in stable order

    ✔ always includes the document directory as first path

    ✔ resolves *INCLUDE_PATH_RELATIVE against document directory

    ✔ invalidates cached include parse results when the document version changes

```
44. async function captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature) {
45.     const trackedFiles = [];
46.     const seen = new Set();
47. 
48.     for (const filePath of getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot)) {
49.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveRootFile(filePath);
50.         const fileCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
51.         if (seen.has(fileCacheKey)) continue;
52.         seen.add(fileCacheKey);
53.         trackedFiles.push({
54.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
55.             signature: await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath),
56.         });
57.     }
58. 
59.     return trackedFiles;
60. }
61. 
62. async function isSnapshotValid(entry, getFileSignature) {
63.     if (!Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles) || entry.trackedFiles.length === 0) {
64.         return false;
65.     }
66. 
67.     for (const trackedFile of entry.trackedFiles) {
68.         try {
69.             const currentSignature = await getFileSignature(trackedFile.filePath);
70.             if (!areFileSignaturesEqual(currentSignature, trackedFile.signature)) {
71.                 return false;
72.             }
73.         } catch (_error) {
74.             return false;
75.         }
76.     }
77. 
78.     return true;
79. }
80.
```

</details>

19. function normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles = []) {
20.     const normalizedFiles = [];
21.     const seen = new Set();
22. 
23.     for (const filePath of trackedFiles) {
24.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveManifestRootFile(filePath);
25.         const fileKey = getManifestRootKey(resolvedFilePath);
26.         if (seen.has(fileKey)) continue;
27.         seen.add(fileKey);
28.         normalizedFiles.push(resolvedFilePath);
29.     }
30. 
31.     return normalizedFiles;
32. }
```

### 💬 Copilot

Perfect. I can now trace through the complete flow. Let me verify there are no issues:

1. **When a snapshot is created**: `trackedFiles` are stored as `trackedFile.filePath` (which are already resolved via `resolveRootFile` in line 49 of indexClient.js)
2. **manifestStore.upsert**: receives these resolved paths and normalizes them again via `normalizeTrackedFiles` (which calls `resolveManifestRootFile` → `path.resolve`)
3. **manifestStore returns**: trackedFiles that are normalized (resolved + case-normalized on Windows)
4. **When file changes**: 
   - VSCode watcher provides `uri.fsPath`
   - `fileInvalidation.findAffectedProjectRoots` receives this path
   - It normalizes via `getTrackedFileKey` (which calls `resolveTrackedFile` → `path.resolve` + lowercase on Windows)
   - It compares against manifest entries' trackedFiles using the same normalization

```json
{
  "command": "cd D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna; node -e \"\nconst path = require('path');\n\n// Simulate fileInvalidation module\nfunction resolveTrackedFile(filePath) {\n    if (typeof filePath !== 'string' || filePath.trim() === '') {\n        throw new TypeError('file invalidation requires a file path');\n    }\n    return path.resolve(filePath);\n}\n\nfunction getTrackedFileKey(filePath) {\n    const resolvedFilePath = resolveTrackedFile(filePath);\n    return process.platform === 'win32'\n        ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()\n        : resolvedFilePath;\n}\n\nfunction findAffectedProjectRoots(changedFilePath, manifestEntries = []) {\n    const changedFileKey = getTrackedFileKey(changedFilePath);\n    const affectedRoots = [];\n    const seenRoots = new Set();\n\n    for (const entry of manifestEntries) {\n        if (!entry || typeof entry.rootFile !== 'string' || !Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles)) continue;\n        const rootFile = path.resolve(entry.rootFile);\n        const rootFileKey = getTrackedFileKey(rootFile);\n        if (seenRoots.has(rootFileKey)) continue;\n\n        const matchesChangedFile = entry.trackedFiles.some(trackedFile => getTrackedFileKey(trackedFile) === changedFileKey);\n        if (!matchesChangedFile) continue;\n\n        seenRoots.add(rootFileKey);\n        affectedRoots.push(rootFile);\n    }\n\n    return affectedRoots;\n}\n\n// Test edge cases\nconsole.log('Edge case 1: Empty manifest');\nconsole.log(findAffectedProjectRoots('test.k', []));\nconsole.log('');\n\nconsole.log('Edge case 2: Malformed entries');\nconst malformedEntries = [\n    null,\n    undefined,\n    {},\n    { rootFile: 'valid.k' },\n    { trackedFiles: [] },\n    { rootFile: 'valid.k', trackedFiles: 'not-an-array' },\n    { rootFile: 123, trackedFiles: [] },\n];\nconsole.log(findAffectedProjectRoots('test.k', malformedEntries));\nconsole.log('');\n\nconsole.log('Edge case 3: Valid entry');\nconst validEntries = [\n    { rootFile: 'project/main.k', trackedFiles: ['project/main.k', 'project/test.k'] },\n];\nconsole.log(findAffectedProjectRoots('project/test.k', validEntries));\n\"",
  "description": "Test edge cases in fileInvalidation"
}

Excellent! All focused tests pass. Let me now perform one final critical check - I want to verify that the watcher won't cause issues if files are changed in rapid succession or if manifest entries contain the same root multiple times:

### ✅ `powershell`

**Test duplicate root handling**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "cd D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna; node -e \"\nconst path = require('path');\n\n// Test duplicate root detection\nfunction resolveTrackedFile(filePath) {\n    if (typeof filePath !== 'string' || filePath.trim() === '') {\n        throw new TypeError('file invalidation requires a file path');\n    }\n    return path.resolve(filePath);\n}\n\nfunction getTrackedFileKey(filePath) {\n    const resolvedFilePath = resolveTrackedFile(filePath);\n    return process.platform === 'win32'\n        ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()\n        : resolvedFilePath;\n}\n\nfunction findAffectedProjectRoots(changedFilePath, manifestEntries = []) {\n    const changedFileKey = getTrackedFileKey(changedFilePath);\n    const affectedRoots = [];\n    const seenRoots = new Set();\n\n    for (const entry of manifestEntries) {\n        if (!entry || typeof entry.rootFile !== 'string' || !Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles)) continue;\n        const rootFile = path.resolve(entry.rootFile);\n        const rootFileKey = getTrackedFileKey(rootFile);\n        if (seenRoots.has(rootFileKey)) continue;\n\n        const matchesChangedFile = entry.trackedFiles.some(trackedFile => getTrackedFileKey(trackedFile) === changedFileKey);\n        if (!matchesChangedFile) continue;\n\n        seenRoots.add(rootFileKey);\n        affectedRoots.push(rootFile);\n    }\n\n    return affectedRoots;\n}\n\n// Test: Same root in manifest multiple times\nconsole.log('Test: Duplicate roots in manifest');\nconst rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');\nconst childFile = path.resolve('project', 'child.k');\nconst manifestWithDuplicates = [\n    { rootFile, trackedFiles: [rootFile, childFile] },\n    { rootFile, trackedFiles: [rootFile, childFile] },\n    { rootFile: rootFile.toUpperCase(), trackedFiles: [rootFile, childFile] }, // case variation\n];\nconst affectedRoots = findAffectedProjectRoots(childFile, manifestWithDuplicates);\nconsole.log('Affected roots:', affectedRoots);\nconsole.log('Count:', affectedRoots.length);\nconsole.log('Expected: 1 (deduplication should work)');\nconsole.log('PASS:', affectedRoots.length === 1);\n\"",
  "description": "Test duplicate root handling"
}
```

fileUriOrPath\n            : fileUriOrPath && fileUriOrPath.fsPath;\n        if (!changedFilePath) return;\n\n        const affectedRoots = findAffectedRoots(changedFilePath, indexClient.getManifestEntries());\n        for (const rootFile of affectedRoots) {\n            indexClient.invalidate(rootFile);\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n// Mock\nconst mockIndexClient = {\n    invalidate: (root) => console.log('Invalidated:', root),\n    getManifestEntries: () => [],\n};\nconst mockFindAffectedRoots = () => [];\n\nconst invalidator = createManifestDrivenInvalidator({\n    indexClient: mockIndexClient,\n    findAffectedRoots: mockFindAffectedRoots,\n});\n\n// Test edge cases\nconsole.log('Test 1: null input');\ninvalidator(null);\nconsole.log('No error - PASS');\n\nconsole.log('');\nconsole.log('Test 2: undefined input');\ninvalidator(undefined);\nconsole.log('No error - PASS');\n\nconsole.log('');\nconsole.log('Test 3: empty object');\ninvalidator({});\nconsole.log('No error - PASS');\n\nconsole.log('');\nconsole.log('Test 4: object without fsPath');\ninvalidator({ someProp: 'value' });\nconsole.log('No error - PASS');\n\nconsole.log('');\nconsole.log('Test 5: URI with fsPath');\ninvalidator({ fsPath: 'D:/test.k' });\nconsole.log('No error - PASS');\n\"",

### 💬 Copilot

Excellent! All edge cases are handled gracefully. Now let me do a final comprehensive review of the entire change to make sure I haven't missed any substantive issues:

## Review Summary

I have completed a thorough review of the uncommitted changes. Here are my findings:

### **PASS**

No significant issues found in the reviewed changes.

---

### **Verification Performed:**

1. ✅ **All tests pass** (109 passing tests, including 5 new tests for this feature)

2. ✅ **Path normalization is consistent** across all modules:
   - `fileInvalidation.js` uses `getTrackedFileKey()` 
   - `cacheManifestStore.js` uses `getManifestRootKey()`
   - Both apply the same logic: `path.resolve()` + lowercase on Windows
   - Tested cross-module compatibility - normalization keys match

3. ✅ **Edge cases are handled correctly:**
   - Empty manifest → returns empty array, no error
   - Malformed manifest entries → skipped gracefully
   - Null/undefined input to invalidator → early return, no crash
   - Duplicate roots in manifest → deduplicated correctly
   - File URI vs string path → handled transparently via `uri.fsPath`

4. ✅ **Watcher pattern matches language definition:**
   - Watcher: `**/*.{k,key,dyna}`
   - Language extensions in package.json: `[".k", ".key", ".dyna"]`
   - Complete coverage

5. ✅ **Initialization order is safe:**
   - `indexClient` created first
   - `invalidateChangedProjectRoots` created with initialized `indexClient`
   - Watcher registered last with all dependencies ready

6. ✅ **Invalidation is synchronous and fast** (just Map operations, no I/O)

7. ✅ **Integration path is verified by existing tests:**
   - Test: "rebuilds cached snapshots when a tracked project file changes"
   - Tests verify cache invalidation → rebuild flow

---

### **Test Coverage Assessment:**

The current tests are **sufficient** for the intended scope. Coverage includes:

**Core logic (`fileInvalidation.js`):**
- ✅ Multi-root invalidation when same file is tracked by multiple projects
- ✅ Case-insensitive path matching on Windows

**Integration (`extension.js`):**
- ✅ Watcher registration with correct glob pattern
- ✅ `createManifestDrivenInvalidator` invalidates all affected roots
- ✅ Untracked files are ignored

**Missing but acceptable given Phase 5 scope:**
- ❌ No test for rapid successive file changes (acceptable - debouncing explicitly deferred to future phase per documentation)
- ❌ No end-to-end integration test from watcher event → cache rebuild (acceptable - existing indexClient tests verify the rebuild path separately)

The test coverage aligns with the documented Phase 5 goals: **invalidation only, no auto-rebuild**.

---

### **Conclusion:**

**PASS** - The implementation is correct, safe, and well-tested for its intended scope. The changes:
- Maintain backward compatibility (all existing tests pass)
- Handle edge cases gracefully
- Use consistent path normalization across modules
- Follow the documented design (invalidate only, defer auto-rebuild)
- Have appropriate test coverage for the feature scope


---

13. const LARGE_DOCUMENT_LINE_THRESHOLD = 100000;
14. const STREAM_SCAN_YIELD_INTERVAL = 50000;
15. const includeDirectiveCache = new WeakMap();
16. 
17. // --- Folding ---
18. 
19. class LsDynaFoldingProvider {
20.     provideFoldingRanges(document) {
21.         if (shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) return [];
22. 
23.         const ranges = [];
24.         let foldStart = -1;
25. 
26.         for (let i = 0; i < document.lineCount; i++) {
27.             if (/^\*/.test(document.lineAt(i).text)) {
28.                 if (foldStart !== -1 && i - 1 > foldStart) {
29.                     ranges.push(new vscode.FoldingRange(foldStart, i - 1));
30.                 }
31.                 foldStart = i;
32.             }
33.         }
34. 
35.         if (foldStart !== -1 && document.lineCount - 1 > foldStart) {
36.             ranges.push(new vscode.FoldingRange(foldStart, document.lineCount - 1));
37.         }
38. 
39.         return ranges;
40.     }
41. }
42. 
43. // --- Symbol Provider ---
44. 
45. class LsdynaKeywordSymbolProvider {
46.     provideDocumentSymbols(document) {
47.         if (shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) return [];
48. 
49.         const symbols = [];
50.         for (let i = 0; i < document.lineCount; i++) {
51.             const line = document.lineAt(i);
52.             if (line.text.startsWith('*')) {
53.                 symbols.push(new vscode.DocumentSymbol(
54.                     line.text.trim(),
55.                     '',
56.                     vscode.SymbolKind.Property,
57.                     line.range,
58.                     line.range
59.                 ));
60.             }
61.         }
62.         return symbols;
63.     }
64. }
65. 
66. // --- Document Link Provider ---
67. 
68. class LsdynaDocumentLinkProvider {
69.     provideDocumentLinks(document) {
70.         return collectIncludeDocumentLinks(document);
71.     }
72. }
73. 
74. // --- Helpers ---
75. 
76. function getIncludeDirectiveData(document) {
77.     const version = document.version ?? null;
78.     const cached = includeDirectiveCache.get(document);
79.     if (cached && cached.version === version) {
80.         return cached.value;
```

<sub>⏱️ 664m 27s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase5_watcher_batching.md\n+# vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 5：watcher 失效批处理）\n+\n+## 1. 当前进度定位\n+\n+上一轮已经完成：\n+\n+1. `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher('**/*.{k,key,dyna}')`\n+2. 基于 manifest 的“变化文件 -> 受影响工程根”反查\n+3. 工作区文件 create/change/delete 时主动调用 `indexClient.invalidate(rootFile)`\n+\n+但仍然有一个明显的企业级稳健性缺口：\n+\n+> **高频保存或短时间内多个文件事件，仍可能导致同一工程根被重复失效多次。**\n+\n+虽然当前 `invalidate()` 的成本不高，但这条链路后面还会继续接：\n+\n+- watcher 驱动的后台重建\n+- worker 调度\n+- 更重的缓存恢复逻辑\n+\n+因此现在就应该把 watcher 路径做成**批处理去抖**，避免后续放大成真正的排队风暴。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. 本轮目标\n+\n+本轮聚焦一个很小但很关键的质量增强：\n+\n+1. 为 manifest 驱动的 watcher 失效增加 batching / debounce\n+2. 确保同一批次里同一根只失效一次\n+3. 确保不同文件事件命中的多个根可以合并到同一批次统一失效\n+\n+本轮**不做**：\n+\n+- 自动后台重建\n+- worker job batching\n+- 文件事件优先级\n+- watcher telemetry\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. 设计选择\n+\n+## 3.1 保持“批处理失效”而不是“批处理重建”\n+\n+本轮仍然只对 watcher 事件做：\n+\n+- 收集\n+- 去重\n+- 延迟一次性 `invalidate()`\n+\n+而不直接批量重建。\n+\n+这样更稳妥，因为当前阶段先解决的是：\n+\n+1. 重复失效\n+2. 同根多次命中\n+3. 高频保存时的稳定性\n+\n+真正的后台重建可以在下一层 worker 设计里继续做。\n+\n+## 3.2 在扩展层做最小调度，不下沉到核心缓存模块\n+\n+这一版 batching 发生在 watcher 使用点附近，而不是直接塞到 `indexClient` 或 `fileInvalidation` 核心模块里。\n+\n+原因是：\n+\n+1. batching 是“事件调度策略”，不是缓存核心语义\n+2. `indexClient.invalidate()` 继续保持同步、直接、可预测\n+3. 后续如果要换成更复杂的调度器，可以只替换扩展层\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. TDD 过程\n+\n+### 4.1 先写失败测试\n+\n+本轮先在 `test/extension.test.js` 新增两条约束：\n+\n+1. **同一根被快速重复命中时，只失效一次**\n+2. **同一批次内不同文件命中多个根时，能够合并失效**\n+\n+先执行聚焦测试：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\extension.test.js --grep \"createBatchedManifestInvalidator\"\n+```\n+\n+结果先红，失败原因正确：\n+\n+- `createBatchedManifestInvalidator` 不存在\n+\n+### 4.2 再写最小实现\n+\n+本轮新增：\n+\n+- `createBatchedManifestInvalidator(...)`\n+\n+核心逻辑：\n+\n+1. 复用已有 `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)` 的受影响根定位语义\n+2. 用 `Map` 累积待失效根\n+3. 每次新文件事件到来时重置 timer\n+4. timer 到期后一次性逐根调用 `invalidate()`\n+\n+并在 `activate()` 中把 watcher 事件入口从：\n+\n+- 立即失效\n+\n+切换为：\n+\n+- batched invalidation\n+\n+### 4.3 验证转绿\n+\n+聚焦测试转绿后，再跑全量测试，确认现有激活路径和 watcher 集成没有退化。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. 实际代码改动\n+\n+## 5.1 `src/extension.js`\n+\n+本轮新增：\n+\n+- `createBatchedManifestInvalidator(...)`\n+\n+并让 `activate()` 里的 watcher 改用该 batched invalidator。\n+\n+实现细节保持很小：\n+\n+1. `delayMs` 默认 100ms\n+2. `schedule/cancel` 可注入，便于测试\n+3. 根去重时做了跨平台 key 处理\n+\n+## 5.2 `test/extension.test.js`\n+\n+新增两条行为测试：\n+\n+1. 高频同根事件合并\n+2. 多根事件合并\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 6. 验证结果\n+\n+执行：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\extension.test.js --grep \"createBatchedManifestInvalidator\"\n+npm test\n+git --no-pager diff --check\n+```\n+\n+本轮提交前以上检查均通过。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 7. 对总体计划的推进意义\n+\n+做到这一步后，Phase 5 的 watcher 路径已经不仅仅是“能失效”，而是更进一步具备：\n+\n+1. manifest 驱动失效\n+2. 同根去重\n+3. 高频事件批处理\n+\n+这让下一步接：\n+\n+1. 后台自动重建\n+2. worker 调度\n+3. 更复杂的刷新策略\n+\n+时，底层事件入口更稳定，也更接近企业级使用要求。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 8. 一句话结论\n+\n+**本轮把 watcher 驱动的缓存失效从“立即逐次执行”提升为了“批处理去抖执行”，让高频文件事件下的缓存失效路径更稳、更可扩展。**\n*** End Patch\n"
````

</details>

diff --git a/src/extension.js b/src/extension.js
index d0a1b0a..60a2000 100644
--- a/src/extension.js
+++ b/src/extension.js
@@ -671,7 +671,7 @@ function activate(context) {
     );
 
     const indexClient = createIndexClient({ buildProjectIndex });
-    const invalidateChangedProjectRoots = createManifestDrivenInvalidator({ indexClient });
+    const invalidateChangedProjectRoots = createBatchedManifestInvalidator({ indexClient });
     const includeTreeProvider = new LsdynaIncludeTreeProvider({
         searchFileFromPaths,
         loadProjectSnapshot: indexClient.loadProjectSnapshot,
@@ -906,6 +906,45 @@ function createManifestDrivenInvalidator({ indexClient, findAffectedRoots = find
     };
 }
 
+function createBatchedManifestInvalidator({
+    indexClient,
+    findAffectedRoots = findAffectedProjectRoots,
+    delayMs = 100,
+    schedule = setTimeout,
+    cancel = clearTimeout,
+} = {}) {
+    const invalidateChangedFile = createManifestDrivenInvalidator({ indexClient, findAffectedRoots });
+    let timer = null;
+    const pendingRoots = new Map();
+
+    return function queueChangedFile(fileUriOrPath) {
+        const changedFilePath = typeof fileUriOrPath === 'string'
+            ? fileUriOrPath
+            : fileUriOrPath && fileUriOrPath.fsPath;
+        if (!changedFilePath) return;
+
+        const affectedRoots = findAffectedRoots(changedFilePath, indexClient.getManifestEntries());
+        for (const rootFile of affectedRoots) {
+            const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);
+            const rootKey = process.platform === 'win32'
+                ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
+                : resolvedRootFile;
+            pendingRoots.set(rootKey, rootFile);
+        }
+        if (pendingRoots.size === 0) return;
+
+        if (timer) cancel(timer);
+        timer = schedule(() => {
+            timer = null;
+            const roots = [...pendingRoots.values()];
+            pendingRoots.clear();
+            for (const rootFile of roots) {
+                invalidateChangedFile(rootFile);
+            }
+        }, delayMs);
+    };
+}
+
 module.exports = { activate, deactivate };
 
 // Exported for unit testing
@@ -937,4 +976,5 @@ module.exports._internals = {
     findNextKeyword,
     findPreviousKeyword,
     createManifestDrivenInvalidator,
+    createBatchedManifestInvalidator,
 };

diff --git a/test/extension.test.js b/test/extension.test.js
index 829ad97..38f57d1 100644
--- a/test/extension.test.js
+++ b/test/extension.test.js
@@ -35,6 +35,7 @@ const {
     collectIncludeFiles,
     shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan,
     createManifestDrivenInvalidator,
+    createBatchedManifestInvalidator,
 } = extensionModule._internals;
 
 const FIXTURE_DIR = path.join(__dirname, 'Bolt_A_Explicit');
@@ -856,6 +857,77 @@ describe('createManifestDrivenInvalidator', () => {
     });
 });
 
+describe('createBatchedManifestInvalidator', () => {
+    it('coalesces rapid file events into one invalidation per affected root', () => {
+        const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'root.k');
+        const changedFile = path.resolve('project', 'shared.key');
+        const invalidatedRoots = [];
+        const scheduled = new Map();
+        let nextTimerId = 1;
+        const batchedInvalidator = createBatchedManifestInvalidator({
+            indexClient: {
+                getManifestEntries() {
+                    return [{ rootFile, trackedFiles: [rootFile, changedFile] }];
+                },
+                invalidate(root) {
+                    invalidatedRoots.push(root);
+                },
+            },
+            delayMs: 50,
+            schedule(callback) {
+                const timerId = nextTimerId++;
+                scheduled.set(timerId, callback);
+                return timerId;
+            },
+            cancel(timerId) {
+                scheduled.delete(timerId);
+            },
+        });
+
+        batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: changedFile });
+        batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: changedFile });
+        scheduled.forEach(callback => callback());
+
+        assert.deepEqual(invalidatedRoots, [rootFile]);
+    });
+
+    it('batches multiple roots discovered across rapid file events', () => {
+        const rootA = path.resolve('project', 'root-a.k');
+        const rootB = path.resolve('project', 'root-b.k');
+        const invalidatedRoots = [];
+        const scheduled = new Map();
+        let nextTimerId = 1;
+        const batchedInvalidator = createBatchedManifestInvalidator({
+            indexClient: {
+                getManifestEntries() {
+                    return [
+                        { rootFile: rootA, trackedFiles: [rootA, path.resolve('project', 'a.key')] },
+                        { rootFile: rootB, trackedFiles: [rootB, path.resolve('project', 'b.key')] },
+                    ];
+                },
+                invalidate(root) {
+                    invalidatedRoots.push(root);
+                },
+            },
+            delayMs: 50,
+            schedule(callback) {
+                const timerId = nextTimerId++;
+                scheduled.set(timerId, callback);
+                return timerId;
+            },
+            cancel(timerId) {
+                scheduled.delete(timerId);
+            },
+        });
+
+        batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: path.resolve('project', 'a.key') });
+        batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: path.resolve('project', 'b.key') });
+        scheduled.forEach(callback => callback());
+
+        assert.deepEqual(invalidatedRoots.sort(), [rootA, rootB].sort());
+    });
+});
+
 // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 // Debounce helpers
 // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. # vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 5：watcher 失效批处理）
2. 
3. ## 1. 当前进度定位
4. 
5. 上一轮已经完成：
6. 
7. 1. `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher('**/*.{k,key,dyna}')`
8. 2. 基于 manifest 的“变化文件 -> 受影响工程根”反查
9. 3. 工作区文件 create/change/delete 时主动调用 `indexClient.invalidate(rootFile)`
10. 
11. 但仍然有一个明显的企业级稳健性缺口：
12. 
13. > **高频保存或短时间内多个文件事件，仍可能导致同一工程根被重复失效多次。**
14. 
15. 虽然当前 `invalidate()` 的成本不高，但这条链路后面还会继续接：
16. 
17. - watcher 驱动的后台重建
18. - worker 调度
19. - 更重的缓存恢复逻辑
20. 
21. 因此现在就应该把 watcher 路径做成**批处理去抖**，避免后续放大成真正的排队风暴。
22. 
23. ---
24. 
25. ## 2. 本轮目标
26. 
27. 本轮聚焦一个很小但很关键的质量增强：
28. 
29. 1. 为 manifest 驱动的 watcher 失效增加 batching / debounce
30. 2. 确保同一批次里同一根只失效一次
31. 3. 确保不同文件事件命中的多个根可以合并到同一批次统一失效
32. 
33. 本轮**不做**：
34. 
35. - 自动后台重建
36. - worker job batching
37. - 文件事件优先级
38. - watcher telemetry
39. 
40. ---
41. 
42. ## 3. 设计选择
43. 
44. ## 3.1 保持“批处理失效”而不是“批处理重建”
45. 
46. 本轮仍然只对 watcher 事件做：
47. 
48. - 收集
49. - 去重
50. - 延迟一次性 `invalidate()`
51. 
52. 而不直接批量重建。
53. 
54. 这样更稳妥，因为当前阶段先解决的是：
55. 
56. 1. 重复失效
57. 2. 同根多次命中
58. 3. 高频保存时的稳定性
59. 
60. 真正的后台重建可以在下一层 worker 设计里继续做。
61. 
62. ## 3.2 在扩展层做最小调度，不下沉到核心缓存模块
63. 
64. 这一版 batching 发生在 watcher 使用点附近，而不是直接塞到 `indexClient` 或 `fileInvalidation` 核心模块里。
65. 
66. 原因是：
67. 
68. 1. batching 是“事件调度策略”，不是缓存核心语义
69. 2. `indexClient.invalidate()` 继续保持同步、直接、可预测
70. 3. 后续如果要换成更复杂的调度器，可以只替换扩展层
71. 
72. ---
73. 
74. ## 4. TDD 过程
75. 
76. ### 4.1 先写失败测试
77. 
78. 本轮先在 `test/extension.test.js` 新增两条约束：
79. 
80. 1. **同一根被快速重复命中时，只失效一次**
81. 2. **同一批次内不同文件命中多个根时，能够合并失效**
82. 
83. 先执行聚焦测试：
84. 
85. ```powershell
86. npx mocha test\extension.test.js --grep "createBatchedManifestInvalidator"
87. ```
88. 
89. 结果先红，失败原因正确：
90. 
91. - `createBatchedManifestInvalidator` 不存在
92. 
93. ### 4.2 再写最小实现
94. 
95. 本轮新增：
96. 
97. - `createBatchedManifestInvalidator(...)`
98. 
99. 核心逻辑：
100. 
101. 1. 复用已有 `createManifestDrivenInvalidator(...)` 的受影响根定位语义
102. 2. 用 `Map` 累积待失效根
103. 3. 每次新文件事件到来时重置 timer
104. 4. timer 到期后一次性逐根调用 `invalidate()`
105. 
106. 并在 `activate()` 中把 watcher 事件入口从：
107. 
108. - 立即失效
109. 
110. 切换为：
111. 
112. - batched invalidation
113. 
114. ### 4.3 验证转绿
115. 
116. 聚焦测试转绿后，再跑全量测试，确认现有激活路径和 watcher 集成没有退化。
117. 
118. ---
119. 
120. ## 5. 实际代码改动
121. 
122. ## 5.1 `src/extension.js`
123. 
124. 本轮新增：
125. 
126. - `createBatchedManifestInvalidator(...)`
127. 
128. 并让 `activate()` 里的 watcher 改用该 batched invalidator。
129. 
130. 实现细节保持很小：
131. 
132. 1. `delayMs` 默认 100ms
133. 2. `schedule/cancel` 可注入，便于测试
134. 3. 根去重时做了跨平台 key 处理
135. 
136. ## 5.2 `test/extension.test.js`
137. 
138. 新增两条行为测试：
139. 
140. 1. 高频同根事件合并
141. 2. 多根事件合并
142. 
143. ---
144. 
145. ## 6. 验证结果
146. 
147. 执行：
148. 
149. ```powershell
150. npx mocha test\extension.test.js --grep "createBatchedManifestInvalidator"
151. npm test
152. git --no-pager diff --check
153. ```
154. 
155. 本轮提交前以上检查均通过。
156. 
157. ---
158. 
159. ## 7. 对总体计划的推进意义
160. 
161. 做到这一步后，Phase 5 的 watcher 路径已经不仅仅是“能失效”，而是更进一步具备：
162. 
163. 1. manifest 驱动失效
164. 2. 同根去重
165. 3. 高频事件批处理
166. 
167. 这让下一步接：
168. 
169. 1. 后台自动重建
170. 2. worker 调度
171. 3. 更复杂的刷新策略
172. 
173. 时，底层事件入口更稳定，也更接近企业级使用要求。
174. 
175. ---
176. 
177. ## 8. 一句话结论
178. 
179. **本轮把 watcher 驱动的缓存失效从“立即逐次执行”提升为了“批处理去抖执行”，让高频文件事件下的缓存失效路径更稳、更可扩展。**
180.
````

</details>

---

## FAIL

### Issue 1: Timer Reset Bug on Untracked File Events
**File:** D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\extension.js:934  
**Severity:** High  
**Problem:** The check `if (pendingRoots.size === 0) return;` occurs AFTER the loop that adds affected roots. This causes the timer to be unnecessarily cancelled and rescheduled when an untracked file event arrives while roots are already pending from a previous event.

**Evidence:**
```javascript
// Lines 926-934
const affectedRoots = findAffectedRoots(changedFilePath, indexClient.getManifestEntries());
for (const rootFile of affectedRoots) {
    const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);
    const rootKey = process.platform === 'win32'
        ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
        : resolvedRootFile;
    pendingRoots.set(rootKey, rootFile);
}
if (pendingRoots.size === 0) return; // ❌ BUG: Checked AFTER loop
```

**Impact:**  
- If a tracked file event schedules invalidation for 100ms
- And an untracked file event arrives 50ms later
- The timer is cancelled and reset to another 100ms
- This can delay invalidations indefinitely under heavy file activity

**Verification:** I ran a simulation that demonstrated:
1. Event 1 (tracked): schedules timer #1
2. Event 2 (untracked): cancels timer #1, schedules timer #2
3. Result: 2 timers scheduled, 1 cancelled (should be 1 scheduled, 0 cancelled)

**Suggested fix:**
```javascript
const affectedRoots = findAffectedRoots(changedFilePath, indexClient.getManifestEntries());
if (affectedRoots.length === 0) return; // Early exit if this event found no roots

for (const rootFile of affectedRoots) {
    const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);
    const rootKey = process.platform === 'win32'
        ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
        : resolvedRootFile;
    pendingRoots.set(rootKey, rootFile);
}

if (timer) cancel(timer);
timer = schedule(() =\> {
    timer = null;
    const roots = [...pendingRoots.values()];
    pendingRoots.clear();
    for (const rootFile of roots) {
        invalidateChangedFile(rootFile);
    }
}, delayMs);
```

---

### Issue 2: Missing Test Coverage
**File:** test/extension.test.js  
**Severity:** Medium  
**Problem:** Tests do not cover the critical edge case of untracked file events arriving while pending roots exist.

**Missing test cases:**
1. ❌ Untracked file event should not schedule a timer when no roots are pending
2. ❌ Untracked file event should not reset an existing timer when roots are pending
3. ❌ Test should verify timer count, not just final invalidation results

**Current tests only verify:**
- ✅ Same tracked file twice → coalesced into one invalidation
- ✅ Two different tracked files → batched into two invalidations

**Suggested additional test:**
```javascript
it('does not reschedule timer when untracked file events occur', () =\> {
    let timerScheduledCount = 0;
    let timerCancelledCount = 0;
    // ... setup ...
    
    batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: trackedFile });
    assert.strictEqual(timerScheduledCount, 1);
    
    batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: untrackedFile });
    assert.strictEqual(timerScheduledCount, 1); // Should NOT increment
    assert.strictEqual(timerCancelledCount, 0); // Should NOT cancel
});
```

---

### Test Results
- ✅ All 111 existing tests pass (no regressions)
- ✅ Activation correctly uses batched invalidator
- ✅ Watcher registration unchanged
- ✅ Platform-specific path normalization looks correct
- ❌ Bug allows timer to be reset by untracked file events
- ❌ Missing edge case test coverage

---

**Are current tests sufficient?** No. The tests verify correctness for the happy path but miss a critical

### 💬 Copilot

Based on my review, I have identified a substantive issue:

---

## FAIL

### Issue 1: Timer Reset Bug on Untracked File Events
**File:** D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\extension.js:934  
**Severity:** High  
**Problem:** The check `if (pendingRoots.size === 0) return;` occurs AFTER the loop that adds affected roots. This causes the timer to be unnecessarily cancelled and rescheduled when an untracked file event arrives while roots are already pending from a previous event.

**Evidence:**
```javascript
// Lines 926-934
const affectedRoots = findAffectedRoots(changedFilePath, indexClient.getManifestEntries());
for (const rootFile of affectedRoots) {
    const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);
    const rootKey = process.platform === 'win32'
        ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
        : resolvedRootFile;
    pendingRoots.set(rootKey, rootFile);
}
if (pendingRoots.size === 0) return; // ❌ BUG: Checked AFTER loop
```

**Impact:**  
- If a tracked file event schedules invalidation for 100ms
- And an untracked file event arrives 50ms later
- The timer is cancelled and reset to another 100ms
- This can delay invalidations indefinitely under heavy file activity

**Verification:** I ran a simulation that demonstrated:
1. Event 1 (tracked): schedules timer #1
2. Event 2 (untracked): cancels timer #1, schedules timer #2
3. Result: 2 timers scheduled, 1 cancelled (should be 1 scheduled, 0 cancelled)

**Suggested fix:**
```javascript
const affectedRoots = findAffectedRoots(changedFilePath, indexClient.getManifestEntries());
if (affectedRoots.length === 0) return; // Early exit if this event found no roots

for (const rootFile of affectedRoots) {
    const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);
    const rootKey = process.platform === 'win32'
        ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
        : resolvedRootFile;
    pendingRoots.set(rootKey, rootFile);
}

if (timer) cancel(timer);
timer = schedule(() =\> {
    timer = null;
    const roots = [...pendingRoots.values()];
    pendingRoots.clear();
    for (const rootFile of roots) {
        invalidateChangedFile(rootFile);
    }
}, delayMs);
```

---

### Issue 2: Missing Test Coverage
**File:** test/extension.test.js  
**Severity:** Medium  
**Problem:** Tests do not cover the critical edge case of untracked file events arriving while pending roots exist.

**Missing test cases:**
1. ❌ Untracked file event should not schedule a timer when no roots are pending
2. ❌ Untracked file event should not reset an existing timer when roots are pending
3. ❌ Test should verify timer count, not just final invalidation results

**Current tests only verify:**
- ✅ Same tracked file twice → coalesced into one invalidation
- ✅ Two different tracked files → batched into two invalidations

**Suggested additional test:**
```javascript
it('does not reschedule timer when untracked file events occur', () =\> {
    let timerScheduledCount = 0;
    let timerCancelledCount = 0;
    // ... setup ...
    
    batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: trackedFile });
    assert.strictEqual(timerScheduledCount, 1);
    
    batchedInvalidator({ fsPath: untrackedFile });
    assert.strictEqual(timerScheduledCount, 1); // Should NOT increment
    assert.strictEqual(timerCancelledCount, 0); // Should NOT cancel
});
```

---

### Test Results
- ✅ All 111 existing tests pass (no regressions)
- ✅ Activation correctly uses batched invalidator
- ✅ Watcher registration unchanged
- ✅ Platform-specific path normalization looks correct
- ❌ Bug allows timer to be reset by untracked file events
- ❌ Missing edge case test coverage

---

**Are current tests sufficient?** No. The tests verify correctness for the happy path but miss a critical edge case that leads to a timer reset bug.


---

<sub>⏱️ 793m 44s</sub>

````json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase5_background_refresh_queue.md\n+# vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 5：watcher 驱动后台自动重建）\n+\n+## 1. 当前进度定位\n+\n+到本轮开始时，Phase 5 已经具备：\n+\n+1. workspace watcher\n+2. manifest 驱动的受影响根定位\n+3. batched invalidation\n+\n+但 watcher 还只做到：\n+\n+> 文件变了 → 相关根缓存失效\n+\n+这意味着下一次真正访问这些根时，才会触发重建。虽然正确性已经有保障，但“**后台自动重建**”这一层还没落地。\n+\n+因此本轮继续推进三件事：\n+\n+1. 新增项目快照后台重建队列\n+2. 把 watcher 批处理失效和后台重建队列串起来\n+3. 给失败路径补上不中断后续根的保护\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. 本轮目标\n+\n+本轮把 watcher 失效链路从：\n+\n+- invalidate only\n+\n+推进到：\n+\n+- batched invalidate\n+- enqueue background refresh\n+\n+具体目标：\n+\n+1. 同一根重复入队时只保留一次\n+2. 多个根按顺序后台重建，避免并发放大\n+3. 某个根重建失败时，不影响后续根继续处理\n+\n+本轮**不做**：\n+\n+- worker 线程\n+- UI 自动刷新\n+- 后台重建进度提示\n+- 重建优先级策略\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. 设计选择\n+\n+## 3.1 先做“顺序后台重建队列”，不做并发重建\n+\n+当前最稳妥的选择不是并行重建多个工程，而是：\n+\n+1. watcher 批量收集受影响根\n+2. 根逐个进入后台重建队列\n+3. 队列顺序执行\n+\n+原因：\n+\n+1. 当前扩展还没有 worker 隔离\n+2. 顺序队列更容易避免并发放大和重复工作\n+3. 下一阶段切到 worker 时，可以在相同边界上把队列执行器替换掉\n+\n+## 3.2 失败不吞掉，但也不阻断后续根\n+\n+后台重建如果某个根失败，本轮选择：\n+\n+1. 记录错误（`console.error`）\n+2. 继续处理队列中的后续根\n+\n+这样做能同时满足：\n+\n+1. **不 silent fail**\n+2. **不因为一个根失败把整批重建卡死**\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. TDD 过程\n+\n+### 4.1 先写失败测试\n+\n+本轮先在 `test/extension.test.js` 新增两组约束：\n+\n+1. **顺序执行 + 根去重**\n+   - A 入队\n+   - A 再次入队\n+   - B 入队\n+   - 结果必须先重建 A，再重建 B，且 A 只重建一次\n+\n+2. **失败不中断**\n+   - A 重建报错\n+   - B 仍然必须继续执行\n+   - 错误通过 `onError(error, rootFile)` 交给调用方\n+\n+先执行聚焦测试：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\extension.test.js --grep \"createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue\"\n+```\n+\n+结果先红，失败原因正确：\n+\n+- `createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue` 不存在\n+\n+### 4.2 再写最小实现\n+\n+本轮新增：\n+\n+- `createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue(...)`\n+\n+它负责：\n+\n+1. 去重待处理根\n+2. 避免当前正在重建的根重复入队\n+3. 用单一调度入口顺序 drain 队列\n+4. 单根失败时调用 `onError(error, rootFile)`，然后继续\n+\n+同时把 `activate()` 改成：\n+\n+1. 先创建 `enqueueProjectSnapshotRefresh`\n+2. 再把 batched invalidator 的 `onInvalidatedRoots` 接到该队列\n+\n+从而实现：\n+\n+- 文件变化\n+- watcher batched invalidation\n+- 背景重建排队\n+\n+这一整条链路。\n+\n+### 4.3 验证转绿\n+\n+聚焦测试转绿后，再跑 watcher 相关整组测试和全量测试，确认没有回归。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. 实际代码改动\n+\n+## 5.1 `src/extension.js`\n+\n+本轮新增：\n+\n+- `createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue(...)`\n+\n+并调整：\n+\n+- `createBatchedManifestInvalidator(...)`\n+\n+支持在完成一批失效后，通过 `onInvalidatedRoots(roots)` 把这些根交给后台重建队列。\n+\n+`activate()` 中则新增：\n+\n+1. `enqueueProjectSnapshotRefresh`\n+2. watcher batch → refresh queue 的接线\n+\n+## 5.2 `test/extension.test.js`\n+\n+新增两组测试：\n+\n+1. 顺序执行 + 去重\n+2. 失败不中断后续根\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 6. 设计取舍\n+\n+## 6.1 当前仍然不自动刷新 UI\n+\n+本轮后台重建只是把缓存预热到新状态，不主动触发：\n+\n+- Include Tree refresh\n+- Keyword Index refresh\n+\n+这是刻意的：\n+\n+1. 先把数据层后台重建做稳\n+2. 再决定 UI 刷新策略\n+\n+## 6.2 让 batched invalidator 只负责“失效 + 通知”\n+\n+本轮没有把后台重建逻辑直接塞进 batched invalidator 内部，而是通过：\n+\n+- `onInvalidatedRoots(roots)`\n+\n+把后续动作解耦出来。\n+\n+这样后续如果要换成：\n+\n+- worker queue\n+- priority queue\n+- telemetry queue\n+\n+都更容易扩展。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 7. 验证结果\n+\n+执行：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\extension.test.js --grep \"createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue|createBatchedManifestInvalidator|createManifestDrivenInvalidator|workspace watcher\"\n+npm test\n+git --no-pager diff --check\n+```\n+\n+本轮提交前以上检查均通过。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 8. 对总体计划的推进意义\n+\n+做到这一步后，Phase 5 已经从“文件变更会让缓存失效”进一步推进到了：\n+\n+1. watcher 发现变化\n+2. manifest 反查受影响根\n+3. batched invalidation\n+4. **后台自动重建队列**\n+\n+这意味着下一步再切到：\n+\n+1. `worker_threads`\n+2. 更强的后台调度\n+3. 磁盘缓存热恢复\n+\n+时，扩展层事件入口已经更接近目标形态。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 9. 一句话结论\n+\n+**本轮把 watcher 失效链路继续推进成了“失效后顺序后台重建”的模式，并通过去重、顺序执行和失败不中断，保证了高频文件变化下的缓存恢复路径依然稳健。**\n*** End Patch\n"
````

</details>

### 💬 Copilot

I'll review the uncommitted changes on the dev branch, focusing on substantive issues like missed refreshes, duplicate refreshes, queue deadlocks, error-handling bugs, and test coverage.Now let me check the current state of the code to understand context better, particularly around the batched invalidator's previous behavior and the index client:Now let me look at the full queue implementation and verify error handling:Now let me check if there are existing tests for the batched invalidator to understand the testing pattern and check for regression risks:Let me check if there are existing tests that verify the `onInvalidatedRoots` callback is wired correctly:Let me run the tests to verify they pass:Now let me verify the existing tests still pass and check for any integration between these components:Let me check how the watcher is wired to the invalidation system:Good, the watcher is properly wired. Now let me look more carefully at a critical aspect of the queue implementation - the concurrency control:Excellent! I've confirmed a critical bug. Let me also verify that the existing tests don't catch this:Now let me check if there are any other issues with the batched invalidator integration:Perfect! I've found a critical bug. Let me write up my findings.

---

## FAIL

I found a **critical concurrency bug** that violates the sequential processing guarantee, plus missing test coverage.

---

## Issue 1: Critical - Concurrent drainQueue Invocations Break Sequential Processing

**File:** `src/extension.js:984`  
**Severity:** Critical

**Problem:**  
`flushScheduled` is set to `false` at the **START** of `drainQueue()` instead of at the **END**. This allows multiple `drainQueue()` invocations to run concurrently, breaking both the sequential processing and deduplication guarantees.

**Evidence:**  
I confirmed this with a runtime test showing `Max concurrent: 2` when enqueueing a second root while the first is processing. The processing log shows both roots start before either finishes:
```
{ "type": "start", "file": "root-a.k", "concurrent": 1 }
{ "type": "start", "file": "root-b.k", "concurrent": 2 }  // BUG: Started before A finished!
```

**Attack scenario:**
1. Root A is enqueued → `drainQueue_1` scheduled
2. `drainQueue_1` starts → sets `flushScheduled = false` → starts loading A
3. While A is loading, root B is enqueued
4. Since `flushScheduled = false`, a second `drainQueue_2` is scheduled
5. `drainQueue_2` starts → begins loading B in parallelllel with A
6. **Result:** Two roots processing concurrently, violating "sequential" guarantee

**Worse:** The shared `activeRootKey` variable gets overwritten by the second drain, so if root A is re-enqueued while both drains are active, it passes the `rootKey === activeRootKey` check and gets queued again even though it's being processed!

**Suggested fix:**
```javascript
async function drainQueue() {
    // DON'T set flushScheduled = false here
    while (pendingRoots.size \> 0) {
        const [rootKey, rootFile] = pendingRoots.entries().next().value;
        pendingRoots.delete(rootKey);
        activeRootKey = rootKey;
        try {
            await loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
        } catch (error) {
            onError(error, rootFile);
        } finally {
            activeRootKey = null;
        }
    }
    flushScheduled = false;  // Move to END
}
```

---

## Issue 2: Missing Test - Items Enqueued During Active Processing

**File:** `test/extension.test.js:973-1002`  
**Severity:** High

**Problem:**  
The existing test only enqueues items **before** starting the drain. It doesn't test the critical scenario where new items arrive **while** the queue is actively processing.

Current test sequence:
1. Enqueue A, A, B (all before drain starts)
2. Start drain
3. Verify sequential processing

Missing test sequence:
1. Enqueue A
2. Start drain
3. **While A is processing**, enqueue B
4. Verify B is NOT processed until A completes (sequential guarantee)

This missing test is why bug #1 wasn't caught.

---

## Issue 3: Missing Test - onInvalidatedRoots Callback

**File:** `test/extension.test.js` (no coverage exists)  
**Severity:** Medium

**Problem:**  
The new `onInvalidatedRoots` callback in `createBatchedManifestInvalidator` has no test coverage. Tests verify that `indexClient.invalidate` is called, but not that the callback receives the correct roots.

This is a key integration point between the batched invalidator and the refresh queue, yet it's untested.

**Suggested test:**
```javascript
it('calls onInvalidatedRoots with batched roots', () =\> {
    const invalidatedRootsList = [];
    const batchedInvalidator = createBatchedManifestInvalidator({
        indexClient: { /* ... */ },
        onInvalidatedRoots(roots) {
            invalidatedRootsList.push([...roots]);
        },
    });
    
    batchedInvalidator(fileA);
    batchedInvalidator(fileB);
    scheduled.forEach(callback =\> callback());
    
    assert.equal(invalidatedRootsList.length, 1);
    assert.deepEqual(invalidatedRootsList[0].sort(), [rootA, rootB].sort());
});
```

---

## Issue 4: Minor - Misleading Error Context in Outer Catch

**File:** `src/extension.js:1012`  
**Severity:** Low

**Problem:**
```javascript
drainQueue().catch(error =\> onError(error, resolvedRootFile));
```

If `drainQueue()` itself throws (not `loadProjectSnapshot`), the `resolvedRootFile` here is the **last file enqueued in this batch**, not the file that actually failed. This creates misleading error reports.

**Impact:** Low, since the inner try/catch should handle all normal errors from `loadProjectSnapshot`. The outer catch would only trigger on programming errors in the loop logic itself.

---

## Test Sufficiency Assessment

**INSUFFICIENT.** Critical gaps:

1. ❌ **No test for dynamic enqueueing** - Items enqueued during active processing (would catch bug #1)
2. ❌ **No test for onInvalidatedRoots callback** - Integration point between invalidator and queue is untested
3. ❌ **No concurrency verification** - Tests use mocked `schedule` and manually control timing, missing real async race conditions

**Needed tests:**
- Verify sequential processing when items arrive during processing
- Verify only one drainQueue runs at a time
- Verify onInvalidatedRoots receives correct batched roots
- Verify deduplication works for items enqueued during processing

---

## Existing Behavior Verification

- ✅ **Watcher invalidation:** Wiring is correct (lines 731-733)
- ✅ **Batching behavior:** Existing tests pass, logic unchanged
- ⚠️ **Cache/index behaviors:** Bug #1 could cause race conditions in cache access if multiple roots load concurrently
- ✅ **Extension activation:** Wiring looks correct

---

## Summary

**Verdict:** FAIL - Fix bug #1 before merging. The concurrent drainQueue bug violates the core design constraint (sequential processing) and could cause cache corruption or duplicate work. Add tests for dyn

+function createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue({

+    schedule = setImmediate,
+} = {}) {
+    if (typeof loadProjectSnapshot !== 'function') {
+        throw new TypeError('createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue requires a loadProjectSnapshot function');

+    if (typeof onError !== 'function') {
+        throw new TypeError('createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue requires an onError function');

+    if (typeof schedule !== 'function') {

+
+    const pendingRoots = new Map();
+    let activeRootKey = null;
+    let flushScheduled = false;
+

+        flushScheduled = false;
+        while (pendingRoots.size > 0) {

+            pendingRoots.delete(rootKey);
+            activeRootKey = rootKey;

+            } finally {
+                activeRootKey = null;
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    return function enqueueProjectSnapshotRefresh(rootFile) {
+        const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);
+        const rootKey = process.platform === 'win32'
+            ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
+            : resolvedRootFile;
+        if (rootKey === activeRootKey || pendingRoots.has(rootKey)) return;
+
+        pendingRoots.set(rootKey, resolvedRootFile);
+        if (flushScheduled) return;
+
+        flushScheduled = true;
+        schedule(() => {
+            drainQueue().catch(error => onError(error, resolvedRootFile));
+        });
+    };
+}

+describe('createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue', () => {
+    it('refreshes queued roots sequentially and deduplicates repeated roots', async () => {
+        const rootA = path.resolve('project', 'root-a.k');
+        const rootB = path.resolve('project', 'root-b.k');
+        const startedRoots = [];
+        const resolvers = [];
+        const scheduled = [];
+        const enqueueRefresh = createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue({
+            loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile) {
+                startedRoots.push(rootFile);
+                return new Promise(resolve => resolvers.push(resolve));
+            },
+            schedule(callback) {
+                scheduled.push(callback);
+                return scheduled.length;
+            },
+        });
+
+        enqueueRefresh(rootA);
+        enqueueRefresh(rootA);
+        enqueueRefresh(rootB);
+
+        assert.equal(scheduled.length, 1);
+
+        scheduled.shift()();
+        assert.deepEqual(startedRoots, [rootA]);
+
+        resolvers.shift()();
+        await new Promise(resolve => setImmediate(resolve));
+        assert.deepEqual(startedRoots, [rootA, rootB]);
+    });
+
+    it('continues refreshing later roots after a refresh failure', async () => {
+        const rootA = path.resolve('project', 'root-a.k');
+        const rootB = path.resolve('project', 'root-b.k');
+        const errors = [];
+        const startedRoots = [];
+        const scheduled = [];
+        const enqueueRefresh = createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue({
+            async loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile) {
+                startedRoots.push(rootFile);
+                if (rootFile === rootA) throw new Error('refresh failed');
+            },
+            onError(error, rootFile) {
+                errors.push({ message: error.message, rootFile });
+            },
+            schedule(callback) {
+                scheduled.push(callback);
+                return scheduled.length;
+            },
+        });
+
+        enqueueRefresh(rootA);
+        enqueueRefresh(rootB);
+        scheduled.shift()();
+        await new Promise(resolve => setImmediate(resolve));
+
+        assert.deepEqual(startedRoots, [rootA, rootB]);
+        assert.deepEqual(errors, [{ message: 'refresh failed', rootFile: rootA }]);
+    });
+});

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_execution_record_phase5_background_refresh_queue.md.**

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_execution_record_phase5_background_refresh_queue.md

<details>
<summary>236 lines</summary>

````
1. # vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 5：watcher 驱动后台自动重建）
2. 
3. ## 1. 当前进度定位
4. 
5. 到本轮开始时，Phase 5 已经具备：
6. 
7. 1. workspace watcher
8. 2. manifest 驱动的受影响根定位
9. 3. batched invalidation
10. 
11. 但 watcher 还只做到：
12. 
13. > 文件变了 → 相关根缓存失效
14. 
15. 这意味着下一次真正访问这些根时，才会触发重建。虽然正确性已经有保障，但“**后台自动重建**”这一层还没落地。
16. 
17. 因此本轮继续推进三件事：
18. 
19. 1. 新增项目快照后台重建队列
20. 2. 把 watcher 批处理失效和后台重建队列串起来
21. 3. 给失败路径补上不中断后续根的保护
22. 
23. ---
24. 
25. ## 2. 本轮目标
26. 
27. 本轮把 watcher 失效链路从：
28. 
29. - invalidate only
30. 
31. 推进到：
32. 
33. - batched invalidate
34. - enqueue background refresh
35. 
36. 具体目标：
37. 
38. 1. 同一根重复入队时只保留一次
39. 2. 多个根按顺序后台重建，避免并发放大
40. 3. 某个根重建失败时，不影响后续根继续处理
41. 
42. 本轮**不做**：
43. 
44. - worker 线程
45. - UI 自动刷新
46. - 后台重建进度提示
47. - 重建优先级策略
48. 
49. ---
50. 
51. ## 3. 设计选择
52. 
53. ## 3.1 先做“顺序后台重建队列”，不做并发重建
54. 
55. 当前最稳妥的选择不是并行重建多个工程，而是：
56. 
57. 1. watcher 批量收集受影响根
58. 2. 根逐个进入后台重建队列
59. 3. 队列顺序执行
60. 
61. 原因：
62. 
63. 1. 当前扩展还没有 worker 隔离
64. 2. 顺序队列更容易避免并发放大和重复工作
65. 3. 下一阶段切到 worker 时，可以在相同边界上把队列执行器替换掉
66. 
67. ## 3.2 失败不吞掉，但也不阻断后续根
68. 
69. 后台重建如果某个根失败，本轮选择：
70. 
71. 1. 记录错误（`console.error`）
72. 2. 继续处理队列中的后续根
73. 
74. 这样做能同时满足：
75. 
76. 1. **不 silent fail**
77. 2. **不因为一个根失败把整批重建卡死**
78. 
79. ---
80. 
81. ## 4. TDD 过程
82. 
83. ### 4.1 先写失败测试
84. 
85. 本轮先在 `test/extension.test.js` 新增两组约束：
86. 
87. 1. **顺序执行 + 根去重**
88.    - A 入队
89.    - A 再次入队
90.    - B 入队
91.    - 结果必须先重建 A，再重建 B，且 A 只重建一次
92. 
93. 2. **失败不中断**
94.    - A 重建报错
95.    - B 仍然必须继续执行
96.    - 错误通过 `onError(error, rootFile)` 交给调用方
97. 
98. 先执行聚焦测试：
99. 
100. ```powershell
101. npx mocha test\extension.test.js --grep "createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue"
102. ```
103. 
104. 结果先红，失败原因正确：
105. 
106. - `createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue` 不存在
107. 
108. ### 4.2 再写最小实现
109. 
110. 本轮新增：
111. 
112. - `createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue(...)`
113. 
114. 它负责：
115. 
116. 1. 去重待处理根
117. 2. 避免当前正在重建的根重复入队
118. 3. 用单一调度入口顺序 drain 队列
119. 4. 单根失败时调用 `onError(error, rootFile)`，然后继续
120. 
121. 同时把 `activate()` 改成：
122. 
123. 1. 先创建 `enqueueProjectSnapshotRefresh`
124. 2. 再把 batched invalidator 的 `onInvalidatedRoots` 接到该队列
125. 
126. 从而实现：
127. 
128. - 文件变化
129. - watcher batched invalidation
130. - 背景重建排队
131. 
132. 这一整条链路。
133. 
134. ### 4.3 验证转绿
135. 
136. 聚焦测试转绿后，再跑 watcher 相关整组测试和全量测试，确认没有回归。
137. 
138. ---
139. 
140. ## 5. 实际代码改动
141. 
142. ## 5.1 `src/extension.js`
143. 
144. 本轮新增：
145. 
146. - `createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue(...)`
147. 
148. 并调整：
149. 
150. - `createBatchedManifestInvalidator(...)`
151. 
152. 支持在完成一批失效后，通过 `onInvalidatedRoots(roots)` 把这些根交给后台重建队列。
153. 
154. `activate()` 中则新增：
155. 
156. 1. `enqueueProjectSnapshotRefresh`
157. 2. watcher batch → refresh queue 的接线
158. 
159. ## 5.2 `test/extension.test.js`
160. 
161. 新增两组测试：
162. 
163. 1. 顺序执行 + 去重
164. 2. 失败不中断后续根
165. 
166. ---
167. 
168. ## 6. 设计取舍
169. 
170. ## 6.1 当前仍然不自动刷新 UI
171. 
172. 本轮后台重建只是把缓存预热到新状态，不主动触发：
173. 
174. - Include Tree refresh
175. - Keyword Index refresh
176. 
177. 这是刻意的：
178. 
179. 1. 先把数据层后台重建做稳
180. 2. 再决定 UI 刷新策略
181. 
182. ## 6.2 让 batched invalidator 只负责“失效 + 通知”
183. 
184. 本轮没有把后台重建逻辑直接塞进 batched invalidator 内部，而是通过：
185. 
186. - `onInvalidatedRoots(roots)`
187. 
188. 把后续动作解耦出来。
189. 
190. 这样后续如果要换成：
191. 
192. - worker queue
193. - priority queue
194. - telemetry queue
195. 
196. 都更容易扩展。
197. 
198. ---
199. 
200. ## 7. 验证结果
201. 
202. 执行：
203. 
204. ```powershell
205. npx mocha test\extension.test.js --grep "createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue|createBatchedManifestInvalidator|createManifestDrivenInvalidator|workspace watcher"
206. npm test
207. git --no-pager diff --check
208. ```
209. 
210. 本轮提交前以上检查均通过。
211. 
212. ---
213. 
214. ## 8. 对总体计划的推进意义
215. 
216. 做到这一步后，Phase 5 已经从“文件变更会让缓存失效”进一步推进到了：
217. 
218. 1. watcher 发现变化
219. 2. manifest 反查受影响根
220. 3. batched invalidation
221. 4. **后台自动重建队列**
222. 
223. 这意味着下一步再切到：
224. 
225. 1. `worker_threads`
226. 2. 更强的后台调度
227. 3. 磁盘缓存热恢复
228. 
229. 时，扩展层事件入口已经更接近目标形态。
230. 
231. ---
232. 
233. ## 9. 一句话结论
234. 
235. **本轮把 watcher 失效链路继续推进成了“失效后顺序后台重建”的模式，并通过去重、顺序执行和失败不中断，保证了高频文件变化下的缓存恢复路径依然稳健。**
236.
````

</details>

```
965. function createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue({
966.     loadProjectSnapshot,
967.     onError = () => {},
968.     schedule = setImmediate,
969. } = {}) {
970.     if (typeof loadProjectSnapshot !== 'function') {
971.         throw new TypeError('createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue requires a loadProjectSnapshot function');
972.     }
973.     if (typeof onError !== 'function') {
974.         throw new TypeError('createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue requires an onError function');
975.     }
976.     if (typeof schedule !== 'function') {
977.         throw new TypeError('createProjectSnapshotRefreshQueue requires a schedule function');
978.     }
979. 
980.     const pendingRoots = new Map();
981.     let activeRootKey = null;
982.     let flushScheduled = false;
983. 
984.     async function drainQueue() {
985.         flushScheduled = false;
986.         while (pendingRoots.size > 0) {
987.             const [rootKey, rootFile] = pendingRoots.entries().next().value;
988.             pendingRoots.delete(rootKey);
989.             activeRootKey = rootKey;
990.             try {
991.                 await loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
992.             } catch (error) {
993.                 onError(error, rootFile);
994.             } finally {
995.                 activeRootKey = null;
996.             }
997.         }
998.     }
999. 
1000.     return function enqueueProjectSnapshotRefresh(rootFile) {
1001.         const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);
1002.         const rootKey = process.platform === 'win32'
1003.             ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
1004.             : resolvedRootFile;
1005.         if (rootKey === activeRootKey || pendingRoots.has(rootKey)) return;
1006. 
1007.         pendingRoots.set(rootKey, resolvedRootFile);
1008.         if (flushScheduled) return;
1009. 
1010.         flushScheduled = true;
1011.         schedule(() => {
1012.             drainQueue().catch(error => onError(error, resolvedRootFile));
1013.         });
1014.     };
1015. }
```

> lsdyna@2.0.7 test
