```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. const vscode = require('vscode');
6. 
7. const keywordScanner = require('../../core/parser/keywordScanner');
8. 
9. class KeywordItem extends vscode.TreeItem {
10.     constructor(keyword) {
11.         super(keyword, vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed);
12.         this.children = [];
13.         this.iconPath = new vscode.ThemeIcon('symbol-keyword');
14.     }
15. }
16. 
17. class KeywordUsageItem extends vscode.TreeItem {
18.     constructor(filePath, lineIndex, rootDir) {
19.         const rel = path.relative(rootDir, filePath);
20.         super(`${rel}  :${lineIndex + 1}`, vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.None);
21.         this.iconPath = new vscode.ThemeIcon('file');
22.         this.tooltip = `${filePath}:${lineIndex + 1}`;
23.         this.command = {
24.             command: 'extension.goToKeywordUsage',
25.             title: 'Go to keyword',
26.             arguments: [filePath, lineIndex],
27.         };
28.     }
29. }
30. 
31. class LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider {
32.     constructor({ collectIncludeFiles, loadProjectSnapshot, shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan } = {}) {
33.         this.collectIncludeFiles = collectIncludeFiles;
34.         this.loadProjectSnapshot = loadProjectSnapshot;
35.         this.shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan = shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan;
36.         this._onDidChangeTreeData = new vscode.EventEmitter();
37.         this.onDidChangeTreeData = this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
38.         this.roots = [];
39.         this._mode = 'local'; // 'local' | 'recursive'
40.     }
41. 
42.     _setMode(mode) {
43.         this._mode = mode;
44.         vscode.commands.executeCommand('setContext', 'lsdyna.keywordIndexMode', mode);
45.     }
46. 
47.     _buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir) {
48.         return [...keywordMap.entries()]
49.             .sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b))
50.             .map(([keyword, usages]) => {
51.                 const item = new KeywordItem(keyword);
52.                 item.children = usages.map(({ filePath, lineIndex }) =>
53.                     new KeywordUsageItem(filePath, lineIndex, rootDir)
54.                 );
55.                 return item;
56.             });
57.     }
58. 
59.     async _buildRootsAsync(filePaths, rootDir) {
60.         const keywordMap = new Map();
61.         for (const filePath of filePaths) {
62.             if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) continue;
63.             const keywords = await keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile(filePath);
64.             for (const { keyword, lineIndex } of keywords) {
65.                 if (!keywordMap.has(keyword)) keywordMap.set(keyword, []);
66.                 keywordMap.get(keyword).push({ filePath, lineIndex });
67.             }
68.         }
69.         return this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir);
70.     }
71. 
72.     _buildRootsFromSnapshot(snapshot, rootDir) {
73.         return this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(snapshot.keywordMap, rootDir);
74.     }
75. 
76.     refreshFromDocument(document) {
77.         if (this._mode !== 'local') return;
78.         if (!document || document.languageId !== 'lsdyna') return;
79.         if (this.shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) {
80.             this.roots = [];
81.             this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
82.             return;
83.         }
84.         const filePath = document.uri.fsPath;
85.         const rootDir = path.dirname(filePath);
86.         const keywordMap = new Map();
87.         for (const { keyword, lineIndex } of keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromLineReader(
88.             document.lineCount,
89.             i => document.lineAt(i).text,
90.             filePath
91.         )) {
92.             if (!keywordMap.has(keyword)) keywordMap.set(keyword, []);
93.             keywordMap.get(keyword).push({ filePath, lineIndex });
94.         }
95.         this.roots = this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir);
96.         this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
97.     }
98. 
99.     async scan() {
100.         const editor = vscode.window.activeTextEditor;
101.         if (!editor || editor.document.languageId !== 'lsdyna') {
102.             vscode.window.showWarningMessage('Open an LS-DYNA file first.');
103.             return;
104.         }
105.         const rootFile = editor.document.uri.fsPath;
106.         const rootDir = path.dirname(rootFile);
107.         await vscode.window.withProgress(
108.             { location: vscode.ProgressLocation.Notification, title: 'Scanning keywords…', cancellable: false },
109.             async (progress) => {
110.                 if (this.loadProjectSnapshot) {
111.                     const snapshot = await this.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
112.                     this.roots = this._buildRootsFromSnapshot(snapshot, rootDir);
113.                 } else {
114.                     const files = await this.collectIncludeFiles(rootFile, (count) => {
115.                         progress.report({ message: `${count} file${count === 1 ? '' : 's'} found` });
116.                     });
117.                     this.roots = await this._buildRootsAsync(files, rootDir);
118.                 }
119.                 this._setMode('recursive');
120.                 this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
121.             }
122.         );
123.     }
124. 
125.     setLocal() {
126.         this._setMode('local');
127.         if (vscode.window.activeTextEditor) {
128.             this.refreshFromDocument(vscode.window.activeTextEditor.document);
129.         } else {
130.             this.roots = [];
131.             this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
132.         }
133.     }
134. 
135.     getTreeItem(element) { return element; }
136. 
137.     getChildren(element) {
138.         if (element) return element.children;
139.         return this.roots;
140.     }
141. }
142. 
143. module.exports = {
144.     LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider,
145. };
146.
```
```

124.                     });
```

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const assert = require('assert');
4. const fs = require('fs');
5. const os = require('os');
6. const path = require('path');
7. 
8. const { buildProjectIndex, createProjectIndexer } = require('../../../src/core/project/projectIndexer');
9. 
10. describe('projectIndexer', () => {
11.     it('recursively aggregates included files and keyword usages into one project snapshot', async () => {
12.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
13.         const submodelsDir = path.join(tempRoot, 'submodels');
14.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
15.         const aFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'a.key');
16.         const bFile = path.join(submodelsDir, 'b.key');
17. 
18.         fs.mkdirSync(submodelsDir);
19.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE_PATH_RELATIVE\nsubmodels\n*INCLUDE\na.key\nb.key\n', 'utf8');
20.         fs.writeFileSync(aFile, '*PART\npart line\n', 'utf8');
21.         fs.writeFileSync(bFile, '*MAT_ELASTIC\nmat line\n', 'utf8');
22. 
23.         try {
24.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
25. 
26.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.files.sort(), [rootFile, aFile, bFile].sort());
27.             assert.deepEqual(
28.                 snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath),
29.                 [aFile]
30.             );
31.             assert.deepEqual(
32.                 snapshot.keywordMap.get('MAT_ELASTIC').map(entry => entry.filePath),
33.                 [bFile]
34.             );
35.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.missingFiles, []);
36.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.cycles, []);
37.         } finally {
38.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
39.         }
40.     });
41. 
42.     it('records missing includes without aborting the rest of the project scan', async () => {
43.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
44.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
45. 
46.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nmissing.key\n*PART\n', 'utf8');
47. 
48.         try {
49.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
50. 
51.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.files, [rootFile]);
52.             assert.equal(snapshot.missingFiles.length, 1);
53.             assert.equal(snapshot.missingFiles[0].fromFile, rootFile);
54.             assert.equal(snapshot.missingFiles[0].fileName, 'missing.key');
55.             assert.deepEqual(
56.                 snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath),
57.                 [rootFile]
58.             );
59.         } finally {
60.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
61.         }
62.     });
63. 
64.     it('builds a project graph with forward and reverse include edges', async () => {
65.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
66.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
67.         const childFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'child.key');
68. 
69.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nchild.key\n', 'utf8');
70.         fs.writeFileSync(childFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
71. 
72.         try {
73.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
74. 
75.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.getChildren(rootFile), [childFile]);
76.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.getParents(childFile), [rootFile]);
77.         } finally {
78.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
79.         }
80.     });
81. 
82.     it('materializes a nested include tree from the project graph in include order', async () => {
83.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
84.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
85.         const aFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'a.key');
86.         const bFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'b.key');

90.         fs.writeFileSync(aFile, '*INCLUDE\nc.key\n', 'utf8');
91.         fs.writeFileSync(bFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
92.         fs.writeFileSync(cFile, '*MAT_ELASTIC\n', 'utf8');
93. 
94.         try {
95.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
96. 
97.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
98.                 filePath: rootFile,
99.                 children: [
100.                     {
101.                         filePath: aFile,
102.                         children: [
103.                             {
104.                                 filePath: cFile,
105.                                 children: [],
106.                             },

120.     it('preserves missing includes as tree nodes in include order', async () => {
121.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
122.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');

126.

130. 
131.         try {

133. 
134.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
135.                 filePath: rootFile,

138.                         filePath: aFile,
139.                         children: [],
140.                     },

143.                         fileName: 'missing.key',
144.                         missing: true,
145.                         children: [],
146.                     },
147.                     {
148.                         filePath: bFile,
149.                         children: [],
150.                     },
151.                 ],
152.             });
153.         } finally {
154.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
155.         }
156.     });
157. 
158.     it('preserves duplicate missing includes as separate tree nodes in stable order', async () => {
159.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
160.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
161.         const childFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'child.key');
162.         const missingFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'missing.key');
163. 
164.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nmissing.key\nmissing.key\nchild.key\n', 'utf8');
165.         fs.writeFileSync(childFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
166. 
167.         try {
168.             const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
169. 
170.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
171.                 filePath: rootFile,
172.                 children: [
173.                     {
174.                         filePath: missingFile,
175.                         fileName: 'missing.key',
176.                         missing: true,
177.                         children: [],
178.                     },
179.                     {
180.                         filePath: missingFile,
181.                         fileName: 'missing.key',
182.                         missing: true,
183.                         children: [],
184.                     },
185.                     {
186.                         filePath: childFile,
187.                         children: [],
188.                     },
189.                 ],
190.             });
191.         } finally {
192.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
193.         }
194.     });
195. 
196.     it('reuses unchanged file scans when rebuilding after a child file change', async () => {
197.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-project-index-'));
198.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
199.         const aFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'a.key');
200.         const bFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'b.key');
201.         const projectDir = path.dirname(rootFile);
202.         const scanCounts = new Map();
203.         const includeFixtures = new Map([
204.             [rootFile, {
205.                 includeEntries: [{ fileName: 'a.key' }, { fileName: 'b.key' }],
206.                 searchPaths: [projectDir],
207.             }],
208.             [aFile, {
209.                 includeEntries: [],
210.                 searchPaths: [projectDir],
211.             }],
212.             [bFile, {
213.                 includeEntries: [],
214.                 searchPaths: [projectDir],
215.             }],
216.         ]);
217.         const keywordFixtures = new Map([
218.             [rootFile, []],
219.             [aFile, [{ keyword: 'PART', filePath: aFile, line: 0 }]],
220.             [bFile, [{ keyword: 'MAT_ELASTIC', filePath: bFile, line: 0 }]],
221.         ]);
222.         const signatures = new Map([
223.             [rootFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }],
224.             [aFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 200 }],
225.             [bFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 300 }],
226.         ]);
227.         const indexer = createProjectIndexer({
228.             getFileSignature: async (filePath) => signatures.get(filePath),
229.             collectKeywordsFromFile: async (filePath) => {
230.                 scanCounts.set(`keywords:${filePath}`, (scanCounts.get(`keywords:${filePath}`) || 0) + 1);
231.                 return keywordFixtures.get(filePath);
232.             },
233.             collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile: async (filePath) => {
234.                 scanCounts.set(`includes:${filePath}`, (scanCounts.get(`includes:${filePath}`) || 0) + 1);
235.                 return includeFixtures.get(filePath);
236.             },
237.         });
238. 
239.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*KEYWORD\n', 'utf8');
240.         fs.writeFileSync(aFile, '*KEYWORD\n', 'utf8');
241.         fs.writeFileSync(bFile, '*KEYWORD\n', 'utf8');
242. 
243.         try {
244.             const initialSnapshot = await indexer.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
245.             assert.deepEqual(initialSnapshot.stats, {
246.                 scannedFileCount: 3,
247.                 reusedFileCount: 0,
248.             });
249. 
250.             signatures.set(bFile, { mtimeMs: 20, size: 300 });
251.             keywordFixtures.set(bFile, [{ keyword: 'SECTION', filePath: bFile, line: 0 }]);
252. 
253.             const updatedSnapshot = await indexer.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
254.             assert.deepEqual(updatedSnapshot.stats, {
255.                 scannedFileCount: 1,
256.                 reusedFileCount: 2,
257.             });
258.             assert.deepEqual(updatedSnapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath), [aFile]);
259.             assert.deepEqual(updatedSnapshot.keywordMap.get('SECTION').map(entry => entry.filePath), [bFile]);
260.             assert.equal(updatedSnapshot.keywordMap.has('MAT_ELASTIC'), false);
```

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\src\\worker\\workerPool.js\n+'use strict';\n+\n+const { Worker } = require('worker_threads');\n+\n+const { ProjectGraph } = require('../core/project/projectGraph');\n+\n+function createWorkerPool({\n+    workerPath,\n+    workerFactory = (nextWorkerPath) => new Worker(nextWorkerPath),\n+} = {}) {\n+    if (typeof workerPath !== 'string' || workerPath.trim() === '') {\n+        throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerPath');\n+    }\n+    if (typeof workerFactory !== 'function') {\n+        throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerFactory function');\n+    }\n+\n+    const worker = workerFactory(workerPath);\n+    const pendingRequests = new Map();\n+    let nextRequestId = 1;\n+    let disposed = false;\n+\n+    function hydrateProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {\n+        const graph = ProjectGraph.fromJSON(snapshot.graph);\n+        return {\n+            ...snapshot,\n+            graph,\n+            keywordMap: new Map(snapshot.keywordMap || []),\n+            missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,\n+            cycles: graph.cycles,\n+        };\n+    }\n+\n+    function rejectPendingRequests(error) {\n+        for (const pendingRequest of pendingRequests.values()) {\n+            pendingRequest.reject(error);\n+        }\n+        pendingRequests.clear();\n+    }\n+\n+    worker.on('message', (message) => {\n+        const pendingRequest = pendingRequests.get(message.requestId);\n+        if (!pendingRequest) return;\n+        pendingRequests.delete(message.requestId);\n+\n+        if (message.type === 'error') {\n+            const error = new Error(message.error.message);\n+            error.name = message.error.name;\n+            error.stack = message.error.stack;\n+            pendingRequest.reject(error);\n+            return;\n+        }\n+\n+        pendingRequest.resolve(hydrateProjectSnapshot(message.snapshot));\n+    });\n+    worker.on('error', (error) => {\n+        disposed = true;\n+        rejectPendingRequests(error);\n+    });\n+    worker.on('exit', (code) => {\n+        if (disposed && pendingRequests.size === 0) return;\n+\n+        disposed = true;\n+        rejectPendingRequests(new Error(`scan worker exited with code ${code}`));\n+    });\n+\n+    return {\n+        buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {\n+            if (disposed) {\n+                return Promise.reject(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));\n+            }\n+\n+            const requestId = nextRequestId++;\n+            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n+                pendingRequests.set(requestId, { reject, resolve });\n+                worker.postMessage({\n+                    requestId,\n+                    rootFile,\n+                    type: 'buildProjectIndex',\n+                });\n+            });\n+        },\n+        async dispose() {\n+            if (disposed) return;\n+            disposed = true;\n+            rejectPendingRequests(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));\n+            await worker.terminate();\n+        },\n+    };\n+}\n+\n+module.exports = {\n+    createWorkerPool,\n+};\n*** End Patch\n"
```

````json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase6_worker_boundary.md\n+# vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 6：worker_threads 执行边界）\n+\n+## 1. 当前进度定位\n+\n+到本轮开始时，扩展已经完成：\n+\n+1. 项目快照缓存\n+2. manifest\n+3. watcher 失效\n+4. watcher batching\n+5. 后台自动重建队列\n+\n+但有一个核心问题仍然存在：\n+\n+> **`buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 依然在 Extension Host 进程里执行。**\n+\n+即使现在已经有缓存和后台队列，真正重的工程级扫描一旦发生，依然会占用扩展宿主线程。\n+\n+因此本轮的目标非常明确：\n+\n+**把项目快照构建正式切到 `worker_threads` 执行边界。**\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. 本轮目标\n+\n+本轮一次性完成四件事：\n+\n+1. 让 `ProjectGraph` 可序列化 / 反序列化\n+2. 新增 worker 入口 `src/worker/scanWorker.js`\n+3. 新增主线程客户端 `src/worker/workerPool.js`\n+4. 在 `src/extension.js` 里引入 lazy `createProjectIndexLoader(...)`，让激活时不立刻起 worker，而是在第一次真正需要项目快照时才创建 worker pool\n+\n+本轮**不做**：\n+\n+- 多 worker 并行池\n+- worker 优先级调度\n+- worker 共享缓存\n+- worker telemetry\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. 设计选择\n+\n+## 3.1 先做单 worker 边界，不做并行 worker pool\n+\n+本轮没有急着上多 worker 并发，而是只做一个 worker 边界。\n+\n+原因很直接：\n+\n+1. 当前最重要的是把**重构建从 Extension Host 拿出去**\n+2. 单 worker 已经能验证：\n+   - 协议形状\n+   - 生命周期\n+   - 错误传播\n+   - snapshot 序列化/反序列化\n+3. 多 worker 调度可以在这个边界稳定后再演进\n+\n+## 3.2 不直接跨线程传 `ProjectGraph` 实例，而是显式序列化\n+\n+`ProjectGraph` 原本是带方法的类实例，`keywordMap` 也是 `Map`。如果不做显式 DTO 处理，worker round-trip 后会丢失：\n+\n+- `graph.toTree()`\n+- `keywordMap.entries()`\n+\n+因此本轮明确做了：\n+\n+1. `ProjectGraph.toJSON()`\n+2. `ProjectGraph.fromJSON()`\n+3. worker 侧把 `keywordMap` 显式转成数组对\n+4. 主线程侧把 `keywordMap` 再 hydrate 成 `Map`\n+\n+并在主线程重建后重新绑定：\n+\n+- `snapshot.missingFiles = snapshot.graph.missingFiles`\n+- `snapshot.cycles = snapshot.graph.cycles`\n+\n+保持和原主线程版本一致的 snapshot 形状。\n+\n+## 3.3 worker pool 必须 reject pending，而不是让请求悬挂\n+\n+本轮特别注意了 worker 生命周期的企业级稳健性：\n+\n+1. `worker.on('error')` 时 reject 所有 pending request\n+2. `worker.on('exit')` 时 reject 所有 pending request\n+3. `dispose()` 时先 reject pending，再 terminate worker\n+\n+这样不会出现“worker 已死，但调用方 promise 永远悬挂”的隐患。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. TDD 过程\n+\n+### 4.1 先写失败测试\n+\n+本轮先新增三类测试：\n+\n+#### A. `test/worker/workerPool.test.js`\n+\n+1. **真实 worker round-trip**\n+   - 临时工程文件\n+   - worker 返回 snapshot\n+   - 主线程侧要求：\n+     - `snapshot.keywordMap instanceof Map`\n+     - `snapshot.graph.toTree()` 仍可用\n+     - `snapshot.missingFiles` / `snapshot.cycles` 继续和 `graph` 内部数组同引用\n+\n+2. **worker error 必须 reject pending**\n+   - 用 fake worker 注入\n+   - 挂起一个请求\n+   - 主动触发 `error`\n+   - 断言 promise 必须 reject，而不是悬挂\n+\n+#### B. `test/extension.test.js`\n+\n+1. `createProjectIndexLoader(...)` 必须：\n+   - 首次调用前不创建 worker pool\n+   - 第一次真正 `buildProjectIndex()` 时才创建\n+   - `dispose()` 能把 worker pool 收掉\n+\n+先执行聚焦测试：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\worker\\workerPool.test.js test\\extension.test.js --grep \"createWorkerPool|createProjectIndexLoader\"\n+```\n+\n+结果先红，失败原因符合预期：\n+\n+- `workerPool` 模块不存在\n+- `createProjectIndexLoader` 不存在\n+\n+### 4.2 再写最小实现\n+\n+本轮依次补上：\n+\n+1. `ProjectGraph.toJSON()/fromJSON()`\n+2. `scanWorker.js`\n+3. `workerPool.js`\n+4. `createProjectIndexLoader(...)`\n+5. `activate()` 中改为通过 lazy loader 把项目快照请求路由给 worker pool\n+\n+### 4.3 验证转绿\n+\n+聚焦测试转绿后，再跑全量测试，确保：\n+\n+1. 扩展激活路径未退化\n+2. worker 边界未破坏现有 Include Tree / Keyword Index / 缓存链路\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. 实际代码改动\n+\n+## 5.1 `src/core/project/projectGraph.js`\n+\n+新增：\n+\n+- `toJSON()`\n+- `fromJSON()`\n+\n+用于跨 worker 传输图状态，同时保留类行为。\n+\n+## 5.2 `src/worker/scanWorker.js`\n+\n+新增 worker 入口，负责：\n+\n+1. 接收 `buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 请求\n+2. 执行项目快照构建\n+3. 返回序列化后的 snapshot\n+4. 返回序列化错误\n+\n+## 5.3 `src/worker/workerPool.js`\n+\n+新增主线程 worker 客户端，负责：\n+\n+1. 创建 worker\n+2. requestId 协议\n+3. main-thread hydration\n+4. error / exit / dispose 时 reject pending\n+\n+## 5.4 `src/extension.js`\n+\n+新增：\n+\n+- `createProjectIndexLoader(...)`\n+\n+并让 `indexClient` 改为依赖：\n+\n+- `projectIndexLoader.buildProjectIndex`\n+\n+同时把 loader 本身放进 `context.subscriptions`，保证扩展释放时 worker 能一起回收。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 6. 真实工程样本验证\n+\n+本轮继续使用用户提供的真实工程：\n+\n+- 根文件：`D:\\temp\\LSDYNA\\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\\combine.key`\n+\n+通过 `workerPool.buildProjectIndex(rootFile)` 实测得到：\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"fileCount\": 4,\n+  \"keywordGroupCount\": 88,\n+  \"rootChildren\": 3,\n+  \"reusedFileCount\": 0\n+}\n+```\n+\n+说明 worker 边界下：\n+\n+1. snapshot 能正常返回\n+2. `keywordMap` hydration 正常\n+3. `graph.toTree()` 可正常工作\n+4. 当前真实工程结构能被完整 round-trip\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 7. 验证结果\n+\n+执行：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\worker\\workerPool.test.js test\\extension.test.js --grep \"createWorkerPool|createProjectIndexLoader\"\n+npm test\n+git --no-pager diff --check\n+```\n+\n+本轮提交前以上检查均通过。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 8. 对总体计划的推进意义\n+\n+做到这一步之后，整体架构终于从：\n+\n+- “主线程里做重工程扫描”\n+\n+推进到了：\n+\n+- “Extension Host 发起请求”\n+- “worker 线程执行项目快照构建”\n+- “主线程 hydration 并继续走现有 cache/provider 链路”\n+\n+这意味着剩下的主要工作已经进一步收敛为：\n+\n+1. worker 调度增强（如果需要）\n+2. Phase 4 磁盘缓存 / L2 治理\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 9. 一句话结论\n+\n+**本轮正式把项目快照构建切到了 `worker_threads` 执行边界，并通过显式 snapshot hydration 保持了 `ProjectGraph` / `keywordMap` / provider 行为兼容，完成了“重扫描脱离 Extension Host 主线程”的关键一步。**\n*** End Patch\n"
````

```
Agent is idle (waiting for messages). agent_id: worker-boundary-review, agent_type: superpowers:code-reviewer, status: idle, description: Review worker boundary, elapsed: 407s, total_turns: 1

[Turn 0]
I found **one meaningful issue**.

**What looks good**
- Lazy `createProjectIndexLoader()` avoids eager worker startup in `activate()`.
- ProjectGraph hydration across the worker boundary looks deliberate and tested.
- Tests cover both worker round-trip and lazy creation/disposal.

**Important**
- **Worker crash/exit does not appear to self-heal**
  - In `src/worker/workerPool.js:56-70`, worker `error`/`exit` disposes the pool / rejects pending work.
  - In `src/extension.js:1030-1047`, `createProjectIndexLoader()` caches that pool and only replaces it on explicit `dispose()`.
  - Result: if the worker dies once, later indexing requests can keep hitting a dead/disposed pool for the rest of the session instead of transparently recreating it.

**Why this matters**
- This is a real **reliability/lifecycle regression** at the new worker boundary: one transient worker failure can permanently break project indexing until extension reload.

**Assessment**
- **No critical issues**, but **not safe to proceed as-is** until worker-pool recovery/recreation after crash/exit is handled.
```

</details>

```
49. describe('findParameterDefinitions', () => {
50.     it('finds basic *PARAMETER definitions', () => {
51.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  tEnd  5.0\nI  count  10\n');
52.         const defs = findParameterDefinitions(doc);
53.         assert.equal(defs.size, 2);
54.         assert.ok(defs.has('TEND'));
55.         assert.equal(defs.get('TEND').value, '5.0');
56.         assert.equal(defs.get('COUNT').value, '10');
57.     });
58. 
59.     it('finds *PARAMETER_EXPRESSION definitions', () => {
60.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  tEnd  5.0\n*PARAMETER_EXPRESSION\nR  dtPlot  tEnd/100.0\n');
61.         const defs = findParameterDefinitions(doc);
62.         assert.ok(defs.has('DTPLOT'));
63.         assert.equal(defs.get('DTPLOT').value, 'tEnd/100.0');
64.     });
65. 
66.     it('is case-insensitive on key lookup', () => {
67.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  MyParam  42.0\n');
68.         const defs = findParameterDefinitions(doc);
69.         assert.ok(defs.has('MYPARAM'));
70.         assert.equal(defs.get('MYPARAM').name, 'MyParam');
71.     });
72. 
73.     it('skips comment lines inside *PARAMETER block', () => {
74.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\n$ a comment\nR  tEnd  5.0\n');
75.         const defs = findParameterDefinitions(doc);
76.         assert.equal(defs.size, 1);
77.     });
78. 
79.     it('stops collecting at next keyword', () => {
80.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  tEnd  5.0\n*CONTROL_TERMINATION\nR  notAParam  0\n');
81.         const defs = findParameterDefinitions(doc);
82.         assert.equal(defs.size, 1);
83.     });
84. 
85.     it('records correct line and column for definition', () => {
86.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR   tEnd   5.0\n');
87.         const defs = findParameterDefinitions(doc);
88.         const def = defs.get('TEND');
89.         assert.equal(def.lineIndex, 1);
90.         assert.equal(doc.lineAt(def.lineIndex).text.slice(def.startChar, def.startChar + def.length), 'tEnd');
91.     });
92. 
93.     it('parses the real fixture file', () => {
94.         const fs = require('fs');
95.         const text = fs.readFileSync(path.join(FIXTURE_DIR, 'mainboltaexpl.k'), 'utf8');
96.         const doc = fakeDoc(text, path.join(FIXTURE_DIR, 'mainboltaexpl.k'));
97.         const defs = findParameterDefinitions(doc);
98.         assert.ok(defs.has('TEND'));
99.         assert.ok(defs.has('DTPLOT'));
100.         assert.ok(defs.has('BLTFORCE'));
101.     });
102. });
103.

106. // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107. 
108. describe('findParameterReferences', () => {
109.     it('finds &name references', () => {
110.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  tEnd  5.0\n*CONTROL_TERMINATION\n     &tEnd\n');
111.         const refs = findParameterReferences(doc);
112.         const r = refs.filter(r => r.name === 'TEND');
113.         assert.equal(r.length, 1);
114.         assert.equal(r[0].lineIndex, 3);
115.     });
116. 
117.     it('finds multiple references to same parameter', () => {
118.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  t  5.0\n*KEYWORD\n&t  &t\n');
119.         const refs = findParameterReferences(doc).filter(r => r.name === 'T');
120.         assert.equal(refs.length, 2);
121.     });
122. 
123.     it('finds bare name references in *PARAMETER_EXPRESSION values', () => {
124.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  tEnd  5.0\n*PARAMETER_EXPRESSION\nR  dtPlot  tEnd/100.0\n');
125.         const refs = findParameterReferences(doc).filter(r => r.name === 'TEND');
126.         assert.equal(refs.length, 1);
127.         assert.equal(refs[0].lineIndex, 3);
128.     });
129. 
130.     it('does not treat expression definition name as a reference', () => {
131.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  tEnd  5.0\n*PARAMETER_EXPRESSION\nR  dtPlot  tEnd/100.0\n');
132.         const refs = findParameterReferences(doc).filter(r => r.name === 'DTPLOT');
133.         assert.equal(refs.length, 0);
134.     });
135. 
136.     it('skips comment lines', () => {
137.         const doc = fakeDoc('*PARAMETER\nR  tEnd  5.0\n$ &tEnd this is a comment\n');
138.         const refs = findParameterReferences(doc).filter(r => r.name === 'TEND');
139.         assert.equal(refs.length, 0);
140.     });
141. });

154.
```

```
1009.     return function enqueueProjectSnapshotRefresh(rootFile) {
1010.         const resolvedRootFile = path.resolve(rootFile);

1013.             : resolvedRootFile;
1014.         if (rootKey === activeRootKey || pendingRoots.has(rootKey)) return;
1015. 
1016.         pendingRoots.set(rootKey, resolvedRootFile);

1022.         });
1023.     };
```

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const { Worker } = require('worker_threads');
4. 
5. const { ProjectGraph } = require('../core/project/projectGraph');
6. 
7. function createWorkerPool({
8.     workerPath,
9.     workerFactory = (nextWorkerPath) => new Worker(nextWorkerPath),
10. } = {}) {
11.     if (typeof workerPath !== 'string' || workerPath.trim() === '') {
12.         throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerPath');
13.     }
14.     if (typeof workerFactory !== 'function') {
15.         throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerFactory function');
16.     }
17. 
18.     const worker = workerFactory(workerPath);
19.     const pendingRequests = new Map();
20.     let nextRequestId = 1;
21.     let disposed = false;
22. 
23.     function hydrateProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
24.         const graph = ProjectGraph.fromJSON(snapshot.graph);
25.         return {
26.             ...snapshot,
27.             graph,
28.             keywordMap: new Map(snapshot.keywordMap || []),
29.             missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
30.             cycles: graph.cycles,
31.         };
32.     }
33. 
34.     function rejectPendingRequests(error) {
35.         for (const pendingRequest of pendingRequests.values()) {
36.             pendingRequest.reject(error);
37.         }
38.         pendingRequests.clear();
39.     }
40. 
41.     worker.on('message', (message) => {
42.         const pendingRequest = pendingRequests.get(message.requestId);
43.         if (!pendingRequest) return;
44.         pendingRequests.delete(message.requestId);
45. 
46.         if (message.type === 'error') {
47.             const error = new Error(message.error.message);
48.             error.name = message.error.name;
49.             error.stack = message.error.stack;
50.             pendingRequest.reject(error);
51.             return;
52.         }
53. 
54.         pendingRequest.resolve(hydrateProjectSnapshot(message.snapshot));
55.     });
56.     worker.on('error', (error) => {
57.         disposed = true;
58.         rejectPendingRequests(error);
59.     });
60.     worker.on('exit', (code) => {
61.         if (disposed && pendingRequests.size === 0) return;
62. 
63.         disposed = true;
64.         rejectPendingRequests(new Error(`scan worker exited with code ${code}`));
65.     });
66. 
67.     return {
68.         buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
69.             if (disposed) {
70.                 return Promise.reject(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
71.             }
72. 
73.             const requestId = nextRequestId++;
74.             return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
75.                 pendingRequests.set(requestId, { reject, resolve });
76.                 worker.postMessage({
77.                     requestId,
78.                     rootFile,
79.                     type: 'buildProjectIndex',
80.                 });
81.             });
82.         },
83.         async dispose() {
84.             if (disposed) return;
85.             disposed = true;
86.             rejectPendingRequests(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
87.             await worker.terminate();
88.         },
89.     };
90. }
91. 
92. module.exports = {
93.     createWorkerPool,
94. };
95.
```

</details>

```
1118.             () => activeDocument,
1119.             (document) => refreshed.push(document.uri.fsPath),
1120.             500,
1121.             (callback) => {
1122.                 const timerId = nextTimerId++;
1123.                 scheduled.set(timerId, callback);
1124.                 return timerId;
1125.             },
1126.             (timerId) => scheduled.delete(timerId)
```

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const assert = require('assert');
4. const fs = require('fs');
5. const os = require('os');
6. const path = require('path');
7. 
8. describe('createWorkerPool', () => {
9.     it('builds project snapshots in a worker and hydrates graph and keywordMap', async () => {
10.         const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
11.         const workerPath = path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', 'src', 'worker', 'scanWorker.js');
12.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-worker-pool-'));
13.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
14.         const childFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'child.key');
15.         const pool = createWorkerPool({ workerPath });
16. 
17.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nchild.key\n', 'utf8');
18.         fs.writeFileSync(childFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
19. 
20.         try {
21.             const snapshot = await pool.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
22.             assert.ok(snapshot.keywordMap instanceof Map);
23.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath), [childFile]);
24.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
25.                 filePath: rootFile,
26.                 children: [
27.                     {
28.                         filePath: childFile,
29.                         children: [],
30.                     },
31.                 ],
32.             });
33.             assert.strictEqual(snapshot.missingFiles, snapshot.graph.missingFiles);
34.             assert.strictEqual(snapshot.cycles, snapshot.graph.cycles);
35.         } finally {
36.             await pool.dispose();
37.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
38.         }
39.     });
40. 
41.     it('rejects pending requests when the worker errors', async () => {
42.         const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
43.         const handlers = new Map();
44.         const fakeWorker = {
45.             on(eventName, handler) {
46.                 handlers.set(eventName, handler);
47.             },
48.             postMessage() {},
49.             terminate() {
50.                 return Promise.resolve(0);
51.             },
52.         };
53.         const pool = createWorkerPool({
54.             workerPath: 'fake-worker.js',
55.             workerFactory: () => fakeWorker,
56.         });
57. 
58.         const pendingRequest = pool.buildProjectIndex('project/main.k');
59.         handlers.get('error')(new Error('worker crashed'));
60. 
61.         await assert.rejects(pendingRequest, /worker crashed/);
62.         await pool.dispose();
63.     });
64. });
```

<sub>⏱️ 815m 50s</sub>

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase6_worker_boundary.md\n@@\n ## 3.3 worker pool 必须 reject pending，而不是让请求悬挂\n \n 本轮特别注意了 worker 生命周期的企业级稳健性：\n \n 1. `worker.on('error')` 时 reject 所有 pending request\n 2. `worker.on('exit')` 时 reject 所有 pending request\n 3. `dispose()` 时先 reject pending，再 terminate worker\n \n 这样不会出现“worker 已死，但调用方 promise 永远悬挂”的隐患。\n+\n+## 3.4 lazy loader 必须在 worker 崩溃后自恢复\n+\n+在实现完第一版后，review 又补抓到一个真实边界：\n+\n+1. `createProjectIndexLoader()` 会缓存 `workerPool`\n+2. 如果底层 worker 已经 crash / exit，对应 pool 会进入 disposed 状态\n+3. 如果 loader 不识别这一状态，后续请求会一直命中一个已经失效的 pool\n+\n+因此本轮又补了一层恢复逻辑：\n+\n+1. `workerPool.isDisposed()` 对外暴露生命周期状态\n+2. `createProjectIndexLoader()` 在取池时会先检查 disposed 状态\n+3. 如果一次构建请求因 fatal worker failure 失败，loader 会清空失效 pool，后续请求自动重建新 pool\n+\n+这样就避免了“worker 只要死一次，整个会话后续索引都永久损坏”的问题。\n@@\n #### B. `test/extension.test.js`\n \n 1. `createProjectIndexLoader(...)` 必须：\n    - 首次调用前不创建 worker pool\n    - 第一次真正 `buildProjectIndex()` 时才创建\n    - `dispose()` 能把 worker pool 收掉\n+2. `createProjectIndexLoader(...)` 还必须：\n+   - 在 fatal worker failure 后识别已失效 pool\n+   - 后续请求自动重建 pool，而不是把整个会话打坏\n@@\n ## 5.4 `src/extension.js`\n \n 新增：\n \n - `createProjectIndexLoader(...)`\n \n 并让 `indexClient` 改为依赖：\n \n - `projectIndexLoader.buildProjectIndex`\n \n 同时把 loader 本身放进 `context.subscriptions`，保证扩展释放时 worker 能一起回收。\n+\n+另外，loader 现在还负责：\n+\n+1. 识别已 disposed 的 worker pool\n+2. 在 fatal worker failure 后清空失效缓存\n+3. 让后续请求可以自动拉起新 pool\n*** End Patch\n"
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 889m 44s</sub>

### ✅ `read_agent`

**Superpowers:code-reviewer agent (Review worker boundary)**

<details>
<summary>17 lines</summary>

```
Agent is still running after waiting 180s. agent_id: worker-boundary-review, agent_type: superpowers:code-reviewer, status: running, description: Review worker boundary, elapsed: 4898s, total_turns: 1, current_intent: "Reviewing updated diff", tool_calls_completed: 3, (timed out waiting for completion) Consider telling the user you're waiting, then end your response with no further tool calls. A completion notification will arrive as a new turn; no need to poll or redo its work.

Completed turns:
[Turn 0]
I found **one meaningful issue**.

**What looks good**
- Lazy `createProjectIndexLoader()` avoids eager worker startup in `activate()`.
- ProjectGraph hydration across the worker boundary looks deliberate and tested.
- Tests cover both worker round-trip and lazy creation/disposal.

**Important**
- **Worker crash/exit does not appear to self-heal**
  - In `src/worker/workerPool.js:56-70`, worker `error`/`exit` disposes the pool / rejects pending work.
  - In `src/extension.js:1030-1047`, `createProjectIndexLoader()` caches that pool and only replaces it on explicit `dispose()`.
  - Result: if the worker dies once, later indexing requests can keep hitting a dead/disposed pool for the rest of the session instead of transparently recreating it.

**Why this matters**
- This is a real **reliability/lifecycle regression** at the new worker boundary: one transient worker failure can permanently break project indexing until extension reload.

**Assessment**
- **No critical issues**, but **not safe to proceed as-is** until worker-pool recovery/recreation after crash/exit is handled.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>43 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const { parentPort } = require('worker_threads');
4. 
5. const { buildProjectIndex } = require('../core/project/projectIndexer');
6. 
7. function serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
8.     return {
9.         ...snapshot,
10.         graph: snapshot.graph.toJSON(),
11.         keywordMap: [...snapshot.keywordMap.entries()],
12.         missingFiles: [...snapshot.missingFiles],
13.         cycles: [...snapshot.cycles],
14.     };
15. }
16. 
17. function serializeError(error) {
18.     return {
19.         message: error.message,
20.         stack: error.stack,
21.         name: error.name,
22.     };
23. }
24. 
25. parentPort.on('message', async (message) => {
26.     if (!message || message.type !== 'buildProjectIndex') return;
27. 
28.     try {
29.         const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(message.rootFile);
30.         parentPort.postMessage({
31.             requestId: message.requestId,
32.             snapshot: serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot),
33.             type: 'result',
34.         });
35.     } catch (error) {
36.         parentPort.postMessage({
37.             error: serializeError(error),
38.             requestId: message.requestId,
39.             type: 'error',
40.         });
41.     }
42. });
43.
```

</details>

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\project\projectGraph.js (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>116 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. class ProjectGraph {
4.     constructor() {
5.         this.children = new Map();
6.         this.includeEntries = new Map();
7.         this.parents = new Map();
8.         this.missingFiles = [];
9.         this.cycles = [];
10.     }
11. 
12.     addFile(filePath) {
13.         if (!this.children.has(filePath)) this.children.set(filePath, []);
14.         if (!this.includeEntries.has(filePath)) this.includeEntries.set(filePath, []);
15.         if (!this.parents.has(filePath)) this.parents.set(filePath, []);
16.     }
17. 
18.     addIncludeEntry(fromFile, entry) {
19.         this.addFile(fromFile);
20.         const entries = this.includeEntries.get(fromFile);
21.         if (entry.missing) {
22.             entries.push(entry);
23.             return;
24.         }
25.         const exists = entries.some(candidate =>
26.             candidate.filePath === entry.filePath && Boolean(candidate.missing) === Boolean(entry.missing)
27.         );
28.         if (!exists) {
29.             entries.push(entry);
30.         }
31.     }
32. 
33.     addIncludeEdge(fromFile, toFile) {
34.         this.addFile(fromFile);
35.         this.addFile(toFile);
36. 
37.         if (!this.children.get(fromFile).includes(toFile)) {
38.             this.children.get(fromFile).push(toFile);
39.         }
40.         if (!this.parents.get(toFile).includes(fromFile)) {
41.             this.parents.get(toFile).push(fromFile);
42.         }
43. 
44.         this.addIncludeEntry(fromFile, { filePath: toFile });
45.     }
46. 
47.     addMissingFile(record) {
48.         this.missingFiles.push(record);
49.         if (record.fromFile && record.filePath) {
50.             this.addIncludeEntry(record.fromFile, {
51.                 filePath: record.filePath,
52.                 fileName: record.fileName,
53.                 missing: true,
54.             });
55.         }
56.     }
57. 
58.     addCycle(record) {
59.         this.cycles.push(record);
60.     }
61. 
62.     getChildren(filePath) {
63.         return [...(this.children.get(filePath) || [])];
64.     }
65. 
66.     getParents(filePath) {
67.         return [...(this.parents.get(filePath) || [])];
68.     }
69. 
70.     getIncludeEntries(filePath) {
71.         return [...(this.includeEntries.get(filePath) || [])];
72.     }
73. 
74.     toTree(rootFile, ancestry = []) {
75.         if (ancestry.includes(rootFile)) {
76.             return {
77.                 filePath: rootFile,
78.                 children: [],
79.             };
80.         }
81. 
82.         return {
83.             filePath: rootFile,
84.             children: this.getIncludeEntries(rootFile).map(entry => (
85.                 entry.missing
86.                     ? { ...entry, children: [] }
87.                     : this.toTree(entry.filePath, [...ancestry, rootFile])
88.             )),
89.         };
90.     }
91. 
92.     toJSON() {
93.         return {
94.             children: [...this.children.entries()].map(([filePath, childFiles]) => [filePath, [...childFiles]]),
95.             includeEntries: [...this.includeEntries.entries()].map(([filePath, entries]) => [filePath, [...entries]]),
96.             parents: [...this.parents.entries()].map(([filePath, parentFiles]) => [filePath, [...parentFiles]]),
97.             missingFiles: [...this.missingFiles],
98.             cycles: [...this.cycles],
99.         };
100.     }
101. 
102.     static fromJSON(data = {}) {
103.         const graph = new ProjectGraph();
104.         graph.children = new Map(data.children || []);
105.         graph.includeEntries = new Map(data.includeEntries || []);
106.         graph.parents = new Map(data.parents || []);
107.         graph.missingFiles = [...(data.missingFiles || [])];
108.         graph.cycles = [...(data.cycles || [])];
109.         return graph;
110.     }
111. }
112. 
113. module.exports = {
114.     ProjectGraph,
115. };
116.
```

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const assert = require('assert');
4. const fs = require('fs');
5. const os = require('os');
6. const path = require('path');
7. 
8. describe('createWorkerPool', () => {
9.     it('builds project snapshots in a worker and hydrates graph and keywordMap', async () => {
10.         const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
11.         const workerPath = path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', 'src', 'worker', 'scanWorker.js');
12.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-worker-pool-'));
13.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
14.         const childFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'child.key');
15.         const pool = createWorkerPool({ workerPath });
16. 
17.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nchild.key\n', 'utf8');
18.         fs.writeFileSync(childFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
19. 
20.         try {
21.             const snapshot = await pool.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
22.             assert.ok(snapshot.keywordMap instanceof Map);
23.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath), [childFile]);
24.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
25.                 filePath: rootFile,
26.                 children: [
27.                     {
28.                         filePath: childFile,
29.                         children: [],
30.                     },
31.                 ],
32.             });
33.             assert.strictEqual(snapshot.missingFiles, snapshot.graph.missingFiles);
34.             assert.strictEqual(snapshot.cycles, snapshot.graph.cycles);
35.         } finally {
36.             await pool.dispose();
37.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
38.         }
39.     });
40. 
41.     it('rejects pending requests when the worker errors', async () => {
42.         const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
43.         const handlers = new Map();
44.         const fakeWorker = {
45.             on(eventName, handler) {
46.                 handlers.set(eventName, handler);
47.             },
48.             postMessage() {},
49.             terminate() {
50.                 return Promise.resolve(0);
51.             },
52.         };
53.         const pool = createWorkerPool({
54.             workerPath: 'fake-worker.js',
55.             workerFactory: () => fakeWorker,
56.         });
57. 
58.         const pendingRequest = pool.buildProjectIndex('project/main.k');
59.         handlers.get('error')(new Error('worker crashed'));
60. 
61.         await assert.rejects(pendingRequest, /worker crashed/);
62.         await pool.dispose();
63.     });
64. });
65.
```

<details>
<summary>98 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const { Worker } = require('worker_threads');
4. 
5. const { ProjectGraph } = require('../core/project/projectGraph');
6. 
7. function createWorkerPool({
8.     workerPath,
9.     workerFactory = (nextWorkerPath) => new Worker(nextWorkerPath),
10. } = {}) {
11.     if (typeof workerPath !== 'string' || workerPath.trim() === '') {
12.         throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerPath');
13.     }
14.     if (typeof workerFactory !== 'function') {
15.         throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerFactory function');
16.     }
17. 
18.     const worker = workerFactory(workerPath);
19.     const pendingRequests = new Map();
20.     let nextRequestId = 1;
21.     let disposed = false;
22. 
23.     function hydrateProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
24.         const graph = ProjectGraph.fromJSON(snapshot.graph);
25.         return {
26.             ...snapshot,
27.             graph,
28.             keywordMap: new Map(snapshot.keywordMap || []),
29.             missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
30.             cycles: graph.cycles,
31.         };
32.     }
33. 
34.     function rejectPendingRequests(error) {
35.         for (const pendingRequest of pendingRequests.values()) {
36.             pendingRequest.reject(error);
37.         }
38.         pendingRequests.clear();
39.     }
40. 
41.     worker.on('message', (message) => {
42.         const pendingRequest = pendingRequests.get(message.requestId);
43.         if (!pendingRequest) return;
44.         pendingRequests.delete(message.requestId);
45. 
46.         if (message.type === 'error') {
47.             const error = new Error(message.error.message);
48.             error.name = message.error.name;
49.             error.stack = message.error.stack;
50.             pendingRequest.reject(error);
51.             return;
52.         }
53. 
54.         pendingRequest.resolve(hydrateProjectSnapshot(message.snapshot));
55.     });
56.     worker.on('error', (error) => {
57.         disposed = true;
58.         rejectPendingRequests(error);
59.     });
60.     worker.on('exit', (code) => {
61.         if (disposed && pendingRequests.size === 0) return;
62. 
63.         disposed = true;
64.         rejectPendingRequests(new Error(`scan worker exited with code ${code}`));
65.     });
66. 
67.     return {
68.         buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
69.             if (disposed) {
70.                 return Promise.reject(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
71.             }
72. 
73.             const requestId = nextRequestId++;
74.             return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
75.                 pendingRequests.set(requestId, { reject, resolve });
76.                 worker.postMessage({
77.                     requestId,
78.                     rootFile,
79.                     type: 'buildProjectIndex',
80.                 });
81.             });
82.         },
83.         async dispose() {
84.             if (disposed) return;
85.             disposed = true;
86.             rejectPendingRequests(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
87.             await worker.terminate();
88.         },
89.         isDisposed() {
90.             return disposed;
91.         },
92.     };
93. }
94. 
95. module.exports = {
96.     createWorkerPool,
97. };
98.
```

</details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const { parentPort } = require('worker_threads');
4. 
5. const { buildProjectIndex } = require('../core/project/projectIndexer');
6. 
7. function serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
8.     return {
9.         ...snapshot,
10.         graph: snapshot.graph.toJSON(),
11.         keywordMap: [...snapshot.keywordMap.entries()],
12.         missingFiles: [...snapshot.missingFiles],
13.         cycles: [...snapshot.cycles],
14.     };
15. }
16. 
17. function serializeError(error) {
18.     return {
19.         message: error.message,
20.         stack: error.stack,
21.         name: error.name,
22.     };
23. }
24. 
25. parentPort.on('message', async (message) => {
26.     if (!message || message.type !== 'buildProjectIndex') return;
27. 
28.     try {
29.         const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(message.rootFile);
30.         parentPort.postMessage({
31.             requestId: message.requestId,
32.             snapshot: serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot),
33.             type: 'result',
34.         });
35.     } catch (error) {
36.         parentPort.postMessage({
37.             error: serializeError(error),
38.             requestId: message.requestId,
39.             type: 'error',
40.         });
41.     }
42. });
43.
```

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const assert = require('assert');
4. const fs = require('fs');
5. const os = require('os');
6. const path = require('path');
7. 
8. describe('createWorkerPool', () => {
9.     it('builds project snapshots in a worker and hydrates graph and keywordMap', async () => {
10.         const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
11.         const workerPath = path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', 'src', 'worker', 'scanWorker.js');
12.         const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-worker-pool-'));
13.         const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
14.         const childFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'child.key');
15.         const pool = createWorkerPool({ workerPath });
16. 
17.         fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nchild.key\n', 'utf8');
18.         fs.writeFileSync(childFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
19. 
20.         try {
21.             const snapshot = await pool.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
22.             assert.ok(snapshot.keywordMap instanceof Map);
23.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath), [childFile]);
24.             assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
25.                 filePath: rootFile,
26.                 children: [
27.                     {
28.                         filePath: childFile,
29.                         children: [],
30.                     },
31.                 ],
32.             });
33.             assert.strictEqual(snapshot.missingFiles, snapshot.graph.missingFiles);
34.             assert.strictEqual(snapshot.cycles, snapshot.graph.cycles);
35.         } finally {
36.             await pool.dispose();
37.             fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
38.         }
39.     });
40. 
41.     it('rejects pending requests when the worker errors', async () => {
42.         const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
43.         const handlers = new Map();
44.         const fakeWorker = {
45.             on(eventName, handler) {
46.                 handlers.set(eventName, handler);
47.             },
48.             postMessage() {},
49.             terminate() {
50.                 return Promise.resolve(0);
51.             },
52.         };
53.         const pool = createWorkerPool({
54.             workerPath: 'fake-worker.js',
55.             workerFactory: () => fakeWorker,
56.         });
57. 
58.         const pendingRequest = pool.buildProjectIndex('project/main.k');
59.         handlers.get('error')(new Error('worker crashed'));
60. 
61.         await assert.rejects(pendingRequest, /worker crashed/);
62.         await pool.dispose();
63.     });
64. });
65.
```

```diff
+    toJSON() {
+        return {
+            children: [...this.children.entries()].map(([filePath, childFiles]) => [filePath, [...childFiles]]),
+            includeEntries: [...this.includeEntries.entries()].map(([filePath, entries]) => [filePath, [...entries]]),
+            parents: [...this.parents.entries()].map(([filePath, parentFiles]) => [filePath, [...parentFiles]]),
+            missingFiles: [...this.missingFiles],
+            cycles: [...this.cycles],
+        };
+    }
+
+    static fromJSON(data = {}) {
+        const graph = new ProjectGraph();
+        graph.children = new Map(data.children || []);
+        graph.includeEntries = new Map(data.includeEntries || []);
+        graph.parents = new Map(data.parents || []);
+        graph.missingFiles = [...(data.missingFiles || [])];
+        graph.cycles = [...(data.cycles || [])];
+        return graph;
+    }

+describe('createProjectIndexLoader', () => {
+    it('creates the worker pool lazily on first project index request and disposes it', async () => {
+        const createdPools = [];
+        let disposed = false;
+        const snapshot = { rootFile: path.resolve('project', 'main.k') };
+        const loader = createProjectIndexLoader({
+            createPool({ workerPath }) {
+                createdPools.push(workerPath);
+                return {
+                    buildProjectIndex: async () => snapshot,
+                    dispose: async () => {
+                        disposed = true;
+                    },
+                };
+            },
+            workerPath: path.resolve('custom', 'scanWorker.js'),
+        });
+
+        assert.deepEqual(createdPools, []);
+        assert.strictEqual(await loader.buildProjectIndex(snapshot.rootFile), snapshot);
+        assert.deepEqual(createdPools, [path.resolve('custom', 'scanWorker.js')]);
+
+        await loader.dispose();
+        assert.equal(disposed, true);
+    });
+
+    it('recreates the worker pool after a fatal worker failure', async () => {
+        const createdPools = [];
+        const snapshot = { rootFile: path.resolve('project', 'main.k') };
+        let firstPoolDisposed = false;
+        const loader = createProjectIndexLoader({
+            createPool() {
+                createdPools.push(createdPools.length + 1);
+                if (createdPools.length === 1) {
+                    return {
+                        async buildProjectIndex() {
+                            firstPoolDisposed = true;
+                            throw new Error('worker crashed');
+                        },
+                        isDisposed() {
+                            return firstPoolDisposed;
+                        },
+                        dispose: async () => {},
+                    };
+                }
+
+                return {
+                    async buildProjectIndex() {
+                        return snapshot;
+                    },
+                    isDisposed() {
+                        return false;
+                    },
+                    dispose: async () => {},
+                };
+            },
+        });
+
+        await assert.rejects(loader.buildProjectIndex(snapshot.rootFile), /worker crashed/);
+        assert.strictEqual(await loader.buildProjectIndex(snapshot.rootFile), snapshot);
+        assert.deepEqual(createdPools, [1, 2]);
+    });
+});
```

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. const { createCacheManifestStore } = require('../../core/cache/cacheManifestStore');
7. 
8. function resolveRootFile(rootFile) {
9.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
10.         throw new TypeError('loadProjectSnapshot requires a rootFile path');
11.     }
12.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
13. }
14. 
15. function getRootCacheKey(rootFile) {
16.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
17.     return process.platform === 'win32'
18.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
19.         : resolvedRootFile;
20. }
21. 
22. function getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot) {
23.     if (Array.isArray(snapshot.files) && snapshot.files.length > 0) {
24.         return snapshot.files;
25.     }
26.     return [snapshot.rootFile];
27. }
28. 
29. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
30.     return left
31.         && right
32.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
33.         && left.size === right.size;
34. }
35. 
36. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
37.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
38.     return {
39.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
40.         size: stat.size,
41.     };
42. }
43. 
44. async function captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature) {
45.     const trackedFiles = [];
46.     const seen = new Set();
47. 
48.     for (const filePath of getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot)) {
49.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveRootFile(filePath);
50.         const fileCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
51.         if (seen.has(fileCacheKey)) continue;
52.         seen.add(fileCacheKey);
53.         trackedFiles.push({
54.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
55.             signature: await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath),
56.         });
57.     }
58. 
59.     return trackedFiles;
60. }
61. 
62. async function isSnapshotValid(entry, getFileSignature) {
63.     if (!Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles) || entry.trackedFiles.length === 0) {
64.         return false;
65.     }
66. 
67.     for (const trackedFile of entry.trackedFiles) {
68.         try {
69.             const currentSignature = await getFileSignature(trackedFile.filePath);
70.             if (!areFileSignaturesEqual(currentSignature, trackedFile.signature)) {
71.                 return false;
72.             }
73.         } catch (_error) {
74.             return false;
75.         }
76.     }
77. 
78.     return true;
79. }
80. 
81. function estimateSnapshotSize(snapshot) {
82.     return Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(snapshot), 'utf8');
83. }
84. 
85. function getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots) {
86.     let cachedSnapshotCount = 0;
87.     let totalSnapshotBytes = 0;
88. 
89.     for (const entry of snapshots.values()) {
90.         if (!entry || !entry.snapshot || typeof entry.byteSize !== 'number') continue;
91.         cachedSnapshotCount += 1;
92.         totalSnapshotBytes += entry.byteSize;
93.     }
94. 
95.     return {
96.         cachedSnapshotCount,
97.         totalSnapshotBytes,
98.     };
99. }
100. 
101. function createIndexClient({
102.     buildProjectIndex,
103.     getFileSignature = readFileSignature,
104.     estimateSnapshotSize: getSnapshotSize = estimateSnapshotSize,
105.     maxSnapshotBytes = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY,
106.     manifestStore = createCacheManifestStore(),
107. } = {}) {
108.     if (typeof buildProjectIndex !== 'function') {
109.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires a buildProjectIndex function');
110.     }
111.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
112.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires getFileSignature to be a function');
113.     }
114.     if (typeof getSnapshotSize !== 'function') {
115.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires estimateSnapshotSize to be a function');
116.     }
117.     if (typeof maxSnapshotBytes !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(maxSnapshotBytes) || maxSnapshotBytes <= 0) {
118.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires maxSnapshotBytes to be a positive number');
119.     }
120.     if (!manifestStore || typeof manifestStore.upsert !== 'function' || typeof manifestStore.remove !== 'function') {
121.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires manifestStore to support upsert and remove');
122.     }
123. 
124.     const snapshots = new Map();
125.     const generations = new Map();
126.     let accessSequence = 0;
127. 
128.     function getGeneration(rootCacheKey) {
129.         return generations.get(rootCacheKey) || 0;
130.     }
131. 
132.     function touchSnapshotEntry(entry) {
133.         entry.lastAccessedAt = ++accessSequence;
134.         manifestStore.upsert({
135.             rootFile: entry.snapshot.rootFile,
136.             trackedFiles: entry.trackedFiles.map(trackedFile => trackedFile.filePath),
137.             byteSize: entry.byteSize,
138.             lastAccessedAt: entry.lastAccessedAt,
139.         });
140.     }
141. 
142.     function evictSnapshotsIfNeeded(protectedRootCacheKey) {
143.         let cacheStats = getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
144.         while (cacheStats.totalSnapshotBytes > maxSnapshotBytes) {
145.             const evictionCandidates = [...snapshots.entries()]
146.                 .filter(([rootCacheKey, entry]) => (
147.                     rootCacheKey !== protectedRootCacheKey
148.                     && entry
149.                     && entry.snapshot
150.                     && typeof entry.byteSize === 'number'
151.                 ))
152.                 .sort((left, right) => left[1].lastAccessedAt - right[1].lastAccessedAt);
153. 
154.             if (evictionCandidates.length === 0) break;
155.             manifestStore.remove(evictionCandidates[0][1].snapshot.rootFile);
156.             snapshots.delete(evictionCandidates[0][0]);
157.             cacheStats = getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
158.         }
159.     }
160. 
161.     async function loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile) {
162.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
163.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
164. 
165.         while (snapshots.has(rootCacheKey)) {
166.             const cachedEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
167.             if (cachedEntry.promise) return cachedEntry.promise;
168. 
169.             const valid = await isSnapshotValid(cachedEntry, getFileSignature);
170.             const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
171.             if (currentEntry !== cachedEntry) continue;
172.             if (valid) {
173.                 touchSnapshotEntry(cachedEntry);
174.                 return cachedEntry.snapshot;
175.             }
176. 
177.             invalidate(resolvedRootFile);
178.         }
179. 
180.         const generation = getGeneration(rootCacheKey);
181.         const promise = Promise.resolve(buildProjectIndex(resolvedRootFile))
182.             .then(async snapshot => {
183.                 let trackedFiles = null;
184.                 try {
185.                     trackedFiles = await captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature);
186.                 } catch (_error) {
187.                     trackedFiles = null;
188.                 }
189. 
190.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
191.                 if (getGeneration(rootCacheKey) === generation && currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
192.                     if (trackedFiles) {
193.                         const entry = {
194.                             snapshot,
195.                             trackedFiles,
196.                             byteSize: getSnapshotSize(snapshot),
197.                         };
198.                         touchSnapshotEntry(entry);
199.                         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, entry);
200.                         evictSnapshotsIfNeeded(rootCacheKey);
201.                     } else {
202.                         manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
203.                         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
204.                     }
205.                 }
206.                 return snapshot;
207.             })
208.             .catch(error => {
209.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
210.                 if (currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
211.                     manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
212.                     snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
213.                 }
214.                 throw error;
215.             });
216. 
217.         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise });
218.         return promise;
219.     }
220. 
221.     function invalidate(rootFile) {
222.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
223.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
224.         generations.set(rootCacheKey, getGeneration(rootCacheKey) + 1);
225.         manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
226.         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
227.     }
228. 
229.     return {
230.         getCacheStats() {
231.             return getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
232.         },
233.         getManifestEntries() {
234.             return manifestStore.list();
235.         },
236.         invalidate,
237.         loadProjectSnapshot,
238.     };
239. }
240. 
241. module.exports = {
242.     createIndexClient,
243. };
```

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. const includeScanner = require('../parser/includeScanner');
7. const keywordScanner = require('../parser/keywordScanner');
8. const { ProjectGraph } = require('./projectGraph');
9. 
10. function resolveProjectFile(filePath) {
11.     return path.resolve(filePath);
12. }
13. 
14. function getProjectFileCacheKey(filePath) {
15.     const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
16.     return process.platform === 'win32'
17.         ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()
18.         : resolvedFilePath;
19. }
20. 
21. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
22.     return left
23.         && right
24.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
25.         && left.size === right.size;
26. }
27. 
28. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
29.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
30.     return {
31.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
32.         size: stat.size,
33.     };
34. }
35. 
36. function resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, searchPaths) {
37.     for (const searchPath of searchPaths) {
38.         const fullPath = path.resolve(searchPath, fileName);
39.         if (fs.existsSync(fullPath)) return fullPath;
40.     }
41.     return null;
42. }
43. 
44. function addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, keywords) {
45.     for (const keywordEntry of keywords) {
46.         if (!keywordMap.has(keywordEntry.keyword)) keywordMap.set(keywordEntry.keyword, []);
47.         keywordMap.get(keywordEntry.keyword).push(keywordEntry);
48.     }
49. }
50. 
51. function createProjectIndexer({
52.     collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile = includeScanner.collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile,
53.     collectKeywordsFromFile = keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile,
54.     getFileSignature = readFileSignature,
55. } = {}) {
56.     if (typeof collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile !== 'function') {
57.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile to be a function');
58.     }
59.     if (typeof collectKeywordsFromFile !== 'function') {
60.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires collectKeywordsFromFile to be a function');
61.     }
62.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
63.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires getFileSignature to be a function');
64.     }
65. 
66.     const fileScanCache = new Map();
67. 
68.     async function loadFileScan(filePath, stats) {
69.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
70.         const fileCacheKey = getProjectFileCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
71.         const signature = await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath);
72.         const cachedEntry = fileScanCache.get(fileCacheKey);
73. 
74.         if (cachedEntry && areFileSignaturesEqual(cachedEntry.signature, signature)) {
75.             stats.reusedFileCount += 1;
76.             return cachedEntry.scanResult;
77.         }
78. 
79.         const keywords = await collectKeywordsFromFile(resolvedFilePath);
80.         const { includeEntries, searchPaths } = await collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(resolvedFilePath);
81.         const scanResult = {
82.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
83.             keywords,
84.             includeEntries,
85.             searchPaths,
86.         };
87. 
88.         fileScanCache.set(fileCacheKey, {
89.             signature,
90.             scanResult,
91.         });
92.         stats.scannedFileCount += 1;
93.         return scanResult;
94.     }
95. 
96.     async function buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
97.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveProjectFile(rootFile);
98.         const files = [];
99.         const keywordMap = new Map();
100.         const graph = new ProjectGraph();
101.         const visited = new Set();
102.         const stats = {
103.             scannedFileCount: 0,
104.             reusedFileCount: 0,
105.         };
106. 
107.         async function visit(filePath, ancestry = []) {
108.             const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
109.             if (visited.has(resolvedFilePath)) return;
110.             visited.add(resolvedFilePath);
111.             files.push(resolvedFilePath);
112.             graph.addFile(resolvedFilePath);
113. 
114.             const scanResult = await loadFileScan(resolvedFilePath, stats);
115.             addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, scanResult.keywords);
116. 
117.             for (const { fileName } of scanResult.includeEntries) {
118.                 const resolvedPath = resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, scanResult.searchPaths);
119.                 if (!resolvedPath) {
120.                     graph.addMissingFile({
121.                         fromFile: resolvedFilePath,
122.                         fileName,
123.                         filePath: path.resolve(scanResult.searchPaths[0] || path.dirname(resolvedFilePath), fileName),
124.                     });
125.                     continue;
126.                 }
127. 
128.                 if (ancestry.includes(resolvedPath) || resolvedPath === resolvedFilePath) {
129.                     graph.addCycle({
130.                         fromFile: resolvedFilePath,
131.                         toFile: resolvedPath,
132.                         path: [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath],
133.                     });
134.                     continue;
135.                 }
136. 
137.                 graph.addIncludeEdge(resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath);
138.                 await visit(resolvedPath, [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath]);
139.             }
140.         }
141. 
142.         await visit(resolvedRootFile);
143. 
144.         return {
145.             rootFile: resolvedRootFile,
146.             files,
147.             graph,
148.             keywordMap,
149.             missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
150.             cycles: graph.cycles,
151.             stats,
152.         };
153.     }
154. 
155.     return {
156.         buildProjectIndex,
157.     };
158. }
159. 
160. const defaultProjectIndexer = createProjectIndexer();
161. 
162. module.exports = {
163.     buildProjectIndex: defaultProjectIndexer.buildProjectIndex,
164.     createProjectIndexer,
165. };
166.
```

</details>

### ✅ `powershell`

**Show diffs for untracked worker files**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "git --no-pager diff --no-index -- NUL src/worker/workerPool.js ; Write-Host '---'; git --no-pager diff --no-index -- NUL src/worker/scanWorker.js ; Write-Host '---'; git --no-pager diff --no-index -- NUL test/worker/workerPool.test.js",
  "description": "Show diffs for untracked worker files",
  "mode": "sync",
  "initial_wait": 30
```
