```

</details>

<details>
<summary>224 lines</summary>

```diff
diff --git a/src/worker/workerPool.js b/src/worker/workerPool.js
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..69f2d48
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/worker/workerPool.js
@@ -0,0 +1,97 @@
+'use strict';
+
+const { Worker } = require('worker_threads');
+
+const { ProjectGraph } = require('../core/project/projectGraph');
+
+function createWorkerPool({
+    workerPath,
+    workerFactory = (nextWorkerPath) => new Worker(nextWorkerPath),
+} = {}) {
+    if (typeof workerPath !== 'string' || workerPath.trim() === '') {
+        throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerPath');
+    }
+    if (typeof workerFactory !== 'function') {
+        throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerFactory function');
+    }
+
+    const worker = workerFactory(workerPath);
+    const pendingRequests = new Map();
+    let nextRequestId = 1;
+    let disposed = false;
+
+    function hydrateProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
+        const graph = ProjectGraph.fromJSON(snapshot.graph);
+        return {
+            ...snapshot,
+            graph,
+            keywordMap: new Map(snapshot.keywordMap || []),
+            missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
+            cycles: graph.cycles,
+        };
+    }
+
+    function rejectPendingRequests(error) {
+        for (const pendingRequest of pendingRequests.values()) {
+            pendingRequest.reject(error);
+        }
+        pendingRequests.clear();
+    }
+
+    worker.on('message', (message) => {
+        const pendingRequest = pendingRequests.get(message.requestId);
+        if (!pendingRequest) return;
+        pendingRequests.delete(message.requestId);
+
+        if (message.type === 'error') {
+            const error = new Error(message.error.message);
+            error.name = message.error.name;
+            error.stack = message.error.stack;
+            pendingRequest.reject(error);
+            return;
+        }
+
+        pendingRequest.resolve(hydrateProjectSnapshot(message.snapshot));
+    });
+    worker.on('error', (error) => {
+        disposed = true;
+        rejectPendingRequests(error);
+    });
+    worker.on('exit', (code) => {
+        if (disposed && pendingRequests.size === 0) return;
+
+        disposed = true;
+        rejectPendingRequests(new Error(`scan worker exited with code ${code}`));
+    });
+
+    return {
+        buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
+            if (disposed) {
+                return Promise.reject(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
+            }
+
+            const requestId = nextRequestId++;
+            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
+                pendingRequests.set(requestId, { reject, resolve });
+                worker.postMessage({
+                    requestId,
+                    rootFile,
+                    type: 'buildProjectIndex',
+                });
+            });
+        },
+        async dispose() {
+            if (disposed) return;
+            disposed = true;
+            rejectPendingRequests(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
+            await worker.terminate();
+        },
+        isDisposed() {
+            return disposed;
+        },
+    };
+}
+
+module.exports = {
+    createWorkerPool,
+};
---
diff --git a/src/worker/scanWorker.js b/src/worker/scanWorker.js
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e2c85d7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/worker/scanWorker.js
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+'use strict';
+
+const { parentPort } = require('worker_threads');
+
+const { buildProjectIndex } = require('../core/project/projectIndexer');
+
+function serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
+    return {
+        ...snapshot,
+        graph: snapshot.graph.toJSON(),
+        keywordMap: [...snapshot.keywordMap.entries()],
+        missingFiles: [...snapshot.missingFiles],
+        cycles: [...snapshot.cycles],
+    };
+}
+
+function serializeError(error) {
+    return {
+        message: error.message,
+        stack: error.stack,
+        name: error.name,
+    };
+}
+
+parentPort.on('message', async (message) => {
+    if (!message || message.type !== 'buildProjectIndex') return;
+
+    try {
+        const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(message.rootFile);
+        parentPort.postMessage({
+            requestId: message.requestId,
+            snapshot: serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot),
+            type: 'result',
+        });
+    } catch (error) {
+        parentPort.postMessage({
+            error: serializeError(error),
+            requestId: message.requestId,
+            type: 'error',
+        });
+    }
+});
---
diff --git a/test/worker/workerPool.test.js b/test/worker/workerPool.test.js
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..aa62eb0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/test/worker/workerPool.test.js
@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+'use strict';
+
+const assert = require('assert');
+const fs = require('fs');
+const os = require('os');
+const path = require('path');
+
+describe('createWorkerPool', () => {
+    it('builds project snapshots in a worker and hydrates graph and keywordMap', async () => {
+        const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
+        const workerPath = path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', 'src', 'worker', 'scanWorker.js');
+        const tempRoot = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-worker-pool-'));
+        const rootFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'main.k');
+        const childFile = path.join(tempRoot, 'child.key');
+        const pool = createWorkerPool({ workerPath });
+
+        fs.writeFileSync(rootFile, '*INCLUDE\nchild.key\n', 'utf8');
+        fs.writeFileSync(childFile, '*PART\n', 'utf8');
+
+        try {
+            const snapshot = await pool.buildProjectIndex(rootFile);
+            assert.ok(snapshot.keywordMap instanceof Map);
+            assert.deepEqual(snapshot.keywordMap.get('PART').map(entry => entry.filePath), [childFile]);
+            assert.deepEqual(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile), {
+                filePath: rootFile,
+                children: [
+                    {
+                        filePath: childFile,
+                        children: [],
+                    },
+                ],
+            });
+            assert.strictEqual(snapshot.missingFiles, snapshot.graph.missingFiles);
+            assert.strictEqual(snapshot.cycles, snapshot.graph.cycles);
+        } finally {
+            await pool.dispose();
+            fs.rmSync(tempRoot, { recursive: true, force: true });
+        }
+    });
+
+    it('rejects pending requests when the worker errors', async () => {
+        const { createWorkerPool } = require('../../src/worker/workerPool');
+        const handlers = new Map();
+        const fakeWorker = {
+            on(eventName, handler) {
+                handlers.set(eventName, handler);
+            },
+            postMessage() {},
+            terminate() {
+                return Promise.resolve(0);
+            },
+        };
+        const pool = createWorkerPool({
+            workerPath: 'fake-worker.js',
+            workerFactory: () => fakeWorker,
+        });
+
+        const pendingRequest = pool.buildProjectIndex('project/main.k');
+        handlers.get('error')(new Error('worker crashed'));
+
+        await assert.rejects(pendingRequest, /worker crashed/);
+        await pool.dispose();
+    });
+});
<exited with exit code 1>

### ✅ `task`

**Review worker diff**

<details>

{
  "description": "Review worker diff",
  "agent_type": "code-review",
  "name": "worker-boundary-review",
  "prompt": "Review the current unstaged changes in D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna with focus on worker-thread project index boundary and self-healing after worker crash. Context: batch adds worker-side project index execution, serializes ProjectGraph and keywordMap across worker boundary, wires createProjectIndexLoader() lazily in src/extension.js, and includes self-healing after worker crash by exposing workerPool.isDisposed() and recreating cached pool on subsequent requests. Inspect the actual current unstaged diff (including untracked files under src/worker and test/worker) and report only substantive correctness, reliability, or regression issues. Ignore style/trivia. If no meaningful issues remain, say so clearly."
}
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>24 lines</summary>

```
So the bug exists in principle, but it can only manifest if:
1. Someone calls `fromJSON` twice with the exact same data object (not just equivalent data, but the same reference)
2. AND someone directly mutates the internal Map arrays (bypassing the public API)

In the actual worker scenario, this can't happen because:
1. Each `postMessage` does a structured clone, creating fresh data objects
2. The snapshot is treated as read-only after construction

This is a latent API bug that doesn't affect the current implementation, but it would be better to fix it. However, the user asked me to focus on "substantive correctness, reliability, or regression issues" and ignore style/trivia. Given that this bug doesn't manifest in the current usage pattern and is protected by the defensive copying in the getters, I'll note it but not flag it as a critical issue.

Let me do one final comprehensive review of all the changes:

## Summary of Review

I've thoroughly reviewed the unstaged changes including:
- `src/core/project/projectGraph.js` - Added `toJSON()` and `fromJSON()` for serialization
- `src/extension.js` - Added `createProjectIndexLoader()` with lazy initialization and self-healing
- `test/extension.test.js` - Added tests for the loader
- `src/worker/scanWorker.js` - Worker-side project index execution
- `src/worker/workerPool.js` - Worker pool management with disposal tracking
- `test/worker/workerPool.test.js` - Worker pool tests

All tests pass (119 passing). The implementation correctly:
1. ✅ Serializes ProjectGraph and keywordMap across the worker boundary
2. ✅ Lazily creates the worker pool on first request
3. ✅ Self-heals after worker crash by detecting disposed state and recreating the pool
4. ✅ Properly disposes the worker pool on extension deactivation
5. ✅ Preserves snapshot structure (`files`, `stats`, `missingFiles`, `cycles`) through serialization
6. ✅ Handles errors gracefully with proper error serialization and rejection

No significant issues found in the reviewed changes.
```

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\client\providers\includeTreeProvider.js

<details>
<summary>119 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. const vscode = require('vscode');
6. 
7. const includeScanner = require('../../core/parser/includeScanner');
8. 
9. class IncludeItem extends vscode.TreeItem {
10.     constructor(filePath, exists) {
11.         super(path.basename(filePath), vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed);
12.         this.filePath = filePath;
13.         this.children = [];
14.         this.tooltip = filePath;
15.         this.iconPath = new vscode.ThemeIcon(exists ? 'file' : 'warning');
16.         if (!exists) this.description = 'not found';
17.         if (exists) {
18.             this.command = { command: 'vscode.open', title: 'Open', arguments: [vscode.Uri.file(filePath)] };
19.         }
20.     }
21. }
22. 
23. class LsdynaIncludeTreeProvider {
24.     constructor({ searchFileFromPaths, loadProjectSnapshot } = {}) {
25.         this.searchFileFromPaths = searchFileFromPaths;
26.         this.loadProjectSnapshot = loadProjectSnapshot;
27.         this._onDidChangeTreeData = new vscode.EventEmitter();
28.         this.onDidChangeTreeData = this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
29.         this.root = null;
30.     }
31. 
32.     async scan() {
33.         const editor = vscode.window.activeTextEditor;
34.         if (!editor || editor.document.languageId !== 'lsdyna') {
35.             vscode.window.showWarningMessage('Open an LS-DYNA file first.');
36.             return;
37.         }
38.         await vscode.window.withProgress(
39.             { location: vscode.ProgressLocation.Notification, title: 'Scanning includes…', cancellable: false },
40.             async (progress) => {
41.                 if (this.loadProjectSnapshot) {
42.                     const snapshot = await this.loadProjectSnapshot(editor.document.uri.fsPath);
43.                     this.root = this._buildRootFromSnapshot(snapshot, editor.document.uri.fsPath);
44.                 } else {
45.                     this.root = await this._buildItem(editor.document.uri.fsPath, new Set(), progress);
46.                 }
47.                 this.root.collapsibleState = vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.Expanded;
48.                 this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
49.             }
50.         );
51.     }
52. 
53.     _buildItemFromTreeNode(node) {
54.         const exists = !node.missing && fs.existsSync(node.filePath);
55.         const item = new IncludeItem(node.filePath, exists);
56.         item.children = (node.children || []).map(childNode => this._buildItemFromTreeNode(childNode));
57.         item.collapsibleState = item.children.length > 0
58.             ? vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed
59.             : vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.None;
60.         return item;
61.     }
62. 
63.     _buildRootFromSnapshot(snapshot, rootFile) {
64.         return this._buildItemFromTreeNode(snapshot.graph.toTree(rootFile));
65.     }
66. 
67.     async _buildItem(filePath, visited, progress) {
68.         const exists = fs.existsSync(filePath);
69.         const item = new IncludeItem(filePath, exists);
70. 
71.         if (!exists || visited.has(filePath)) {
72.             item.collapsibleState = vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.None;
73.             return item;
74.         }
75. 
76.         visited.add(filePath);
77.         progress.report({ message: path.basename(filePath) });
78. 
79.         let includeEntries;
80.         let searchPaths;
81.         try {
82.             ({ includeEntries, searchPaths } = await includeScanner.collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(filePath));
83.         } catch (error) {
84.             item.description = 'scan failed';
85.             item.tooltip = `${filePath}\n${error.message}`;
86.             item.collapsibleState = vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.None;
87.             return item;
88.         }
89. 
90.         for (const { fileName } of includeEntries) {
91.             let childPath;
92.             try {
93.                 childPath = this.searchFileFromPaths(fileName, searchPaths);
94.             } catch (e) {
95.                 childPath = path.resolve(path.dirname(filePath), fileName);
96.             }
97.             item.children.push(await this._buildItem(childPath, new Set(visited), progress));
98.         }
99. 
100.         item.collapsibleState = item.children.length > 0
101.             ? vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed
102.             : vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.None;
103. 
104.         await new Promise(r => setImmediate(r));
105.         return item;
106.     }
107. 
108.     getTreeItem(element) { return element; }
109. 
110.     getChildren(element) {
111.         if (!this.root) return [];
112.         return element ? element.children : [this.root];
113.     }
114. }
115. 
116. module.exports = {
117.     LsdynaIncludeTreeProvider,
118. };
119.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>146 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. const vscode = require('vscode');
6. 
7. const keywordScanner = require('../../core/parser/keywordScanner');
8. 
9. class KeywordItem extends vscode.TreeItem {
10.     constructor(keyword) {
11.         super(keyword, vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.Collapsed);
12.         this.children = [];
13.         this.iconPath = new vscode.ThemeIcon('symbol-keyword');
14.     }
15. }
16. 
17. class KeywordUsageItem extends vscode.TreeItem {
18.     constructor(filePath, lineIndex, rootDir) {
19.         const rel = path.relative(rootDir, filePath);
20.         super(`${rel}  :${lineIndex + 1}`, vscode.TreeItemCollapsibleState.None);
21.         this.iconPath = new vscode.ThemeIcon('file');
22.         this.tooltip = `${filePath}:${lineIndex + 1}`;
23.         this.command = {
24.             command: 'extension.goToKeywordUsage',
25.             title: 'Go to keyword',
26.             arguments: [filePath, lineIndex],
27.         };
28.     }
29. }
30. 
31. class LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider {
32.     constructor({ collectIncludeFiles, loadProjectSnapshot, shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan } = {}) {
33.         this.collectIncludeFiles = collectIncludeFiles;
34.         this.loadProjectSnapshot = loadProjectSnapshot;
35.         this.shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan = shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan;
36.         this._onDidChangeTreeData = new vscode.EventEmitter();
37.         this.onDidChangeTreeData = this._onDidChangeTreeData.event;
38.         this.roots = [];
39.         this._mode = 'local'; // 'local' | 'recursive'
40.     }
41. 
42.     _setMode(mode) {
43.         this._mode = mode;
44.         vscode.commands.executeCommand('setContext', 'lsdyna.keywordIndexMode', mode);
45.     }
46. 
47.     _buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir) {
48.         return [...keywordMap.entries()]
49.             .sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b))
50.             .map(([keyword, usages]) => {
51.                 const item = new KeywordItem(keyword);
52.                 item.children = usages.map(({ filePath, lineIndex }) =>
53.                     new KeywordUsageItem(filePath, lineIndex, rootDir)
54.                 );
55.                 return item;
56.             });
57.     }
58. 
59.     async _buildRootsAsync(filePaths, rootDir) {
60.         const keywordMap = new Map();
61.         for (const filePath of filePaths) {
62.             if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) continue;
63.             const keywords = await keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile(filePath);
64.             for (const { keyword, lineIndex } of keywords) {
65.                 if (!keywordMap.has(keyword)) keywordMap.set(keyword, []);
66.                 keywordMap.get(keyword).push({ filePath, lineIndex });
67.             }
68.         }
69.         return this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir);
70.     }
71. 
72.     _buildRootsFromSnapshot(snapshot, rootDir) {
73.         return this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(snapshot.keywordMap, rootDir);
74.     }
75. 
76.     refreshFromDocument(document) {
77.         if (this._mode !== 'local') return;
78.         if (!document || document.languageId !== 'lsdyna') return;
79.         if (this.shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) {
80.             this.roots = [];
81.             this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
82.             return;
83.         }
84.         const filePath = document.uri.fsPath;
85.         const rootDir = path.dirname(filePath);
86.         const keywordMap = new Map();
87.         for (const { keyword, lineIndex } of keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromLineReader(
88.             document.lineCount,
89.             i => document.lineAt(i).text,
90.             filePath
91.         )) {
92.             if (!keywordMap.has(keyword)) keywordMap.set(keyword, []);
93.             keywordMap.get(keyword).push({ filePath, lineIndex });
94.         }
95.         this.roots = this._buildRootsFromKeywordMap(keywordMap, rootDir);
96.         this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
97.     }
98. 
99.     async scan() {
100.         const editor = vscode.window.activeTextEditor;
101.         if (!editor || editor.document.languageId !== 'lsdyna') {
102.             vscode.window.showWarningMessage('Open an LS-DYNA file first.');
103.             return;
104.         }
105.         const rootFile = editor.document.uri.fsPath;
106.         const rootDir = path.dirname(rootFile);
107.         await vscode.window.withProgress(
108.             { location: vscode.ProgressLocation.Notification, title: 'Scanning keywords…', cancellable: false },
109.             async (progress) => {
110.                 if (this.loadProjectSnapshot) {
111.                     const snapshot = await this.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
112.                     this.roots = this._buildRootsFromSnapshot(snapshot, rootDir);
113.                 } else {
114.                     const files = await this.collectIncludeFiles(rootFile, (count) => {
115.                         progress.report({ message: `${count} file${count === 1 ? '' : 's'} found` });
116.                     });
117.                     this.roots = await this._buildRootsAsync(files, rootDir);
118.                 }
119.                 this._setMode('recursive');
120.                 this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
121.             }
122.         );
123.     }
124. 
125.     setLocal() {
126.         this._setMode('local');
127.         if (vscode.window.activeTextEditor) {
128.             this.refreshFromDocument(vscode.window.activeTextEditor.document);
129.         } else {
130.             this.roots = [];
131.             this._onDidChangeTreeData.fire(undefined);
132.         }
133.     }
134. 
135.     getTreeItem(element) { return element; }
136. 
137.     getChildren(element) {
138.         if (element) return element.children;
139.         return this.roots;
140.     }
141. }
142. 
143. module.exports = {
144.     LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider,
145. };
146.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 897m 19s</sub>

### ✅ `read_agent`

**Superpowers:code-reviewer agent (Review worker boundary)**

<details>
<summary>32 lines</summary>

```
Agent is idle (waiting for messages). agent_id: worker-boundary-review, agent_type: superpowers:code-reviewer, status: idle, description: Review worker boundary, elapsed: 5353s, total_turns: 2

[Turn 0]
I found **one meaningful issue**.

**What looks good**
- Lazy `createProjectIndexLoader()` avoids eager worker startup in `activate()`.
- ProjectGraph hydration across the worker boundary looks deliberate and tested.
- Tests cover both worker round-trip and lazy creation/disposal.

**Important**
- **Worker crash/exit does not appear to self-heal**
  - In `src/worker/workerPool.js:56-70`, worker `error`/`exit` disposes the pool / rejects pending work.
  - In `src/extension.js:1030-1047`, `createProjectIndexLoader()` caches that pool and only replaces it on explicit `dispose()`.
  - Result: if the worker dies once, later indexing requests can keep hitting a dead/disposed pool for the rest of the session instead of transparently recreating it.

**Why this matters**
- This is a real **reliability/lifecycle regression** at the new worker boundary: one transient worker failure can permanently break project indexing until extension reload.

**Assessment**
- **No critical issues**, but **not safe to proceed as-is** until worker-pool recovery/recreation after crash/exit is handled.

[Turn 1]
[Message]
I addressed your lifecycle concern. Changes: src/worker/workerPool.js now exposes isDisposed(); src/extension.js createProjectIndexLoader() now checks whether the cached pool is disposed before reuse and clears the cached pool after a fatal failure from a disposed worker, so subsequent requests recreate a fresh pool. I also added a regression test in test/extension.test.js that fails on the old behavior and now passes. Please review the updated unstaged diff and tell me if any substantive correctness or reliability issues remain.

[Response]
I reviewed the updated unstaged diff and **don’t see any substantive issues remaining**.

**What’s good**
- The prior dead cached-pool reuse bug appears properly fixed.
- `workerPool` now exposes disposal state cleanly.
- `createProjectIndexLoader()` now avoids reusing a disposed pool and clears the cache on fatal disposed-worker failure.
- The new regression test covers the previously broken path.

**Findings**
- **Critical:** None
- **Important:** None
- **Suggestions:** None

**Assessment**
- **Safe to proceed.**
```

</details>

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:143:### L2 层：基于持久化存储设施的序列化磁盘缓存（Disk-backed Cache）
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:144:对于那些从 L1 被逐出，或者诸如标准件库、大面积网格结构定义（通常长达数月不会被工程师改动）等底层文件，系统需要利用 L2 缓存层将其状态持久化至物理磁盘上。Visual Studio Code 官方规范为此提供了 `ExtensionContext.globalStorageUri` 工作区专用存储路径。
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:149:一旦底层文件的时间戳自上次关闭后毫无更改，L2 缓存管理器便会将 FlatBuffers 二进制数据块直接映射进内存，绕过词法解析器、语法分析器和树生成器的所有流程，瞬间在插件界面上复原包含所有层级深度的 Include Tree 列表与 Keyword Index 全局变量字典，从而将项目的完全冷启动时间由几分钟压缩至几百毫秒之内。
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:154:| **L2（持久缓存）** | 工作区目录下的 SQLite / 本地序列化二进制簇 | 深度序列化压实后的宏观节点坐标、关键字统计分布与引用链表 | 磁盘配额上限约束（例如设定最大2GB工作区限额） | 微小延迟 (10 - 50ms) |
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:167:全量模式则由位于后台核心的 Language Server 负责汇总调度。它会在并行的 DAG 检索通道中，整合从 L1 读取的活动索引表和从 L2 抽取出的休眠文件索引序列。
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:173:通过将负载转移至基于 Language Server Protocol 协议与 Web/Node Worker 线程池结合的异步隔离架构中，系统完美达成了“无阻塞卡顿”的严苛设计指标 [7, 16]。利用抛弃全量字符串挂载，转而采取直击底层的二进制缓存滑窗字节识别算法，将 400MB 级别体积文件的解析阻力压缩至极具竞争力的毫秒级。而巧妙融合了区间树增量检测技术的机制与具备图拓扑认知能力的 L1/L2 阶梯淘汰制磁盘持久化缓存相结合，使得系统在应对局部代码细微修改与重度断电重启加载的严酷场景下，依然能够游刃有余，拒绝任何形式的算力与磁盘 I/O 资源浪费。
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:227:*   **L2 本地数据库缓存**：
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:251:这是一个非常关键且切中要害的问题。考虑到 LS-DYNA 的单一文件动辄数百兆，如果在解析多个大型工程项目后不对持久化数据进行限制，本地缓存（L2 缓存）会极快地吞噬用户的硬盘空间。
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:253:为了有效管理存放在 VS Code `ExtensionContext.globalStorageUri` 路径下的磁盘缓存，建议在架构设计中引入以下机制：
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:347:对于动辄包含数十个大文件的项目，全量重新解析不可接受。同时，LS-DYNA 文件体积庞大，如果不加节制地缓存，用户的硬盘会迅速被吞噬。必须建立 L1/L2 多层生命周期缓存，并辅以严格的磁盘清查机制。
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:352:*   **L2 磁盘持久化缓存 (Disk-backed Cache)**：
D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_plan.md:377:本开发指南所构建的高性能架构，通过 LSP + Worker 线程池实现彻底的前后端解耦；通过 `fs.createReadStream` 结合二进制特征码寻优算法完成极致的磁盘读取；依托 Interval Tree 实现高能效的细粒度文本增量修补；最终凭借带有严格空间配额与 Auto-Vacuum 机制的 SQLite L2 缓存体系，解决项目的秒级加载与内存、硬盘资源侵占问题。遵循此技术路线，团队即便是新手也能构建出一个在大型 CAE 工业环境中运行稳健、体验丝滑的顶级 VS Code 插件工具。

1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. const { createCacheManifestStore } = require('../../core/cache/cacheManifestStore');
7. 
8. function resolveRootFile(rootFile) {
9.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
10.         throw new TypeError('loadProjectSnapshot requires a rootFile path');
11.     }
12.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
13. }
14. 
15. function getRootCacheKey(rootFile) {
16.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
17.     return process.platform === 'win32'
18.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
19.         : resolvedRootFile;
20. }
21. 
22. function getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot) {
23.     if (Array.isArray(snapshot.files) && snapshot.files.length > 0) {
24.         return snapshot.files;
25.     }
26.     return [snapshot.rootFile];
27. }
28. 
29. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
30.     return left
31.         && right
32.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
33.         && left.size === right.size;
34. }
35. 
36. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
37.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
38.     return {
39.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
40.         size: stat.size,
41.     };
42. }
43. 
44. async function captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature) {
45.     const trackedFiles = [];
46.     const seen = new Set();
47. 
48.     for (const filePath of getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot)) {
49.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveRootFile(filePath);
50.         const fileCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
51.         if (seen.has(fileCacheKey)) continue;
52.         seen.add(fileCacheKey);
53.         trackedFiles.push({
54.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
55.             signature: await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath),
56.         });
57.     }
58. 
59.     return trackedFiles;
60. }
61. 
62. async function isSnapshotValid(entry, getFileSignature) {
63.     if (!Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles) || entry.trackedFiles.length === 0) {
64.         return false;
65.     }
66. 
67.     for (const trackedFile of entry.trackedFiles) {
68.         try {
69.             const currentSignature = await getFileSignature(trackedFile.filePath);
70.             if (!areFileSignaturesEqual(currentSignature, trackedFile.signature)) {
71.                 return false;
72.             }
73.         } catch (_error) {
74.             return false;
75.         }
76.     }
77. 
78.     return true;
79. }
80. 
81. function estimateSnapshotSize(snapshot) {
82.     return Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(snapshot), 'utf8');
83. }
84. 
85. function getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots) {
86.     let cachedSnapshotCount = 0;
87.     let totalSnapshotBytes = 0;
88. 
89.     for (const entry of snapshots.values()) {
90.         if (!entry || !entry.snapshot || typeof entry.byteSize !== 'number') continue;
91.         cachedSnapshotCount += 1;
92.         totalSnapshotBytes += entry.byteSize;
93.     }
94. 
95.     return {
96.         cachedSnapshotCount,
97.         totalSnapshotBytes,
98.     };
99. }
100. 
101. function createIndexClient({
102.     buildProjectIndex,
103.     getFileSignature = readFileSignature,
104.     estimateSnapshotSize: getSnapshotSize = estimateSnapshotSize,
105.     maxSnapshotBytes = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY,
106.     manifestStore = createCacheManifestStore(),
107. } = {}) {
108.     if (typeof buildProjectIndex !== 'function') {
109.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires a buildProjectIndex function');
110.     }
111.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
112.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires getFileSignature to be a function');
113.     }
114.     if (typeof getSnapshotSize !== 'function') {
115.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires estimateSnapshotSize to be a function');
116.     }
117.     if (typeof maxSnapshotBytes !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(maxSnapshotBytes) || maxSnapshotBytes <= 0) {
118.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires maxSnapshotBytes to be a positive number');
119.     }
120.     if (!manifestStore || typeof manifestStore.upsert !== 'function' || typeof manifestStore.remove !== 'function') {
121.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires manifestStore to support upsert and remove');
122.     }
123. 
124.     const snapshots = new Map();
125.     const generations = new Map();
126.     let accessSequence = 0;
127. 
128.     function getGeneration(rootCacheKey) {
129.         return generations.get(rootCacheKey) || 0;
130.     }
131. 
132.     function touchSnapshotEntry(entry) {
133.         entry.lastAccessedAt = ++accessSequence;
134.         manifestStore.upsert({
135.             rootFile: entry.snapshot.rootFile,
136.             trackedFiles: entry.trackedFiles.map(trackedFile => trackedFile.filePath),
137.             byteSize: entry.byteSize,
138.             lastAccessedAt: entry.lastAccessedAt,
139.         });
140.     }
141. 
142.     function evictSnapshotsIfNeeded(protectedRootCacheKey) {
143.         let cacheStats = getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
144.         while (cacheStats.totalSnapshotBytes > maxSnapshotBytes) {
145.             const evictionCandidates = [...snapshots.entries()]
146.                 .filter(([rootCacheKey, entry]) => (
147.                     rootCacheKey !== protectedRootCacheKey
148.                     && entry
149.                     && entry.snapshot
150.                     && typeof entry.byteSize === 'number'
151.                 ))
152.                 .sort((left, right) => left[1].lastAccessedAt - right[1].lastAccessedAt);
153. 
154.             if (evictionCandidates.length === 0) break;
155.             manifestStore.remove(evictionCandidates[0][1].snapshot.rootFile);
156.             snapshots.delete(evictionCandidates[0][0]);
157.             cacheStats = getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
158.         }
159.     }
160. 
161.     async function loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile) {
162.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
163.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
164. 
165.         while (snapshots.has(rootCacheKey)) {
166.             const cachedEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
167.             if (cachedEntry.promise) return cachedEntry.promise;
168. 
169.             const valid = await isSnapshotValid(cachedEntry, getFileSignature);
170.             const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
171.             if (currentEntry !== cachedEntry) continue;
172.             if (valid) {
173.                 touchSnapshotEntry(cachedEntry);
174.                 return cachedEntry.snapshot;
175.             }
176. 
177.             invalidate(resolvedRootFile);
178.         }
179. 
180.         const generation = getGeneration(rootCacheKey);
181.         const promise = Promise.resolve(buildProjectIndex(resolvedRootFile))
182.             .then(async snapshot => {
183.                 let trackedFiles = null;
184.                 try {
185.                     trackedFiles = await captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature);
186.                 } catch (_error) {
187.                     trackedFiles = null;
188.                 }
189. 
190.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
191.                 if (getGeneration(rootCacheKey) === generation && currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
192.                     if (trackedFiles) {
193.                         const entry = {
194.                             snapshot,
195.                             trackedFiles,
196.                             byteSize: getSnapshotSize(snapshot),
197.                         };
198.                         touchSnapshotEntry(entry);
199.                         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, entry);
200.                         evictSnapshotsIfNeeded(rootCacheKey);
201.                     } else {
202.                         manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
203.                         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
204.                     }
205.                 }
206.                 return snapshot;
207.             })
208.             .catch(error => {
209.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
210.                 if (currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
211.                     manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
212.                     snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
213.                 }
214.                 throw error;
215.             });
216. 
217.         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise });
218.         return promise;
219.     }
220. 
221.     function invalidate(rootFile) {
222.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
223.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
224.         generations.set(rootCacheKey, getGeneration(rootCacheKey) + 1);
225.         manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
226.         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
227.     }
228. 
229.     return {
230.         getCacheStats() {
231.             return getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
232.         },
233.         getManifestEntries() {
234.             return manifestStore.list();
235.         },
236.         invalidate,
237.         loadProjectSnapshot,
238.     };
239. }
240. 
241. module.exports = {
242.     createIndexClient,
243. };
244.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 897m 52s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\cache\cacheManifestStore.js.**

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\cache\cacheManifestStore.js (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>113 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const path = require('path');
4. 
5. function resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile) {
6.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
7.         throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a rootFile path');
8.     }
9.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
10. }
11. 
12. function getManifestRootKey(rootFile) {
13.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile);
14.     return process.platform === 'win32'
15.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
16.         : resolvedRootFile;
17. }
18. 
19. function normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles = []) {
20.     const normalizedFiles = [];
21.     const seen = new Set();
22. 
23.     for (const filePath of trackedFiles) {
24.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveManifestRootFile(filePath);
25.         const fileKey = getManifestRootKey(resolvedFilePath);
26.         if (seen.has(fileKey)) continue;
27.         seen.add(fileKey);
28.         normalizedFiles.push(resolvedFilePath);
29.     }
30. 
31.     return normalizedFiles;
32. }
33. 
34. function cloneManifestEntry(entry) {
35.     if (!entry) return null;
36.     return {
37.         rootFile: entry.rootFile,
38.         trackedFiles: [...entry.trackedFiles],
39.         trackedFileCount: entry.trackedFileCount,
40.         byteSize: entry.byteSize,
41.         lastAccessedAt: entry.lastAccessedAt,
42.     };
43. }
44. 
45. function createCacheManifestStore() {
46.     const entries = new Map();
47. 
48.     function upsert({ rootFile, trackedFiles, byteSize, lastAccessedAt }) {
49.         if (typeof byteSize !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(byteSize) || byteSize < 0) {
50.             throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a non-negative byteSize');
51.         }
52.         if (typeof lastAccessedAt !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(lastAccessedAt) || lastAccessedAt < 0) {
53.             throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a non-negative lastAccessedAt');
54.         }
55. 
56.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile);
57.         const normalizedTrackedFiles = normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles && trackedFiles.length > 0
58.             ? trackedFiles
59.             : [resolvedRootFile]);
60.         const entry = {
61.             rootFile: resolvedRootFile,
62.             trackedFiles: normalizedTrackedFiles,
63.             trackedFileCount: normalizedTrackedFiles.length,
64.             byteSize,
65.             lastAccessedAt,
66.         };
67. 
68.         entries.set(getManifestRootKey(resolvedRootFile), entry);
69.         return cloneManifestEntry(entry);
70.     }
71. 
72.     function get(rootFile) {
73.         return cloneManifestEntry(entries.get(getManifestRootKey(rootFile)));
74.     }
75. 
76.     function remove(rootFile) {
77.         return entries.delete(getManifestRootKey(rootFile));
78.     }
79. 
80.     function list() {
81.         return [...entries.values()]
82.             .sort((left, right) => right.lastAccessedAt - left.lastAccessedAt)
83.             .map(cloneManifestEntry);
84.     }
85. 
86.     function getStats() {
87.         let entryCount = 0;
88.         let totalBytes = 0;
89. 
90.         for (const entry of entries.values()) {
91.             entryCount += 1;
92.             totalBytes += entry.byteSize;
93.         }
94. 
95.         return {
96.             entryCount,
97.             totalBytes,
98.         };
99.     }
100. 
101.     return {
102.         get,
103.         getStats,
104.         list,
105.         remove,
106.         upsert,
107.     };
108. }
109. 
110. module.exports = {
111.     createCacheManifestStore,
112. };
113.
```

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\project\projectIndexer.js.**

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\src\core\project\projectIndexer.js (lines 1-280)

<details>
<summary>166 lines</summary>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. const includeScanner = require('../parser/includeScanner');
7. const keywordScanner = require('../parser/keywordScanner');
8. const { ProjectGraph } = require('./projectGraph');
9. 
10. function resolveProjectFile(filePath) {
11.     return path.resolve(filePath);
12. }
13. 
14. function getProjectFileCacheKey(filePath) {
15.     const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
16.     return process.platform === 'win32'
17.         ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()
18.         : resolvedFilePath;
19. }
20. 
21. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
22.     return left
23.         && right
24.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
25.         && left.size === right.size;
26. }
27. 
28. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
29.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
30.     return {
31.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
32.         size: stat.size,
33.     };
34. }
35. 
36. function resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, searchPaths) {
37.     for (const searchPath of searchPaths) {
38.         const fullPath = path.resolve(searchPath, fileName);
39.         if (fs.existsSync(fullPath)) return fullPath;
40.     }
41.     return null;
42. }
43. 
44. function addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, keywords) {
45.     for (const keywordEntry of keywords) {
46.         if (!keywordMap.has(keywordEntry.keyword)) keywordMap.set(keywordEntry.keyword, []);
47.         keywordMap.get(keywordEntry.keyword).push(keywordEntry);
48.     }
49. }
50. 
51. function createProjectIndexer({
52.     collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile = includeScanner.collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile,
53.     collectKeywordsFromFile = keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile,
54.     getFileSignature = readFileSignature,
55. } = {}) {
56.     if (typeof collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile !== 'function') {
57.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile to be a function');
58.     }
59.     if (typeof collectKeywordsFromFile !== 'function') {
60.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires collectKeywordsFromFile to be a function');
61.     }
62.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
63.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires getFileSignature to be a function');
64.     }
65. 
66.     const fileScanCache = new Map();
67. 
68.     async function loadFileScan(filePath, stats) {
69.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
70.         const fileCacheKey = getProjectFileCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
71.         const signature = await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath);
72.         const cachedEntry = fileScanCache.get(fileCacheKey);
73. 
74.         if (cachedEntry && areFileSignaturesEqual(cachedEntry.signature, signature)) {
75.             stats.reusedFileCount += 1;
76.             return cachedEntry.scanResult;
77.         }
78. 
79.         const keywords = await collectKeywordsFromFile(resolvedFilePath);
80.         const { includeEntries, searchPaths } = await collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(resolvedFilePath);
81.         const scanResult = {
82.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
83.             keywords,
84.             includeEntries,
85.             searchPaths,
86.         };
87. 
88.         fileScanCache.set(fileCacheKey, {
89.             signature,
90.             scanResult,
91.         });
92.         stats.scannedFileCount += 1;
93.         return scanResult;
94.     }
95. 
96.     async function buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
97.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveProjectFile(rootFile);
98.         const files = [];
99.         const keywordMap = new Map();
100.         const graph = new ProjectGraph();
101.         const visited = new Set();
102.         const stats = {
103.             scannedFileCount: 0,
104.             reusedFileCount: 0,
105.         };
106. 
107.         async function visit(filePath, ancestry = []) {
108.             const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
109.             if (visited.has(resolvedFilePath)) return;
110.             visited.add(resolvedFilePath);
111.             files.push(resolvedFilePath);
112.             graph.addFile(resolvedFilePath);
113. 
114.             const scanResult = await loadFileScan(resolvedFilePath, stats);
115.             addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, scanResult.keywords);
116. 
117.             for (const { fileName } of scanResult.includeEntries) {
118.                 const resolvedPath = resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, scanResult.searchPaths);
119.                 if (!resolvedPath) {
120.                     graph.addMissingFile({
121.                         fromFile: resolvedFilePath,
122.                         fileName,
123.                         filePath: path.resolve(scanResult.searchPaths[0] || path.dirname(resolvedFilePath), fileName),
124.                     });
125.                     continue;
126.                 }
127. 
128.                 if (ancestry.includes(resolvedPath) || resolvedPath === resolvedFilePath) {
129.                     graph.addCycle({
130.                         fromFile: resolvedFilePath,
131.                         toFile: resolvedPath,
132.                         path: [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath],
133.                     });
134.                     continue;
135.                 }
136. 
137.                 graph.addIncludeEdge(resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath);
138.                 await visit(resolvedPath, [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath]);
139.             }
140.         }
141. 
142.         await visit(resolvedRootFile);
143. 
144.         return {
145.             rootFile: resolvedRootFile,
146.             files,
147.             graph,
148.             keywordMap,
149.             missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
150.             cycles: graph.cycles,
151.             stats,
152.         };
153.     }
154. 
155.     return {
156.         buildProjectIndex,
157.     };
158. }
159. 
160. const defaultProjectIndexer = createProjectIndexer();
161. 
162. module.exports = {
163.     buildProjectIndex: defaultProjectIndexer.buildProjectIndex,
164.     createProjectIndexer,
165. };
166.
```

</details>

```
331. ## Phase 4：磁盘缓存与空间治理
332. 
333. **目标：** 在 `globalStorageUri` 下建立受控的 L2 缓存，同时解决磁盘膨胀问题。
334. 
335. **涉及文件：**
336. - Create: `src/core/cache/sqliteCacheStore.js`
337. - Modify: `src/core/cache/cacheManifestStore.js`
338. - Modify: `package.json`
339. - Modify: `README.md`
340. 
341. - [ ] 第一优先级是定义缓存目录结构、配额、清理机制
342. - [ ] 若保持轻量，可先使用“manifest + 二进制/JSON 快照文件”方案
343. - [ ] 若热启动性能和检索能力不足，再接入 `better-sqlite3`
344. - [ ] 无论是否使用 SQLite，都必须具备：
345.   - 最大缓存空间上限
346.   - 最近最少使用（LRU）逐出
347.   - TTL 清理
348.   - 崩溃后可恢复的清单文件
349. - [ ] 若引入 SQLite，再补齐：
350.   - schema version
351.   - `auto_vacuum`
352.   - vacuum 触发策略
353.   - 损坏数据库回退机制
354. 
355. **阶段产物：**
356. - 工程关闭再打开时可热启动
357. - 缓存空间可控
358. - 缓存损坏不影响主功能
359. 
360. **阶段验收：**
361. - 在缓存命中情况下，可直接恢复工程级索引
362. - 缓存超过阈值时，会自动逐出旧工程数据
363. - 删除缓存后系统仍可正常重建
364. 
365. ## Phase 5：文件级增量更新与 Watcher 驱动刷新
366. 
367. **目标：** 将“只重扫变化文件”从手动触发扩展到工作区文件变更与激活文档编辑。
368. 
369. **涉及文件：**
370. - Create: `src/server/fileWatcher.js`（若此阶段尚未上 LSP，可先放 `src/core/incremental`）
371. - Create: `src/core/incremental/blockIndex.js`
372. - Modify: `src/core/project/projectIndexer.js`
373. - Modify: `src/client/services/indexClient.js`
374. - Modify: `src/extension.js`
375. 
376. - [ ] 接入 `workspace.createFileSystemWatcher` 监听 `.k/.key/.dyna`
377. - [ ] 为每个文件记录反向依赖，文件变化时只刷新受影响分支
378. - [ ] 激活文档变更时，先做 debounce，再决定是否触发局部重扫
379. - [ ] 第一版增量更新做到“文件级”即可
380. - [ ] 对于超大已打开文档，优先保证“后台重建索引”，不要在主线程做复杂实时语义
381. - [ ] `blockIndex` 作为下一步块级增量的预埋结构，先记录关键字块的行区间
382. 
383. **阶段产物：**
384. - 文件系统变更可自动使缓存失效
385. - 项目索引具备反向依赖驱动刷新
386. - 为后续 Interval Tree / 块级补丁保留数据入口
387. 
388. **阶段验收：**
389. - 被 include 的子文件变化后，上层工程索引会自动刷新
390. - 未受影响的文件结果继续复用
391. - 高频保存不会导致重复排队扫描失控
392. 
393. ## Phase 6：LSP 化迁移（重扫描与索引服务下沉）
394. 
395. **目标：** 当 Worker MVP 稳定后，将重能力迁移到独立 Server 进程，彻底隔离 Extension Host。
396. 
397. **涉及文件：**
398. - Create: `src/server/server.js`
399. - Create: `src/server/sessionManager.js`
400. - Create: `src/server/requestRouter.js`
401. - Create: `src/shared/protocol.js`
402. - Modify: `package.json`
403. - Modify: `src/client/services/indexClient.js`
404. - Modify: `src/extension.js`
405. 
406. - [ ] 引入 `vscode-languageclient` 与 `vscode-languageserver`
407. - [ ] 将扫描请求、缓存请求、索引请求定义为明确协议
408. - [ ] 客户端只保留命令/UI/状态同步
409. - [ ] 服务端承载：
410.   - 工程级索引状态
411.   - Worker 调度
412.   - 磁盘缓存访问
413.   - 文件变更响应
414. - [ ] 先迁移 Include Tree / Keyword Index
415. - [ ] hover、diagnostics、rename 等当前轻能力可暂时留在 Extension Host，避免迁移面过大
416. 
417. **阶段产物：**
418. - Extension Host 基本不做重扫描
419. - 后台服务可独立维护工程索引生命周期
420. - 后续继续迁移语义能力时边界清晰
421. 
422. **阶段验收：**
423. - 大工程扫描时，Extension Host 不再承担 CPU 密集型递归逻辑
424. - 重启扩展或重新打开工作区时，服务端能恢复缓存状态
425. - 客户端与服务端协议版本不一致时有明确失败提示
426. 
427. ## Phase 7：块级增量、聚合渲染与最终收口
428. 
429. **目标：** 在前述架构稳定后，补齐 `large_file_plan.md` 中真正有价值、且已被数据证明值得实现的高级能力。
430.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 897m 56s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_execution_record_phase3_cache_manifest_store.md.**

D:\Project\vscode-lsdyna\.github\doc\large_file_execution_record_phase3_cache_manifest_store.md (lines 1-220)

<details>
<summary>220 lines</summary>

````
1. # vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 3：cache manifest store）
2. 
3. ## 1. 当前进度定位
4. 
5. 到本轮开始时，Phase 3 已经完成：
6. 
7. 1. `indexClient` L1 项目快照缓存
8. 2. 显式失效与 stale in-flight 防污染
9. 3. 自动过期校验
10. 4. `projectIndexer` 单文件扫描复用
11. 5. 缓存大小统计与 LRU 逐出
12. 
13. 但总体计划里还有一项尚未显式收口：
14. 
15. > **建立工程清单（manifest），记录每个工程包含哪些文件、最后访问时间、摘要大小。**
16. 
17. 此前这些信息虽然已经散落在 `indexClient` 的缓存条目里，但还没有形成一个清晰、稳定、可复用的模块边界。
18. 
19. 因此本轮聚焦：
20. 
21. **引入 `cacheManifestStore`，把项目快照缓存的工程级元数据正式建模并接入 `indexClient`。**
22. 
23. ---
24. 
25. ## 2. 本轮目标
26. 
27. 本轮只做 manifest 的最小可用版：
28. 
29. 1. 新增 `src/core/cache/cacheManifestStore.js`
30. 2. 让 manifest 记录：
31.    - `rootFile`
32.    - `trackedFiles`
33.    - `trackedFileCount`
34.    - `byteSize`
35.    - `lastAccessedAt`
36. 3. 让 `indexClient` 在以下时机维护 manifest：
37.    - 快照写入
38.    - 快照命中（更新时间戳）
39.    - 手动失效
40.    - LRU 逐出
41. 4. 增加独立单测和联动测试
42. 
43. 本轮**不做**：
44. 
45. - 磁盘持久化
46. - JSON/二进制序列化文件
47. - manifest 崩溃恢复
48. - watcher 自动消费 manifest
49. 
50. ---
51. 
52. ## 3. 设计选择
53. 
54. ## 3.1 先做“内存 manifest store”，不提前碰磁盘
55. 
56. 总体计划里 manifest 最终会服务于更远一点的目标：
57. 
58. 1. L2 磁盘缓存
59. 2. watcher 驱动失效
60. 3. 后台服务恢复
61. 
62. 但本轮没有直接跳到磁盘持久化，而是先建立一个**内存态、接口稳定的 manifest store**。
63. 
64. 这样做的好处是：
65. 
66. 1. 先把数据模型和调用边界做稳
67. 2. 后续要不要落盘，只需要替换 store 背后的存储方式
68. 3. 当前风险更低，不会把 Phase 3 和 Phase 4 混在一起
69. 
70. ## 3.2 manifest 只存“工程级元数据”，不直接存快照 payload
71. 
72. manifest store 当前只负责记录：
73. 
74. - 这个工程是谁
75. - 它跟踪了哪些文件
76. - 最后访问时间是多少
77. - 估算大小是多少
78. 
79. 而不负责存：
80. 
81. - `ProjectGraph`
82. - `keywordMap`
83. - `missingFiles`
84. 
85. 这让 manifest 保持轻量，也更适合作为后续缓存治理、恢复和观察的辅助层。
86. 
87. ---
88. 
89. ## 4. TDD 过程
90. 
91. ### 4.1 先写失败测试
92. 
93. 本轮先新增两类测试：
94. 
95. #### A. `test/core/cache/cacheManifestStore.test.js`
96. 
97. 约束 `createCacheManifestStore()` 必须：
98. 
99. 1. 对 `rootFile` 做别名归一化
100. 2. 读取时支持别名路径命中同一条 manifest 记录
101. 3. `list()` 按最近访问顺序返回
102. 4. `getStats()` 能聚合：
103.    - `entryCount`
104.    - `totalBytes`
105. 
106. #### B. `test/client/services/indexClient.test.js`
107. 
108. 约束 `indexClient` 必须在以下行为中同步更新 manifest：
109. 
110. 1. 首次缓存快照时写入 manifest
111. 2. 再次命中缓存时刷新 `lastAccessedAt`
112. 3. 调用 `invalidate(rootFile)` 时移除 manifest 条目
113. 
114. 先执行聚焦测试：
115. 
116. ```powershell
117. npx mocha test\core\cache\cacheManifestStore.test.js test\client\services\indexClient.test.js --grep "manifest|createCacheManifestStore"
118. ```
119. 
120. 结果先红，失败原因准确：
121. 
122. - `cacheManifestStore` 模块不存在
123. 
124. ### 4.2 再写最小实现
125. 
126. 本轮新增：
127. 
128. - `createCacheManifestStore()`
129. 
130. 支持：
131. 
132. 1. `upsert(...)`
133. 2. `get(rootFile)`
134. 3. `remove(rootFile)`
135. 4. `list()`
136. 5. `getStats()`
137. 
138. 并在 `indexClient` 中接入：
139. 
140. 1. 默认创建一个内部 manifest store
141. 2. 允许通过 `manifestStore` 注入自定义实例，便于测试与未来扩展
142. 3. `touchSnapshotEntry()` 更新 LRU 顺序时同步更新 manifest
143. 4. `invalidate()` / 逐出 / 失败清理时同步删除 manifest 记录
144. 
145. ### 4.3 验证转绿
146. 
147. 聚焦测试转绿后，再把整组 `indexClient` 测试重新跑完，全部通过。
148. 
149. 说明：
150. 
151. 1. manifest 行为本身已成立
152. 2. 旧有缓存/失效/逐出逻辑没有被带坏
153. 3. 后续代码审查中提出的“**命中缓存校验途中被 invalidate，可能把 stale manifest 写回**”这一竞态，经补充回归测试后确认当前实现已经被 `currentEntry !== cachedEntry` 身份检查正确拦住，没有出现 stale manifest 回写
154. 
155. ---
156. 
157. ## 5. 实际代码改动
158. 
159. ## 5.1 `src/core/cache/cacheManifestStore.js`
160. 
161. 本轮新增了一个最小 manifest store，内部维护：
162. 
163. - 规范化 key
164. - 去重后的 `trackedFiles`
165. - 派生的 `trackedFileCount`
166. 
167. 并且对外只暴露清晰的小接口：
168. 
169. 1. `upsert`
170. 2. `get`
171. 3. `remove`
172. 4. `list`
173. 5. `getStats`
174. 
175. ## 5.2 `src/client/services/indexClient.js`
176. 
177. 本轮把 manifest 正式接入到 L1 缓存生命周期里：
178. 
179. 1. 快照首次写入时写 manifest
180. 2. 快照被命中时刷新 `lastAccessedAt`
181. 3. 被逐出时清掉 manifest
182. 4. 手动失效时清掉 manifest
183. 
184. 从而让 manifest 真正成为缓存层的一部分，而不是一个孤立的工具模块。
185. 
186. ## 5.3 测试文件
187. 
188. 新增：
189. 
190. - `test/core/cache/cacheManifestStore.test.js`
191. 
192. 扩展：
193. 
194. - `test/client/services/indexClient.test.js`
195. 
196. 其中新增了一条并发回归测试，专门钉住：
197. 
198. - 缓存命中校验进行中
199. - 外部同时 `invalidate(rootFile)`
200. - 当前请求必须重建，且不能把旧 manifest 条目写回
201. 
202. ---
203. 
204. ## 6. 真实工程样本验证
205. 
206. 本轮继续使用用户提供的真实工程：
207. 
208. - 根文件：`D:\temp\LSDYNA\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\combine.key`
209. 
210. 通过注入自定义 `manifestStore` 后，加载一次真实工程快照，得到：
211. 
212. ```json
213. {
214.   "manifestEntry": {
215.     "rootFile": "D:\\temp\\LSDYNA\\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\\combine.key",
216.     "trackedFiles": [
217.       "D:\\temp\\LSDYNA\\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\\combine.key",
218.       "D:\\temp\\LSDYNA\\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\\rogue-v3.key",

220.       "D:\\temp\\LSDYNA\\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\\wall.key"
````

</details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const path = require('path');
4. 
5. function resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile) {
6.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
7.         throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a rootFile path');
8.     }
9.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
10. }
11. 
12. function getManifestRootKey(rootFile) {
13.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile);
14.     return process.platform === 'win32'
15.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
16.         : resolvedRootFile;
17. }
18. 
19. function normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles = []) {
20.     const normalizedFiles = [];
21.     const seen = new Set();
22. 
23.     for (const filePath of trackedFiles) {
24.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveManifestRootFile(filePath);
25.         const fileKey = getManifestRootKey(resolvedFilePath);
26.         if (seen.has(fileKey)) continue;
27.         seen.add(fileKey);
28.         normalizedFiles.push(resolvedFilePath);
29.     }
30. 
31.     return normalizedFiles;
32. }
33. 
34. function cloneManifestEntry(entry) {
35.     if (!entry) return null;
36.     return {
37.         rootFile: entry.rootFile,
38.         trackedFiles: [...entry.trackedFiles],
39.         trackedFileCount: entry.trackedFileCount,
40.         byteSize: entry.byteSize,
41.         lastAccessedAt: entry.lastAccessedAt,
42.     };
43. }
44. 
45. function createCacheManifestStore() {
46.     const entries = new Map();
47. 
48.     function upsert({ rootFile, trackedFiles, byteSize, lastAccessedAt }) {
49.         if (typeof byteSize !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(byteSize) || byteSize < 0) {
50.             throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a non-negative byteSize');
51.         }
52.         if (typeof lastAccessedAt !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(lastAccessedAt) || lastAccessedAt < 0) {
53.             throw new TypeError('cache manifest entries require a non-negative lastAccessedAt');
54.         }
55. 
56.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveManifestRootFile(rootFile);
57.         const normalizedTrackedFiles = normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles && trackedFiles.length > 0
58.             ? trackedFiles
59.             : [resolvedRootFile]);
60.         const entry = {
61.             rootFile: resolvedRootFile,
62.             trackedFiles: normalizedTrackedFiles,
63.             trackedFileCount: normalizedTrackedFiles.length,
64.             byteSize,
65.             lastAccessedAt,
66.         };
67. 
68.         entries.set(getManifestRootKey(resolvedRootFile), entry);
69.         return cloneManifestEntry(entry);
70.     }
71. 
72.     function get(rootFile) {
73.         return cloneManifestEntry(entries.get(getManifestRootKey(rootFile)));
74.     }
75. 
76.     function remove(rootFile) {
77.         return entries.delete(getManifestRootKey(rootFile));
78.     }
79. 
80.     function list() {
81.         return [...entries.values()]
82.             .sort((left, right) => right.lastAccessedAt - left.lastAccessedAt)
83.             .map(cloneManifestEntry);
84.     }
85. 
86.     function getStats() {
87.         let entryCount = 0;
88.         let totalBytes = 0;
89. 
90.         for (const entry of entries.values()) {
91.             entryCount += 1;
92.             totalBytes += entry.byteSize;
93.         }
94. 
95.         return {
96.             entryCount,
97.             totalBytes,
98.         };
99.     }
100. 
101.     return {
102.         get,
103.         getStats,
104.         list,
105.         remove,
106.         upsert,
107.     };
108. }
109. 
110. module.exports = {
111.     createCacheManifestStore,
112. };
113.
```

</details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. const includeScanner = require('../parser/includeScanner');
7. const keywordScanner = require('../parser/keywordScanner');
8. const { ProjectGraph } = require('./projectGraph');
9. 
10. function resolveProjectFile(filePath) {
11.     return path.resolve(filePath);
12. }
13. 
14. function getProjectFileCacheKey(filePath) {
15.     const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
16.     return process.platform === 'win32'
17.         ? resolvedFilePath.toLowerCase()
18.         : resolvedFilePath;
19. }
20. 
21. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
22.     return left
23.         && right
24.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
25.         && left.size === right.size;
26. }
27. 
28. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
29.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
30.     return {
31.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
32.         size: stat.size,
33.     };
34. }
35. 
36. function resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, searchPaths) {
37.     for (const searchPath of searchPaths) {
38.         const fullPath = path.resolve(searchPath, fileName);
39.         if (fs.existsSync(fullPath)) return fullPath;
40.     }
41.     return null;
42. }
43. 
44. function addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, keywords) {
45.     for (const keywordEntry of keywords) {
46.         if (!keywordMap.has(keywordEntry.keyword)) keywordMap.set(keywordEntry.keyword, []);
47.         keywordMap.get(keywordEntry.keyword).push(keywordEntry);
48.     }
49. }
50. 
51. function createProjectIndexer({
52.     collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile = includeScanner.collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile,
53.     collectKeywordsFromFile = keywordScanner.collectKeywordsFromFile,
54.     getFileSignature = readFileSignature,
55. } = {}) {
56.     if (typeof collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile !== 'function') {
57.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile to be a function');
58.     }
59.     if (typeof collectKeywordsFromFile !== 'function') {
60.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires collectKeywordsFromFile to be a function');
61.     }
62.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
63.         throw new TypeError('createProjectIndexer requires getFileSignature to be a function');
64.     }
65. 
66.     const fileScanCache = new Map();
67. 
68.     async function loadFileScan(filePath, stats) {
69.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
70.         const fileCacheKey = getProjectFileCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
71.         const signature = await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath);
72.         const cachedEntry = fileScanCache.get(fileCacheKey);
73. 
74.         if (cachedEntry && areFileSignaturesEqual(cachedEntry.signature, signature)) {
75.             stats.reusedFileCount += 1;
76.             return cachedEntry.scanResult;
77.         }
78. 
79.         const keywords = await collectKeywordsFromFile(resolvedFilePath);
80.         const { includeEntries, searchPaths } = await collectIncludeDirectivesFromFile(resolvedFilePath);
81.         const scanResult = {
82.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
83.             keywords,
84.             includeEntries,
85.             searchPaths,
86.         };
87. 
88.         fileScanCache.set(fileCacheKey, {
89.             signature,
90.             scanResult,
91.         });
92.         stats.scannedFileCount += 1;
93.         return scanResult;
94.     }
95. 
96.     async function buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
97.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveProjectFile(rootFile);
98.         const files = [];
99.         const keywordMap = new Map();
100.         const graph = new ProjectGraph();
101.         const visited = new Set();
102.         const stats = {
103.             scannedFileCount: 0,
104.             reusedFileCount: 0,
105.         };
106. 
107.         async function visit(filePath, ancestry = []) {
108.             const resolvedFilePath = resolveProjectFile(filePath);
109.             if (visited.has(resolvedFilePath)) return;
110.             visited.add(resolvedFilePath);
111.             files.push(resolvedFilePath);
112.             graph.addFile(resolvedFilePath);
113. 
114.             const scanResult = await loadFileScan(resolvedFilePath, stats);
115.             addKeywordUsages(keywordMap, scanResult.keywords);
116. 
117.             for (const { fileName } of scanResult.includeEntries) {
118.                 const resolvedPath = resolveIncludeFromSearchPaths(fileName, scanResult.searchPaths);
119.                 if (!resolvedPath) {
120.                     graph.addMissingFile({
121.                         fromFile: resolvedFilePath,
122.                         fileName,
123.                         filePath: path.resolve(scanResult.searchPaths[0] || path.dirname(resolvedFilePath), fileName),
124.                     });
125.                     continue;
126.                 }
127. 
128.                 if (ancestry.includes(resolvedPath) || resolvedPath === resolvedFilePath) {
129.                     graph.addCycle({
130.                         fromFile: resolvedFilePath,
131.                         toFile: resolvedPath,
132.                         path: [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath],
133.                     });
134.                     continue;
135.                 }
136. 
137.                 graph.addIncludeEdge(resolvedFilePath, resolvedPath);
138.                 await visit(resolvedPath, [...ancestry, resolvedFilePath]);
139.             }
140.         }
141. 
142.         await visit(resolvedRootFile);
143. 
144.         return {
145.             rootFile: resolvedRootFile,
146.             files,
147.             graph,
148.             keywordMap,
149.             missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
150.             cycles: graph.cycles,
151.             stats,
152.         };
153.     }
154. 
155.     return {
156.         buildProjectIndex,
157.     };
158. }
159. 
160. const defaultProjectIndexer = createProjectIndexer();
161. 
162. module.exports = {
163.     buildProjectIndex: defaultProjectIndexer.buildProjectIndex,
164.     createProjectIndexer,
165. };
166.
```

</details>

1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. const { createCacheManifestStore } = require('../../core/cache/cacheManifestStore');
7. 
8. function resolveRootFile(rootFile) {
9.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
10.         throw new TypeError('loadProjectSnapshot requires a rootFile path');
11.     }
12.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
13. }
14. 
15. function getRootCacheKey(rootFile) {
16.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
17.     return process.platform === 'win32'
18.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
19.         : resolvedRootFile;
20. }
21. 
22. function getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot) {
23.     if (Array.isArray(snapshot.files) && snapshot.files.length > 0) {
24.         return snapshot.files;
25.     }
26.     return [snapshot.rootFile];
27. }
28. 
29. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
30.     return left
31.         && right
32.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
33.         && left.size === right.size;
34. }
35. 
36. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
37.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
38.     return {
39.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
40.         size: stat.size,
41.     };
42. }
43. 
44. async function captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature) {
45.     const trackedFiles = [];
46.     const seen = new Set();
47. 
48.     for (const filePath of getTrackedSnapshotFiles(snapshot)) {
49.         const resolvedFilePath = resolveRootFile(filePath);
50.         const fileCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(resolvedFilePath);
51.         if (seen.has(fileCacheKey)) continue;
52.         seen.add(fileCacheKey);
53.         trackedFiles.push({
54.             filePath: resolvedFilePath,
55.             signature: await getFileSignature(resolvedFilePath),
56.         });
57.     }
58. 
59.     return trackedFiles;
60. }
61. 
62. async function isSnapshotValid(entry, getFileSignature) {
63.     if (!Array.isArray(entry.trackedFiles) || entry.trackedFiles.length === 0) {
64.         return false;
65.     }
66. 
67.     for (const trackedFile of entry.trackedFiles) {
68.         try {
69.             const currentSignature = await getFileSignature(trackedFile.filePath);
70.             if (!areFileSignaturesEqual(currentSignature, trackedFile.signature)) {
71.                 return false;
72.             }
73.         } catch (_error) {
74.             return false;
75.         }
76.     }
77. 
78.     return true;
79. }
80. 
81. function estimateSnapshotSize(snapshot) {
82.     return Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(snapshot), 'utf8');
83. }
84. 
85. function getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots) {
86.     let cachedSnapshotCount = 0;
87.     let totalSnapshotBytes = 0;
88. 
89.     for (const entry of snapshots.values()) {
90.         if (!entry || !entry.snapshot || typeof entry.byteSize !== 'number') continue;
91.         cachedSnapshotCount += 1;
92.         totalSnapshotBytes += entry.byteSize;
93.     }
94. 
95.     return {
96.         cachedSnapshotCount,
97.         totalSnapshotBytes,
98.     };
99. }
100. 
101. function createIndexClient({
102.     buildProjectIndex,
103.     getFileSignature = readFileSignature,
104.     estimateSnapshotSize: getSnapshotSize = estimateSnapshotSize,
105.     maxSnapshotBytes = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY,
106.     manifestStore = createCacheManifestStore(),
107. } = {}) {
108.     if (typeof buildProjectIndex !== 'function') {
109.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires a buildProjectIndex function');
110.     }
111.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
112.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires getFileSignature to be a function');
113.     }
114.     if (typeof getSnapshotSize !== 'function') {
115.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires estimateSnapshotSize to be a function');
116.     }
117.     if (typeof maxSnapshotBytes !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(maxSnapshotBytes) || maxSnapshotBytes <= 0) {
118.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires maxSnapshotBytes to be a positive number');
119.     }
120.     if (!manifestStore || typeof manifestStore.upsert !== 'function' || typeof manifestStore.remove !== 'function') {
121.         throw new TypeError('createIndexClient requires manifestStore to support upsert and remove');
122.     }
123. 
124.     const snapshots = new Map();
125.     const generations = new Map();
126.     let accessSequence = 0;
127. 
128.     function getGeneration(rootCacheKey) {
129.         return generations.get(rootCacheKey) || 0;
130.     }
131. 
132.     function touchSnapshotEntry(entry) {
133.         entry.lastAccessedAt = ++accessSequence;
134.         manifestStore.upsert({
135.             rootFile: entry.snapshot.rootFile,
136.             trackedFiles: entry.trackedFiles.map(trackedFile => trackedFile.filePath),
137.             byteSize: entry.byteSize,
138.             lastAccessedAt: entry.lastAccessedAt,
139.         });
140.     }
141. 
142.     function evictSnapshotsIfNeeded(protectedRootCacheKey) {
143.         let cacheStats = getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
144.         while (cacheStats.totalSnapshotBytes > maxSnapshotBytes) {
145.             const evictionCandidates = [...snapshots.entries()]
146.                 .filter(([rootCacheKey, entry]) => (
147.                     rootCacheKey !== protectedRootCacheKey
148.                     && entry
149.                     && entry.snapshot
150.                     && typeof entry.byteSize === 'number'
151.                 ))
152.                 .sort((left, right) => left[1].lastAccessedAt - right[1].lastAccessedAt);
153. 
154.             if (evictionCandidates.length === 0) break;
155.             manifestStore.remove(evictionCandidates[0][1].snapshot.rootFile);
156.             snapshots.delete(evictionCandidates[0][0]);
157.             cacheStats = getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
158.         }
159.     }
160. 
161.     async function loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile) {
162.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
163.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
164. 
165.         while (snapshots.has(rootCacheKey)) {
166.             const cachedEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
167.             if (cachedEntry.promise) return cachedEntry.promise;
168. 
169.             const valid = await isSnapshotValid(cachedEntry, getFileSignature);
170.             const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
171.             if (currentEntry !== cachedEntry) continue;
172.             if (valid) {
173.                 touchSnapshotEntry(cachedEntry);
174.                 return cachedEntry.snapshot;
175.             }
176. 
177.             invalidate(resolvedRootFile);
178.         }
179. 
180.         const generation = getGeneration(rootCacheKey);
181.         const promise = Promise.resolve(buildProjectIndex(resolvedRootFile))
182.             .then(async snapshot => {
183.                 let trackedFiles = null;
184.                 try {
185.                     trackedFiles = await captureTrackedFiles(snapshot, getFileSignature);
186.                 } catch (_error) {
187.                     trackedFiles = null;
188.                 }
189. 
190.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
191.                 if (getGeneration(rootCacheKey) === generation && currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
192.                     if (trackedFiles) {
193.                         const entry = {
194.                             snapshot,
195.                             trackedFiles,
196.                             byteSize: getSnapshotSize(snapshot),
197.                         };
198.                         touchSnapshotEntry(entry);
199.                         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, entry);
200.                         evictSnapshotsIfNeeded(rootCacheKey);
201.                     } else {
202.                         manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
203.                         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
204.                     }
205.                 }
206.                 return snapshot;
207.             })
208.             .catch(error => {
209.                 const currentEntry = snapshots.get(rootCacheKey);
210.                 if (currentEntry && currentEntry.promise === promise) {
211.                     manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
212.                     snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
213.                 }
214.                 throw error;
215.             });
216. 
217.         snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise });
218.         return promise;
219.     }
220. 
221.     function invalidate(rootFile) {
222.         const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
223.         const rootCacheKey = getRootCacheKey(rootFile);
224.         generations.set(rootCacheKey, getGeneration(rootCacheKey) + 1);
225.         manifestStore.remove(resolvedRootFile);
226.         snapshots.delete(rootCacheKey);
227.     }
228. 
229.     return {
230.         getCacheStats() {
231.             return getSnapshotCacheStats(snapshots);
232.         },
233.         getManifestEntries() {
234.             return manifestStore.list();
235.         },
236.         invalidate,
237.         loadProjectSnapshot,
238.     };
239. }
240. 
241. module.exports = {
242.     createIndexClient,
243. };
244.
```

</details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. class ProjectGraph {
4.     constructor() {
5.         this.children = new Map();
6.         this.includeEntries = new Map();
7.         this.parents = new Map();
8.         this.missingFiles = [];
9.         this.cycles = [];
10.     }
11. 
12.     addFile(filePath) {
13.         if (!this.children.has(filePath)) this.children.set(filePath, []);
14.         if (!this.includeEntries.has(filePath)) this.includeEntries.set(filePath, []);
15.         if (!this.parents.has(filePath)) this.parents.set(filePath, []);
16.     }
17. 
18.     addIncludeEntry(fromFile, entry) {
19.         this.addFile(fromFile);
20.         const entries = this.includeEntries.get(fromFile);
21.         if (entry.missing) {
22.             entries.push(entry);
23.             return;
24.         }
25.         const exists = entries.some(candidate =>
26.             candidate.filePath === entry.filePath && Boolean(candidate.missing) === Boolean(entry.missing)
27.         );
28.         if (!exists) {
29.             entries.push(entry);
30.         }
31.     }
32. 
33.     addIncludeEdge(fromFile, toFile) {
34.         this.addFile(fromFile);
35.         this.addFile(toFile);
36. 
37.         if (!this.children.get(fromFile).includes(toFile)) {
38.             this.children.get(fromFile).push(toFile);
39.         }
40.         if (!this.parents.get(toFile).includes(fromFile)) {
41.             this.parents.get(toFile).push(fromFile);
42.         }
43. 
44.         this.addIncludeEntry(fromFile, { filePath: toFile });
45.     }
46. 
47.     addMissingFile(record) {
48.         this.missingFiles.push(record);
49.         if (record.fromFile && record.filePath) {
50.             this.addIncludeEntry(record.fromFile, {
51.                 filePath: record.filePath,
52.                 fileName: record.fileName,
53.                 missing: true,
54.             });
55.         }
56.     }
57. 
58.     addCycle(record) {
59.         this.cycles.push(record);
60.     }
61. 
62.     getChildren(filePath) {
63.         return [...(this.children.get(filePath) || [])];
64.     }
65. 
66.     getParents(filePath) {
67.         return [...(this.parents.get(filePath) || [])];
68.     }
69. 
70.     getIncludeEntries(filePath) {
71.         return [...(this.includeEntries.get(filePath) || [])];
72.     }
73. 
74.     toTree(rootFile, ancestry = []) {
75.         if (ancestry.includes(rootFile)) {
76.             return {
77.                 filePath: rootFile,
78.                 children: [],
79.             };
80.         }
81. 
82.         return {
83.             filePath: rootFile,
84.             children: this.getIncludeEntries(rootFile).map(entry => (
85.                 entry.missing
86.                     ? { ...entry, children: [] }
87.                     : this.toTree(entry.filePath, [...ancestry, rootFile])
88.             )),
89.         };
90.     }
91. 
92.     toJSON() {
93.         return {
94.             children: [...this.children.entries()].map(([filePath, childFiles]) => [filePath, [...childFiles]]),
95.             includeEntries: [...this.includeEntries.entries()].map(([filePath, entries]) => [filePath, [...entries]]),
96.             parents: [...this.parents.entries()].map(([filePath, parentFiles]) => [filePath, [...parentFiles]]),
97.             missingFiles: [...this.missingFiles],
98.             cycles: [...this.cycles],
99.         };
100.     }
101. 
102.     static fromJSON(data = {}) {
103.         const graph = new ProjectGraph();
104.         graph.children = new Map(data.children || []);
105.         graph.includeEntries = new Map(data.includeEntries || []);
106.         graph.parents = new Map(data.parents || []);
107.         graph.missingFiles = [...(data.missingFiles || [])];
108.         graph.cycles = [...(data.cycles || [])];
109.         return graph;
110.     }
111. }
112. 
113. module.exports = {
114.     ProjectGraph,
115. };
116.
```

1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const { parentPort } = require('worker_threads');
4. 
5. const { buildProjectIndex } = require('../core/project/projectIndexer');
6. 
7. function serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
8.     return {
9.         ...snapshot,
10.         graph: snapshot.graph.toJSON(),
11.         keywordMap: [...snapshot.keywordMap.entries()],
12.         missingFiles: [...snapshot.missingFiles],
13.         cycles: [...snapshot.cycles],
14.     };
15. }
16. 
17. function serializeError(error) {
18.     return {
19.         message: error.message,
20.         stack: error.stack,
21.         name: error.name,
22.     };
23. }
24. 
25. parentPort.on('message', async (message) => {
26.     if (!message || message.type !== 'buildProjectIndex') return;
27. 
28.     try {
29.         const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(message.rootFile);
30.         parentPort.postMessage({
31.             requestId: message.requestId,
32.             snapshot: serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot),
33.             type: 'result',
34.         });
35.     } catch (error) {
36.         parentPort.postMessage({
37.             error: serializeError(error),
38.             requestId: message.requestId,
39.             type: 'error',
40.         });
41.     }
42. });
43.
```
