```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const { Worker } = require('worker_threads');
4. 
5. const { ProjectGraph } = require('../core/project/projectGraph');
6. 
7. function createWorkerPool({
8.     workerPath,
9.     workerFactory = (nextWorkerPath) => new Worker(nextWorkerPath),
10. } = {}) {
11.     if (typeof workerPath !== 'string' || workerPath.trim() === '') {
12.         throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerPath');
13.     }
14.     if (typeof workerFactory !== 'function') {
15.         throw new TypeError('createWorkerPool requires a workerFactory function');
16.     }
17. 
18.     const worker = workerFactory(workerPath);
19.     const pendingRequests = new Map();
20.     let nextRequestId = 1;
21.     let disposed = false;
22. 
23.     function hydrateProjectSnapshot(snapshot) {
24.         const graph = ProjectGraph.fromJSON(snapshot.graph);
25.         return {
26.             ...snapshot,
27.             graph,
28.             keywordMap: new Map(snapshot.keywordMap || []),
29.             missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,
30.             cycles: graph.cycles,
31.         };
32.     }
33. 
34.     function rejectPendingRequests(error) {
35.         for (const pendingRequest of pendingRequests.values()) {
36.             pendingRequest.reject(error);
37.         }
38.         pendingRequests.clear();
39.     }
40. 
41.     worker.on('message', (message) => {
42.         const pendingRequest = pendingRequests.get(message.requestId);
43.         if (!pendingRequest) return;
44.         pendingRequests.delete(message.requestId);
45. 
46.         if (message.type === 'error') {
47.             const error = new Error(message.error.message);
48.             error.name = message.error.name;
49.             error.stack = message.error.stack;
50.             pendingRequest.reject(error);
51.             return;
52.         }
53. 
54.         pendingRequest.resolve(hydrateProjectSnapshot(message.snapshot));
55.     });
56.     worker.on('error', (error) => {
57.         disposed = true;
58.         rejectPendingRequests(error);
59.     });
60.     worker.on('exit', (code) => {
61.         if (disposed && pendingRequests.size === 0) return;
62. 
63.         disposed = true;
64.         rejectPendingRequests(new Error(`scan worker exited with code ${code}`));
65.     });
66. 
67.     return {
68.         buildProjectIndex(rootFile) {
69.             if (disposed) {
70.                 return Promise.reject(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
71.             }
72. 
73.             const requestId = nextRequestId++;
74.             return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
75.                 pendingRequests.set(requestId, { reject, resolve });
76.                 worker.postMessage({
77.                     requestId,
78.                     rootFile,
79.                     type: 'buildProjectIndex',
80.                 });
81.             });
82.         },
83.         async dispose() {
84.             if (disposed) return;
85.             disposed = true;
86.             rejectPendingRequests(new Error('scan worker pool has been disposed'));
87.             await worker.terminate();
88.         },
89.         isDisposed() {
90.             return disposed;
91.         },
92.     };
93. }
94. 
95. module.exports = {
96.     createWorkerPool,
97. };
98.
```

</details>

1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const assert = require('assert');
4. const path = require('path');
5. 
6. describe('createIndexClient', () => {
7.     it('exposes loadProjectSnapshot as the shared snapshot entry point', async () => {
8.         let createIndexClient;
9.         try {
10.             ({ createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient'));
11.         } catch (error) {
12.             if (error.code !== 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND') throw error;
13.         }
14. 
15.         assert.equal(typeof createIndexClient, 'function');
16. 
17.         const rootFile = path.join('project', 'main.k');
18.         const snapshot = { rootFile: path.resolve(rootFile) };
19.         const client = createIndexClient({
20.             buildProjectIndex: async (rootFile) => {
21.                 assert.equal(rootFile, snapshot.rootFile);
22.                 return snapshot;
23.             },
24.         });
25. 
26.         assert.equal(typeof client.loadProjectSnapshot, 'function');
27.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshot);
28.     });
29. 
30.     it('reuses cached snapshots across normalized root aliases', async () => {
31.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
32.         const canonicalRoot = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');
33.         const aliasedRoot = `${path.dirname(canonicalRoot)}${path.sep}.${path.sep}${path.basename(canonicalRoot)}`;
34.         const snapshot = { rootFile: canonicalRoot };
35.         const calls = [];
36.         const client = createIndexClient({
37.             buildProjectIndex: async (rootFile) => {
38.                 calls.push(rootFile);
39.                 return snapshot;
40.             },
41.             getFileSignature: async () => ({ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }),
42.         });
43. 
44.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(canonicalRoot), snapshot);
45.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(aliasedRoot), snapshot);
46.         assert.deepEqual(calls, [canonicalRoot]);
47.     });
48. 
49.     it('rebuilds cached snapshots after invalidation across normalized root aliases', async () => {
50.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
51.         const canonicalRoot = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');
52.         const aliasedRoot = `${path.dirname(canonicalRoot)}${path.sep}.${path.sep}${path.basename(canonicalRoot)}`;
53.         const snapshots = [
54.             { rootFile: canonicalRoot, version: 1 },
55.             { rootFile: canonicalRoot, version: 2 },
56.         ];
57.         let callCount = 0;
58.         const client = createIndexClient({
59.             buildProjectIndex: async () => snapshots[callCount++],
60.             getFileSignature: async () => ({ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }),
61.         });
62. 
63.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(canonicalRoot), snapshots[0]);
64.         client.invalidate(aliasedRoot);
65.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(canonicalRoot), snapshots[1]);
66.         assert.equal(callCount, 2);
67.     });
68. 
69.     it('bypasses stale in-flight loads after invalidation', async () => {
70.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
71.         const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');
72.         const resolvers = [];
73.         let buildCount = 0;
74.         const client = createIndexClient({
75.             buildProjectIndex: async (rootPath) => new Promise(resolve => {
76.                 buildCount += 1;
77.                 const snapshot = { rootFile: rootPath, version: buildCount };
78.                 resolvers.push(() => resolve(snapshot));
79.             }),
80.             getFileSignature: async () => ({ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }),
81.         });
82. 
83.         const staleLoad = client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
84.         client.invalidate(rootFile);
85.         const freshLoad = client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
86. 
87.         assert.equal(buildCount, 2);
88. 
89.         resolvers[0]();
90.         const staleSnapshot = await staleLoad;
91.         assert.equal(staleSnapshot.version, 1);
92. 
93.         const sharedFreshLoad = client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
94.         assert.equal(buildCount, 2);
95. 
96.         resolvers[1]();
97.         const freshSnapshot = await freshLoad;
98.         assert.equal(freshSnapshot.version, 2);
99.         assert.strictEqual(await sharedFreshLoad, freshSnapshot);
100.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), freshSnapshot);
101.         assert.equal(buildCount, 2);
102.     });
103. 
104.     it('rebuilds cached snapshots when a tracked project file changes', async () => {
105.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
106.         const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');
107.         const childFile = path.resolve('project', 'child.key');
108.         const snapshots = [
109.             { rootFile, files: [rootFile, childFile], version: 1 },
110.             { rootFile, files: [rootFile, childFile], version: 2 },
111.         ];
112.         const signatures = new Map([
113.             [rootFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }],
114.             [childFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 200 }],
115.         ]);
116.         let buildCount = 0;
117.         const client = createIndexClient({
118.             buildProjectIndex: async () => snapshots[buildCount++],
119.             getFileSignature: async (filePath) => signatures.get(filePath),
120.         });
121. 
122.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshots[0]);
123. 
124.         signatures.set(childFile, { mtimeMs: 20, size: 200 });
125. 
126.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshots[1]);
127.         assert.equal(buildCount, 2);
128.     });
129. 
130.     it('rebuilds cached snapshots when a tracked project file disappears', async () => {
131.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
132.         const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');
133.         const childFile = path.resolve('project', 'child.key');
134.         const snapshots = [
135.             { rootFile, files: [rootFile, childFile], version: 1 },
136.             { rootFile, files: [rootFile], version: 2 },
137.         ];
138.         const missing = new Error('ENOENT');
139.         missing.code = 'ENOENT';
140.         const signatures = new Map([
141.             [rootFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }],
142.             [childFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 200 }],
143.         ]);
144.         let buildCount = 0;
145.         const client = createIndexClient({
146.             buildProjectIndex: async () => snapshots[buildCount++],
147.             getFileSignature: async (filePath) => {
148.                 if (!signatures.has(filePath)) throw missing;
149.                 return signatures.get(filePath);
150.             },
151.         });
152. 
153.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshots[0]);
154. 
155.         signatures.delete(childFile);
156. 
157.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshots[1]);
158.         assert.equal(buildCount, 2);
159.     });
160. 
161.     it('evicts the least recently used snapshot when the cache size limit is exceeded', async () => {
162.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
163.         const roots = [
164.             path.resolve('project', 'a.k'),
165.             path.resolve('project', 'b.k'),
166.             path.resolve('project', 'c.k'),
167.         ];
168.         const buildCounts = new Map();
169.         const client = createIndexClient({
170.             buildProjectIndex: async (rootFile) => {
171.                 buildCounts.set(rootFile, (buildCounts.get(rootFile) || 0) + 1);
172.                 return { rootFile, files: [rootFile] };
173.             },
174.             getFileSignature: async () => ({ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }),
175.             estimateSnapshotSize: () => 10,
176.             maxSnapshotBytes: 20,
177.         });
178. 
179.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(roots[0]);
180.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(roots[1]);
181.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(roots[0]);
182.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(roots[2]);
183. 
184.         assert.equal(client.getCacheStats().cachedSnapshotCount, 2);
185.         assert.equal(client.getCacheStats().totalSnapshotBytes, 20);
186. 
187.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(roots[1]);
188. 
189.         assert.equal(buildCounts.get(roots[0]), 1);
190.         assert.equal(buildCounts.get(roots[1]), 2);
191.         assert.equal(buildCounts.get(roots[2]), 1);
192.     });
193. 
194.     it('updates snapshot cache stats after invalidation', async () => {
195.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
196.         const rootA = path.resolve('project', 'a.k');
197.         const rootB = path.resolve('project', 'b.k');
198.         const client = createIndexClient({
199.             buildProjectIndex: async (rootFile) => ({ rootFile, files: [rootFile] }),
200.             getFileSignature: async () => ({ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }),
201.             estimateSnapshotSize: () => 12,
202.             maxSnapshotBytes: 100,
203.         });
204. 
205.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootA);
206.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootB);
207.         assert.deepEqual(client.getCacheStats(), {
208.             cachedSnapshotCount: 2,
209.             totalSnapshotBytes: 24,
210.         });
211. 
212.         client.invalidate(rootA);
213. 
214.         assert.deepEqual(client.getCacheStats(), {
215.             cachedSnapshotCount: 1,
216.             totalSnapshotBytes: 12,
217.         });
218.     });
219. 
220.     it('updates the manifest store when caching, touching, and invalidating snapshots', async () => {
221.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
222.         const { createCacheManifestStore } = require('../../../src/core/cache/cacheManifestStore');
223.         const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');
224.         const childFile = path.resolve('project', 'child.key');
225.         const manifestStore = createCacheManifestStore();
226.         const client = createIndexClient({
227.             buildProjectIndex: async () => ({ rootFile, files: [rootFile, childFile] }),
228.             getFileSignature: async () => ({ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }),
229.             estimateSnapshotSize: () => 24,
230.             manifestStore,
231.         });
232. 
233.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
234.         const initialEntry = manifestStore.get(rootFile);
235.         assert.equal(initialEntry.byteSize, 24);
236.         assert.equal(initialEntry.trackedFileCount, 2);
237.         assert.deepEqual(initialEntry.trackedFiles, [rootFile, childFile]);
238. 
239.         await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
240.         const touchedEntry = manifestStore.get(rootFile);
241.         assert.ok(touchedEntry.lastAccessedAt > initialEntry.lastAccessedAt);
242. 
243.         client.invalidate(rootFile);
244.         assert.equal(manifestStore.get(rootFile), null);
245.     });
246. 
247.     it('rebuilds instead of restoring stale manifest entries when invalidated during cache validation', async () => {
248.         const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');
249.         const { createCacheManifestStore } = require('../../../src/core/cache/cacheManifestStore');
250.         const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');
251.         const manifestStore = createCacheManifestStore();
252.         const signatures = [{ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }];
253.         const snapshots = [
254.             { rootFile, files: [rootFile], version: 1 },
255.             { rootFile, files: [rootFile], version: 2 },
256.         ];
257.         let buildCount = 0;
258.         let validationDeferred;
259.         let validateAsync = false;
260.         const client = createIndexClient({
261.             buildProjectIndex: async () => snapshots[buildCount++],
262.             getFileSignature: async () => {
263.                 if (!validateAsync) return signatures[0];
264.                 return new Promise(resolve => {
265.                     validationDeferred = () => resolve(signatures[0]);
266.                 });
267.             },
268.             manifestStore,
269.         });
270. 
271.         assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshots[0]);
272. 
273.         validateAsync = true;
274.         const pendingLoad = client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile);
275.         while (!validationDeferred) {
276.             await new Promise(resolve => setImmediate(resolve));
277.         }
278. 
279.         client.invalidate(rootFile);
280.         validateAsync = false;
281.         validationDeferred();
282. 
283.         const refreshedSnapshot = await pendingLoad;
284.         assert.strictEqual(refreshedSnapshot, snapshots[1]);
285.         assert.equal(buildCount, 2);
286.         assert.deepEqual(manifestStore.get(rootFile), {
287.             rootFile,
288.             trackedFiles: [rootFile],
289.             trackedFileCount: 1,
290.             byteSize: Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(snapshots[1]), 'utf8'),
291.             lastAccessedAt: 2,
292.         });
293.     });
294. });
295.
```

</details>

---

## Phase 4 L2 Disk Cache — Critique

### Orientation: What the Code Actually Shows

Before critiquing the plan on its own terms, three code-level facts matter:

1. **Serialization already exists, split across two files.** `scanWorker.js` has `serializeProjectSnapshot()` and `workerPool.js` has `hydrateProjectSnapshot()apshot()`. The proposed "new serializer module" is a straight extraction, not a net-new design. The plan should say so explicitly to prevent a reimplementation.

2. **`ProjectGraph.toJSON()` / `fromJSON()` already work.** Graph serialization is solved. The serializer module only needs to additionally handle `keywordMap` (Map ↔ entries array), which is also already in the worker code.

3. **`lastAccessedAt` in `indexClient` is a session-local monotonic integer counter** (`accessSequence = 0` resets every extension activation), not a wall-clock timestamp. This has direct implications for the disk LRU design described below.

---

## Blocking Issues

### 1. Non-Atomic Index Writes → Full Disk Cache Corruption Risk

**Issue:** The plan describes a single shared index file for all projects. If the extension host process is killed, VS Code crashes, or the OS shuts down mid-write, the index file is partially written. Since it is JSON, a partial write produces invalid JSON on the next read. The result is not a single project's cache miss — it is the entire disk cache becoming unreadable, with no way to recover individual paylal payload files unless they are self-describing.

**Impact:** High. Windows process kills are common during development. The first time this happens, every user loses their full disk cache silently (or worse, an unhandled `JSON.parse` exception propagates upward).

**Fix:** Every index mutation must use a **write-to-temp-then-rename** pattern within the same directory (ensuring the rename is atomic at the OS level). `fs.rename` is atomic on the same filesystem on both Linux and Windows. Never overwrite the index file in-place.

---

### 2. In-Flight Deduplication Gap When Adding the Disk Read Path

**Issue:** The existing `indexClient` sets the promise sentinel on the `snapshots` map **before** any async work begins. This is what prevents two concurrent `loadProjectSnapshot` callers from both triggering a fresh build:

```js
// loadProjectSnapshot, after the while loop:
const promise = Promise.resolve(buildProjectIndex(...)).then(...);
snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise }); // ← set synchronously, BEFORE awaiting
return promise;
```

The plan says "on L1 miss, attempt disk restore." If the disk restore is implemented as a step *before* setting the promise sentinel—e.g., `await diskStore.tryRestore(rootFile)` then `snapshots.set(...)` only on miss—then between the two async awaits there is a window where a second concurrent caller sees no entry on `snapshots`, also falls through to disk, and both proceed to a fresh build in parallel. The generation + promise sentinel system breaks.

**Impact:** Correctness bug. Doubles I/O and CPU on concurrent callers, and can result in the second build's result silently overwriting the first depending on timing.

**Fix:** The disk read must be wrapped inside the same promise that is set on the map synchronously before any await. The correct structure is:

```js
// After the while loop exits
const generation = getGeneration(rootCacheKey);
const promise = (async () =\> {
    // disk read attempt here — inside the covering promise
    const diskEntry = await diskStore.tryRestore(resolvedRootFile);
    if (diskEntry && await isSnapshotValid(diskEntry, getFileSignature)) {
        // hydrate and populate L1 synchronously before returning
        ...
        return diskEntry.snapshot;
    }
    // fallthrough to fresh build
    const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(resolvedRootFile);
    await diskStore.tryPersist(...); // fire-and-forget or awaited
    return snapshot;
})();

snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise }); // ← set before any I/O is awaited
return promise;
```

The generation check after resolution already handles invalidation during a disk read.

---

### 3. `lastAccessedAt` Semantics Are Wrong for Disk LRU

**Issue:** The current `accessSequence` counter in `indexClient` resets to `0` on every extension activation. Persisting this integer to the disk index and comparing it across sessions produces nonsense LRU ordering. A project last accessed at counter=500 in yesterday's session compares as *older* than one accessed at counter=1 in today's session, causing incorrect eviction regardless of actual recency.

**Impact:** The disk LRU eviction policy will evict entries incorrectly from day one on any multi-session use case.

**Fix:** The disk index must use **`Date.now()`** (Unix millisecond timestamp) for `lastAccessedAt` at the disk tier. The in-memory L1 can keep its monotonic counter for its own LRU. These are separate tiers with separate ordering needs and must not share the same value.

---

### 4. Index-as-Single-Point-of-Failure Has No Defined Recovery Path

**Issue:** The plan states corrupted entries "should be isolated and deleted without breaking main flow," but if the index *file itself* is corrupted (not just a payload), no per-entry isolation is possible. You cannot read which payload files belong to which projects, so you cannot clean up stale payloads or partially restore the cache. The plan does not define what happens in this case.

**Impact:** Disk storage leak (orphaned payloads), or full-wipe behavior that undoes the cache benefit on every corruption event.

**Fix:** Pick one of two designs and state it explicitly:
- **(A) Self-describing payloads:** Each payload JSON file includes its own `rootFile`, `trackedFiles`+signatures, and `schemaVersion`. The index is then reconstructible by scanning the directory at startup. This is more resilient but requires a startup scan.
- **(B) Accept index loss = full wipe:** Document that index corruption clears all payloads and the directory. Simpler but users lose everything. Paired with atomic index writes (Issue 1), the window for this is very small.

Either is acceptable for a first slice. The current plan implies (B) but does not say so.

---

## Non-Blocking Issues

### 5. Payload Filename Scheme Unspecified — Case-Insensitive FS Collision Risk

**Issue:** The plan does not specify how payload filenames are derived from `rootFile` paths. On Windows (case-insensitive filesystem), `C:\Project\main.k` and `c:\project\main.k` are the same file but would produce different hashes if the path is not normalized before hashing. The existing code already solves this with `getManifestRootKey()` / `getRootCacheKey()` (lowercase on Win32). The disk store must apply the same normalization before deriving the storage key.

**Fix:** Hash the output of `getRootCacheKey(rootFile)` (already lowercased on Win32) rather than the raw path. This is a one-line rule that must be stated in the design.

---

### 6. Large Payload JSON Parse Blocks Extension Host Main Thread

**Issue:** `JSON.parse` is synchronous. A project with thousands of keywords and a large graph (which LS-DYNA projects with deep `*INCLUDE` trees produce) can easily produce 5–15 MB payload files. Parsing these synchronously on the extension host thread causes visible UI freezes.

**Impact:** Non-blocking for correctness but directly user-visible for large projects.

**Fix (minimum viable):** State a hard payload byte limit (e.g., 4 MB) above which disk caching is skipped. The `byteSize` from `estimateSnapshotSize` is already available at write time and the check is trivial. Compression is out of scope — a size gate is not.

---

### 7. `schemaVersion` Mismatch Behavior Is Undefined

**Issue:** The plan mentions `schemaVersion` in the disk entry but does not define: what value it takes (extension version? a hardcoded constant?), where it is checked (index read vs. payload parse), or what action is taken on mismatch.

**Fix:** Define it as a hardcoded integer constant in the disk store module, incremented manually whenever the payload shape changes. On mismatch: delete the stale entry and treat as a miss. This must be documented explicitly or an implementer will skip the check.

---

## Suggestions

### 8. Smallest Robust First Slice

The plan bundles serializer extraction + disk store implementation + `indexClient` integration into a single scope. These have very different risk profiles:

- **Step 1 — Extract serializer (zero risk):** Move `serializeProjectSnapshot` and `hydrateProjectSnapshot` from `scanWorker.js` and `workerPool.js` into `src/core/cache/snapshotSerializer.js`. Wire both files to import from there. No behavior change. Tests verify round-trip fidelity.
- **Step 2 — Write-only disk path (low risk):** After a successful `buildProjectIndex` inside `indexClient`, fire-and-forget a disk write (catch and log, no `await` in the caller's hot path). No read path yet. This proves atomicity and payload integrity before any read-through is attempted.
- **Step 3 — Read-through (highest risk, last):** Add disk restore inside the covering promise block as described in Issue 2. This is the only step that alters the critical `loadProjectSnapshot` path and must have its own test pass before merging.

Jumping straight to read-through/write-through in one step is the highest blast-radius option.

---

## Must-Have Tests Before Writing Any Integration Code

| # | Test description | What it guards |
|---|---|---|
| **T1** | `hydrate(serialize(snapshot))` produces graph with identical `getChildren()`/`getParents()`, `keywordMap`, `missingFiles`, `cycles` | Serializer round-trip — foundation for everything |
| **T2** | Disk store: `write` then `read` returns snapshot with correct `keywordMap` and `graph` state | Basic disk I/O |
| **T3** | Disk store: `read` with a changed file signature (mtime or size differs) returns `null` | Stale detection before integration |
| **T4** | Disk store: corrupted index file at startup → reads return `null`, subsequent writes succeed | No-throw on index corruption |
| **T5** | Disk store: corrupted payload file → that entry returns `null`, other entries unaffected | Per-entry isolation, not global failure |
| **T6** | Disk store: simulate partial write (truncate temp file, skip rename) → next startup reads prior clean entry intact | Atomic write validation |
| **T7** | `indexClient` + disk store: L1 miss with valid disk entry → `buildProjectIndex` is NOT called | Proves the read-through path works |
| **T8** | `indexClient` + disk store: two concurrent `loadProjectSnapshot` on L1 miss → exactly ONE disk read, ONE build | The deduplication gap from Issue 2 |
| **T9** | `indexClient` + disk store: `invalidate()` during in-flight disk read → disk entry bypassed, fresh build result used | Generation-check covers disk restore |
| **T10** | `indexClient` + disk store: LRU eviction from L1 → disk entry survives → next `loadProjectSnapshot` restores from disk without calling `buildProjectIndex` | Proves L2 warm-restore after L1 eviction |

T8 and T9 are the most likely to catch real bugs and the most likely to be skipped. They should be written before the integration code, not after.

<details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. // Intercept require('vscode') before any module tries to load it
4. const Module = require('module');
5. const vscodeMock = require('./vscode-mock');
6. const _load = Module._load;
7. Module._load = function (request, parent, isMain) {
8.     if (request === 'vscode') return vscodeMock;
9.     return _load.call(this, request, parent, isMain);
10. };
11. 
12. const { Range, Position } = vscodeMock;
13. 
14. /**
15.  * Creates a minimal fake TextDocument from a string.
16.  * Supports getText(), lineAt(), lineCount, getWordRangeAtPosition(), and uri.fsPath.
17.  */
18. function fakeDoc(text, fsPath = '/test/file.k') {
19.     const lines = text.split('\n');
20.     return {
21.         getText(range) {
22.             if (!range) return text;
23.             const { start, end } = range;
24.             if (start.line === end.line) return lines[start.line].slice(start.character, end.character);
25.             return lines.slice(start.line, end.line + 1)
26.                 .map((l, i, a) => i === 0 ? l.slice(start.character) : i === a.length - 1 ? l.slice(0, end.character) : l)
27.                 .join('\n');
28.         },
29.         lineCount: lines.length,
30.         uri: { fsPath },
31.         lineAt: i => ({ text: lines[i], range: new Range(i, 0, i, lines[i].length) }),
32.         getWordRangeAtPosition(position, regex) {
33.             const line = lines[position.line];
34.             if (!line) return undefined;
35.             const re = new RegExp(regex.source, 'g');
36.             let match;
37.             while ((match = re.exec(line)) !== null) {
38.                 const start = match.index;
39.                 const end = start + match[0].length;
40.                 if (position.character >= start && position.character <= end) {
41.                     return new Range(position.line, start, position.line, end);
42.                 }
43.             }
44.             return undefined;
45.         },
46.     };
47. }
48. 
49. module.exports = { fakeDoc, vscodeMock };
50.
```

</details>

## Phase 4 L2 Disk Cache — Critique

### Orientation: What the Code Actually Shows

Before critiquing the plan on its own terms, three code-level facts matter:

1. **Serialization already exists, split across two files.** `scanWorker.js` has `serializeProjectSnapshot()` and `workerPool.js` has `hydrateProjectSnapshot()`. The proposed "new serializer module" is a straight extraction, not a net-new design. The plan should say so explicitly to prevent a reimplementation.

2. **`ProjectGraph.toJSON()` / `fromJSON()` already work.** Graph serialization is solved. The serializer module only needs to additionally handle `keywordMap` (Map ↔ entries array), which is also already in the worker code.

3. **`lastAccessedAt` in `indexClient` is a session-local monotonic integer counter** (`accessSequence = 0` resets every extension activation), not a wall-clock timestamp. This has direct implications for the disk LRU design described below.

---

## Blocking Issues

### 1. Non-Atomic Index Writes → Full Disk Cache Corruption Risk

**Issue:** The plan describes a single shared index file for all projects. If the extension host process is killed, VS Code crashes, or the OS shuts down mid-write, the index file is partially written. Since it is JSON, a partial write produces invalid JSON on the next read. The result is not a single project's cache miss — it is the entire disk cache becoming unreadable, with no way to recover individual payload files unless they are self-describing.

**Impact:** High. Windows process kills are common during development. The first time this happens, every user loses their full disk cache silently (or worse, an unhandled `JSON.parse` exception propagates upward).

**Fix:** Every index mutation must use a **write-to-temp-then-rename** pattern within the same directory (ensuring the rename is atomic at the OS level). `fs.rename` is atomic on the same filesystem on both Linux and Windows. Never overwrite the index file in-place.

---

### 2. In-Flight Deduplication Gap When Adding the Disk Read Path

**Issue:** The existing `indexClient` sets the promise sentinel on the `snapshots` map **before** any async work begins. This is what prevents two concurrent `loadProjectSnapshot` callers from both triggering a fresh build:

```js
// loadProjectSnapshot, after the while loop:
const promise = Promise.resolve(buildProjectIndex(...)).then(...);
snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise }); // ← set synchronously, BEFORE awaiting
return promise;
```

The plan says "on L1 miss, attempt disk restore." If the disk restore is implemented as a step *before* setting the promise sentinel—e.g., `await diskStore.tryRestore(rootFile)` then `snapshots.set(...)` only on miss—then between the two async awaits there is a window where a second concurrent caller sees no entry on `snapshots`, also falls through to disk, and both proceed to a fresh build in parallel. The generation + promise sentinel system breaks.

**Impact:** Correctness bug. Doubles I/O and CPU on concurrent callers, and can result in the second build's result silently overwriting the first depending on timing.

**Fix:** The disk read must be wrapped inside the same promise that is set on the map synchronously before any await. The correct structure is:

```js
// After the while loop exits
const generation = getGeneration(rootCacheKey);
const promise = (async () =\> {
    // disk read attempt here — inside the covering promise
    const diskEntry = await diskStore.tryRestore(resolvedRootFile);
    if (diskEntry && await isSnapshotValid(diskEntry, getFileSignature)) {
        // hydrate and populate L1 synchronously before returning
        ...
        return diskEntry.snapshot;
    }
    // fallthrough to fresh build
    const snapshot = await buildProjectIndex(resolvedRootFile);
    await diskStore.tryPersist(...); // fire-and-forget or awaited
    return snapshot;
})();

snapshots.set(rootCacheKey, { promise }); // ← set before any I/O is awaited
return promise;
```

The generation check after resolution already handles invalidation during a disk read.

---

### 3. `lastAccessedAt` Semantics Are Wrong for Disk LRU

**Issue:** The current `accessSequence` counter in `indexClient` resets to `0` on every extension activation. Persisting this integer to the disk index and comparing it across sessions produces nonsense LRU ordering. A project last accessed at counter=500 in yesterday's session compares as *older* than one accessed at counter=1 in today's session, causing incorrect eviction regardless of actual recency.

**Impact:** The disk LRU eviction policy will evict entries incorrectly from day one on any multi-session use case.

**Fix:** The disk index must use **`Date.now()`** (Unix millisecond timestamp) for `lastAccessedAt` at the disk tier. The in-memory L1 can keep its monotonic counter for its own LRU. These are separate tiers with separate ordering needs and must not share the same value.

---

### 4. Index-as-Single-Point-of-Failure Has No Defined Recovery Path

**Issue:** The plan states corrupted entries "should be isolated and deleted without breaking main flow," but if the index *file itself* is corrupted (not just a payload), no per-entry isolation is possible. You cannot read which payload files belong to which projects, so you cannot clean up stale payloads or partially restore the cache. The plan does not define what happens in this case.

**Impact:** Disk storage leak (orphaned payloads), or full-wipe behavior that undoes the cache benefit on every corruption event.

**Fix:** Pick one of two designs and state it explicitly:
- **(A) Self-describing payloads:** Each payload JSON file includes its own `rootFile`, `trackedFiles`+signatures, and `schemaVersion`. The index is then reconstructible by scanning the directory at startup. This is more resilient but requires a startup scan.
- **(B) Accept index loss = full wipe:** Document that index corruption clears all payloads and the directory. Simpler but users lose everything. Paired with atomic index writes (Issue 1), the window for this is very small.

Either is acceptable for a first slice. The current plan implies (B) but does not say so.

---

## Non-Blocking Issues

### 5. Payload Filename Scheme Unspecified — Case-Insensitive FS Collision Risk

**Issue:** The plan does not specify how payload filenames are derived from `rootFile` paths. On Windows (case-insensitive filesystem), `C:\Project\main.k` and `c:\project\main.k` are the same file but would produce different hashes if the path is not normalized before hashing. The existing code already solves this with `getManifestRootKey()` / `getRootCacheKey()` (lowercase on Win32). The disk store must apply the same normalization before deriving the storage key.

**Fix:** Hash the output of `getRootCacheKey(rootFile)` (already lowercased on Win32) rather than the raw path. This is a one-line rule that must be stated in the design.

---

### 6. Large Payload JSON Parse Blocks Extension Host Main Thread

**Issue:** `JSON.parse` is synchronous. A project with thousands of keywords and a large graph (which LS-DYNA projects with deep `*INCLUDE` trees produce) can easily produce 5–15 MB payload files. Parsing these synchronously on the extension host thread causes visible UI freezes.

**Impact:** Non-blocking for correctness but directly user-visible for large projects.

**Fix (minimum viable):** State a hard payload byte limit (e.g., 4 MB) above which disk caching is skipped. The `byteSize` from `estimateSnapshotSize` is already available at write time and the check is trivial. Compression is out of scope — a size gate is not.

---

### 7. `schemaVersion` Mismatch Behavior Is Undefined

**Issue:** The plan mentions `schemaVersion` in the disk entry but does not define: what value it takes (extension version? a hardcoded constant?), where it is checked (index read vs. payload parse), or what action is taken on mismatch.

**Fix:** Define it as a hardcoded integer constant in the disk store module, incremented manually whenever the payload shape changes. On mismatch: delete the stale entry and treat as a miss. This must be documented explicitly or an implementer will skip the check.

---

## Suggestions

### 8. Smallest Robust First Slice

The plan bundles serializer extraction + disk store implementation + `indexClient` integration into a single scope. These have very different risk profiles:

- **Step 1 — Extract serializer (zero risk):** Move `serializeProjectSnapshot` and `hydrateProjectSnapshot` from `scanWorker.js` and `workerPool.js` into `src/core/cache/snapshotSerializer.js`. Wire both files to import from there. No behavior change. Tests verify round-trip fidelity.
- **Step 2 — Write-only disk path (low risk):** After a successful `buildProjectIndex` inside `indexClient`, fire-and-forget a disk write (catch and log, no `await` in the caller's hot path). No read path yet. This proves atomicity and payload integrity before any read-through is attempted.
- **Step 3 — Read-through (highest risk, last):** Add disk restore inside the covering promise block as described in Issue 2. This is the only step that alters the critical `loadProjectSnapshot` path and must have its own test pass before merging.

Jumping straight to read-through/write-through in one step is the highest blast-radius option.

---

## Must-Have Tests Before Writing Any Integration Code

| # | Test description | What it guards |
|---|---|---|
| **T1** | `hydrate(serialize(snapshot))` produces graph with identical `getChildren()`/`getParents()`, `keywordMap`, `missingFiles`, `cycles` | Serializer round-trip — foundation for everything |
| **T2** | Disk store: `write` then `read` returns snapshot with correct `keywordMap` and `graph` state | Basic disk I/O |
| **T3** | Disk store: `read` with a changed file signature (mtime or size differs) returns `null` | Stale detection before integration |
| **T4** | Disk store: corrupted index file at startup → reads return `null`, subsequent writes succeed | No-throw on index corruption |
| **T5** | Disk store: corrupted payload file → that entry returns `null`, other entries unaffected | Per-entry isolation, not global failure |
| **T6** | Disk store: simulate partial write (truncate temp file, skip rename) → next startup reads prior clean entry intact | Atomic write validation |
| **T7** | `indexClient` + disk store: L1 miss with valid disk entry → `buildProjectIndex` is NOT called | Proves the read-through path works |
| **T8** | `indexClient` + disk store: two concurrent `loadProjectSnapshot` on L1 miss → exactly ONE disk read, ONE build | The deduplication gap from Issue 2 |
| **T9** | `indexClient` + disk store: `invalidate()` during in-flight disk read → disk entry bypassed, fresh build result used | Generation-check covers disk restore |
| **T10** | `indexClient` + disk store: LRU eviction from L1 → disk entry survives → next `loadProjectSnapshot` restores from disk without calling `buildProjectIndex` | Proves L2 warm-restore after L1 eviction |

T8 and T9 are the most likely to catch real bugs and the most likely to be skipped. They should be written before the integration code, not after.


---

"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\test\\core\\cache\\snapshotSerializer.test.js\n+'use strict';\n+\n+const assert = require('assert');\n+const path = require('path');\n+\n+const { ProjectGraph } = require('../../../src/core/project/projectGraph');\n+\n+describe('snapshotSerializer', () => {\n+    it('round-trips project snapshots with graph and keywordMap hydration', () => {\n+        const { hydrateProjectSnapshot, serializeProjectSnapshot } = require('../../../src/core/cache/snapshotSerializer');\n+        const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');\n+        const childFile = path.resolve('project', 'child.key');\n+        const graph = new ProjectGraph();\n+        graph.addIncludeEdge(rootFile, childFile);\n+        graph.addMissingFile({\n+            fromFile: rootFile,\n+            fileName: 'missing.key',\n+            filePath: path.resolve('project', 'missing.key'),\n+        });\n+        graph.addCycle({\n+            fromFile: childFile,\n+            toFile: rootFile,\n+            path: [rootFile, childFile, rootFile],\n+        });\n+        const snapshot = {\n+            rootFile,\n+            files: [rootFile, childFile],\n+            graph,\n+            keywordMap: new Map([\n+                ['KEYWORD', [{ keyword: 'KEYWORD', filePath: childFile, lineIndex: 1 }]],\n+            ]),\n+            missingFiles: graph.missingFiles,\n+            cycles: graph.cycles,\n+            stats: { scannedFileCount: 1, reusedFileCount: 1 },\n+        };\n+\n+        const hydrated = hydrateProjectSnapshot(serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot));\n+\n+        assert.ok(hydrated.keywordMap instanceof Map);\n+        assert.deepEqual(hydrated.keywordMap.get('KEYWORD'), snapshot.keywordMap.get('KEYWORD'));\n+        assert.deepEqual(hydrated.graph.toTree(rootFile), {\n+            filePath: rootFile,\n+            children: [\n+                {\n+                    filePath: childFile,\n+                    children: [],\n+                },\n+                {\n+                    filePath: path.resolve('project', 'missing.key'),\n+                    fileName: 'missing.key',\n+                    missing: true,\n+                    children: [],\n+                },\n+            ],\n+        });\n+        assert.strictEqual(hydrated.missingFiles, hydrated.graph.missingFiles);\n+        assert.strictEqual(hydrated.cycles, hydrated.graph.cycles);\n+    });\n+});\n*** End Patch\n"

path.resolve(path.dirname(rootFile), 'child.key')) {\n+    const graph = new ProjectGraph();\n+    graph.addIncludeEdge(rootFile, childFile);\n+    return {\n+        rootFile,\n+        files: graph.missingFiles,\n+        cycles: graph.cycles,\n+        stats: { scannedFileCount: 2, reusedFileCount: 0 },\n+    };\n+}\n+\n+describe('createDiskSnapshotStore', () => {\n+    it('persists and cacheDirectory = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-disk-cache-'));\n+        const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');\n+        const childFile = path.resolve('project', 'child.key');\n+        const snapshot = createSnapshot(rootFile, childFile);\n+        const signatures = new Map([\n+            [rootFile, { mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }],\n+            [childFile, { signatures.get(filePath),\n+            now: () => 1000,\n+        });\n+\n+        try {\n+            await store.persist({\n+                snapshot,\n+                trackedFiles: [\n+ signatures.get(filePath),\n+            now: () => 1000,\n+        });\n+\n+        try {\n+            await store.persist({\n+                snapshot,\n+                trackedFiles: snapshot.files.map(filePath => ({ filePath, signature: signatures.get(filePath) })),\n+            });\n+\n+            signatures.set(snapshot.files[1], { mtimeMs: 999, size: 200 });\n+\n+            assert.equal(await store.restore(rootFile), null);\n+            assert.deepEqual(store.getStats(), {\n+                entryCount: 0,\n+                totalBytes: 0,\n+            });\n+        } finally {\n+            fs.rmSync(cacheDirectory, { recursive: true, force: true });\n+        }\n+    });\n+\n+    it('isolates corrupted payload files without breaking other entries', async () => {\n+        const { createDiskSnapshotStore } = require('../../../src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore');\n+        const cacheDirectory = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-disk-cache-'));\n+ 'a-child.key'));\n+        const snapshotB = createSnapshot(rootB, path.resolve('project', 'b-child.key'));\n+        const signatures = new Map([\n+            ...snapshotA.files.map((filePath, index) => [filePath, { mtimeMs: index + 1, size: (index + 1) * 100 }]),\n+            ...snapshotB.files.map((filePath, index) => [filePath, { mtimeMs: index + 11, size: (index + 1) * 200 }]),\n+          let tick = 0;\n+                return () => ++tick * 1000;\n+            })(),\n+        });\n+\n+        try {\n+            await store.persist({\n+                snapshot: snapshotA,\n+ snapshotB,\n+                trackedFiles: snapshotB.files.map(filePath => ({ filePath, signature: signatures.get(filePath) })),\n+            });\n+\n+            const entryA = store.listEntries().find(entry => entry.rootFile === rootA);\n+            fs.writeFileSync(path.join(cacheDirectory, 'payloads', entryA.payloadFileName), '{bad json', 'utf8');\n+\n+            assert.equal(await store.restore(rootA), null);\n+            const restoredB = await store.restore(rootB);\n+            assert.equal(restoredB.rootFile, rootB);\n+            assert.deepEqual(store.listEntries().map(entry => entry.rootFile), [rootB]);\n+        } finally {\n+            fs.rmSync(cacheDirectory, { recursive: true, force: true });\n+        }\n+    });\n+\n+    it('recovers from a corrupted index file and can persist again', async () => {\n+        const { createDiskSnapshotStore } = require('../../../src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore');\n+ createSnapshot(rootFile);\n+        const signatures = new Map(snapshot.files.map((filePath, index) => [\n+            filePath,\n+            { mtimeMs: index + 1, size: (index + 1) * 100 },\n+        ]));\n+\n+        try {\n+            fs.mkdirSync(cacheDirectory, { recursive: true });\n+            fs.writeFileSync(path.join(cacheDirectory, 'index.json'), '{bad json', 'utf8');\n+\n+     });\n+\n+            assert.equal(await store.restore(rootFile), null);\n+\n+            await store.persist({\n+                snapshot,\n+                trackedFiles: snapshot.files.map(filePath => ({ filePath, signature: signatures.get(filePath) })),\n+            });\n+\n+            const restored = await store.restore(rootFile);\n+            assert.equal(restored.rootFile, rootFile);\n+        } finally {\n+            fs.rmSync(cacheDirectory, { recursive: true, force: true });\n+        }\n+    });\n+\n+    it('evicts least recently used entries when the disk cache exceeds the byte budget', async () => {\n+        const { createDiskSnapshotStore } = require('../../../src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore');\n+        const cacheDirectory = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'lsdyna-disk-cache-'));\n+        const nowValues = [1000, 2000, 3000];\n+        const roots = [\n+            path.resolve('project', 'a.k'),\n+            path.resolve('project', 'b.k'),\n+            path.resolve('project', 'c.k'),\n+        ];\n+        const snapshots = roots.map((rootFile, index) => createSnapshot(rootFile, path.resolve('project', `child-${index}.key`)));\n+        const signatures = new Map();\n+        for (const snapshot of snapshots) {\n+            for (const [index, filePath] of snapshot.files.entries()) {\n+                signatures.set(filePath, { mtimeMs: index + 1, size: (index + 1) * 100 });\n+            }\n+        }\n+        const store = createDiskSnapshotStore({\n+            cacheDirectory,\n+            getFileSignature: async (filePath) => signatures.get(filePath),\n+            now: () => nowValues.shift() || 4000,\n+            maxCacheBytes: 600,\n+        });\n+\n+        try {\n+            for (const snapshot of snapshots) {\n+                await store.persist({\n+                    snapshot,\n+                    trackedFiles: snapshot.files.map(filePath => ({ filePath, signature: signatures.get(filePath) })),\n+                });\n+            }\n+\n+            assert.ok(store.getStats().totalBytes <= 600);\n+            assert.deepEqual(store.listEntries().map(entry => entry.rootFile), [roots[2], roots[1]]);\n+        } finally {\n+            fs.rmSync(cacheDirectory, { recursive: true, force: true });\n+        }\n+    });\n+});\n*** End Patch\n"

{ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }\n+                    : { mtimeMs: 20, size: 200 }\n+            ),\n+            persistentCache: {\n+                async persist(entry) {\n+                    persistCalls.push(entry);\n+                },\n+            },\n+        });\n+\n+        assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshot);\n+        assert.equal(persistCalls.length, 1);\n+        assert.strictEqual(persistCalls[0].snapshot, snapshot);\n+        assert.deepEqual(persistCalls[0].trackedFiles, [\n+            { filePath: rootFile, signature: { mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 } },\n+            { filePath: childFile, signature: { mtimeMs: 20, size: 200 } },\n+        ]);\n+    });\n+\n+    it('ignores disk cache persistence failures and keeps the in-memory snapshot', async () => {\n+        const { createIndexClient } = require('../../../src/client/services/indexClient');\n+        const rootFile = path.resolve('project', 'main.k');\n+        const snapshot = { rootFile, files: [rootFile], version: 1 };\n+        let buildCount = 0;\n+        const client = createIndexClient({\n+            buildProjectIndex: async () => {\n+                buildCount += 1;\n+                return snapshot;\n+            },\n+            getFileSignature: async () => ({ mtimeMs: 10, size: 100 }),\n+            persistentCache: {\n+                async persist() {\n+                    throw new Error('disk write failed');\n+                },\n+            },\n+        });\n+\n+        assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshot);\n+        assert.strictEqual(await client.loadProjectSnapshot(rootFile), snapshot);\n+        assert.equal(buildCount, 1);\n+    });\n });\n*** End Patch\n"

    await writeFileAtomically(indexFilePath, serializedIndex);\n+    }\n+\n+    async function resetStorage() {\n+        await fs.promises.rm(payloadDirectoryPath, { recursive: true, force: true });\n+        await removeFileIfExists(indexFilePath);\n+        await ensureDirectories();\n+        state = { entries: new Map() };\n+        return state;\n+    }\n+\n+    async function loadState() {\n+        if (state) return state;\n+        if (statePromise) return statePromise;\n+\n+        statePromise = (async () => {\n+            await ensureDirectories();\n+            try    }\n+        }\n+        return true;\n+    }\n+\n+    async function evictEntriesIfNeeded(currentState, protectedRootCacheKey) {\n+        while (getStateStats(currentState).totalBytes > maxCacheBytes) {\n+            const evictionCandidates = [...currentState.entries.entries()]\n+                .filter(([rootCacheKey]) => rootCacheKey !== protectedRootCacheKey)\n+ => undefined, () => undefined);\n+        return promise;\n+    }\n+\n+    return {\n+        async persist({ snapshot, trackedFiles }) {\n+            return runExclusive(async () => {\n+ 1. const vscode = require('vscode');
2. const path = require('path');
3. const fs = require('fs');
4. const readline = require('readline');
5. const { LsdynaIncludeTreeProvider } = require('./client/providers/includeTreeProvider');
6. const { LsdynaKeywordIndexProvider } = require('./client/providers/keywordIndexProvider');
7. const { createIndexClient } = require('./client/services/indexClient');
8. const { findAffectedProjectRoots } = require('./core/incremental/fileInvalidation');
9. const includeScanner = require('./core/parser/includeScanner');
10. const keywordScanner = require('./core/parser/keywordScanner');
11. const { createWorkerPool } = require('./worker/workerPool');
12. 
13. const LARGE_DOCUMENT_LINE_THRESHOLD = 100000;
14. const STREAM_SCAN_YIELD_INTERVAL = 50000;
15. const includeDirectiveCache = new WeakMap();
16. 
17. // --- Folding ---
18. 
19. class LsDynaFoldingProvider {
20.     provideFoldingRanges(document) {
21.         if (shouldSkipAutomaticDocumentScan(document)) return [];
22. 
23.         const ranges = [];
24.         let foldStart = -1;
25. 
26.         for (let i = 0; i < document.lineCount; i++) {
27.             if (/^\*/.test(document.lineAt(i).text)) {
28.                 if (foldStart !== -1 && i - 1 > foldStart) {
29.                     ranges.push(new vscode.FoldingRange(foldStart, i - 1));
30.                 }
31.                 foldStart = i;
32.             }
33.         }
34. 
35.         if (foldStart !== -1 && document.lineCount - 1 > foldStart) {
36.             ranges.push(new vscode.FoldingRange(foldStart, document.lineCount - 1));
37.         }
38. 
39.         return ranges;
40.     }
```

<sub>⏱️ 907m 32s</sub>

### ✅ `apply_patch`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

````json
"*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase4_disk_cache_foundation.md\n+# vscode-lsdyna 大文件方案执行记录（Phase 4：磁盘缓存基础设施 / Batch 1）\n+\n+## 1. 当前进度定位\n+\n+到本轮开始时，项目已经具备：\n+\n+1. `projectIndexer` 工程级快照构建\n+2. `indexClient` L1 内存快照缓存\n+3. manifest / 自动校验 / LRU 逐出\n+4. watcher 失效 / batching / 后台重建\n+5. worker 线程执行边界\n+\n+但还有一个明显空档：\n+\n+> **扩展重启后，L1 内容全部丢失，缺少受控的 L2 磁盘缓存基础设施。**\n+\n+因此本轮不直接把“磁盘读路径”一次性切进热路径，而是先完成更稳妥的第一批：\n+\n+1. 抽取共享快照序列化模块\n+2. 建立轻量磁盘快照 store\n+3. 让 `indexClient` 在 fresh build 成功后执行 write-through 落盘\n+4. 让扩展入口按 `globalStorageUri` 安全接线\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. 本轮目标\n+\n+本轮只做 **Phase 4 Batch 1**：\n+\n+1. 新增 `snapshotSerializer`\n+2. 新增 `diskSnapshotStore`\n+3. 让 worker 与未来 L2 共用同一套 snapshot DTO\n+4. 让 `indexClient` 成功构建后 best-effort 写入磁盘缓存\n+5. 让 `activate()` 在有 `globalStorageUri` 时创建磁盘缓存实例\n+\n+本轮**刻意不做**：\n+\n+- L2 read-through 恢复\n+- 启动即热恢复\n+- SQLite\n+- 压缩\n+- 跨进程并发读写协调\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. 设计选择\n+\n+## 3.1 先抽共享 serializer，避免 worker / disk cache 各写一套 DTO\n+\n+在 worker 边界落地时，序列化逻辑实际上已经存在，只是分散在：\n+\n+1. `src/worker/scanWorker.js`\n+2. `src/worker/workerPool.js`\n+\n+如果 Phase 4 再复制一套 disk payload 序列化逻辑，后续极易出现：\n+\n+- worker 能 hydrate\n+- disk cache 不能 hydrate\n+- 两边 payload shape 漂移\n+\n+因此本轮先抽到：\n+\n+- `src/core/cache/snapshotSerializer.js`\n+\n+统一负责：\n+\n+1. `ProjectGraph.toJSON()/fromJSON()`\n+2. `keywordMap` 的 `Map <-> entries[]`\n+3. `missingFiles/cycles` 与 graph 内部数组重新挂接\n+\n+## 3.2 磁盘缓存先做“轻量 JSON payload + index”方案，不引入 SQLite\n+\n+这一步主要是验证：\n+\n+1. 持久化边界\n+2. 目录结构\n+3. 配额治理\n+4. 损坏隔离\n+\n+因此本轮不引入 `better-sqlite3`，而是采用：\n+\n+1. `index.json`\n+2. `payloads/*.json`\n+\n+先把正确性、生命周期和恢复语义做稳。\n+\n+## 3.3 index / payload 都必须原子写\n+\n+磁盘缓存最危险的问题不是“读不到”，而是：\n+\n+> **崩溃时写到一半，把整份缓存元数据写坏。**\n+\n+因此本轮所有 index / payload 写入都采用：\n+\n+1. 先写临时文件\n+2. 再 rename 覆盖目标文件\n+\n+避免直接覆盖式写入带来的半写 JSON。\n+\n+## 3.4 index 损坏时直接清空整个 L2 目录，不让主流程受影响\n+\n+第一批实现里，为了保持简单和稳健，本轮明确采用：\n+\n+- **index 损坏 = 清空磁盘缓存目录并回退为空 store**\n+\n+而不是尝试做复杂的 payload 扫描重建。\n+\n+这样虽然保守，但满足两个核心原则：\n+\n+1. 主功能不能被磁盘缓存拖死\n+2. 损坏缓存必须可自愈\n+\n+## 3.5 write-through 失败必须静默降级，不能反向拖垮 fresh build\n+\n+磁盘缓存只是 L2 加速层，不是主数据源。\n+\n+所以本轮在 `indexClient` 里明确采用：\n+\n+- `persistentCache.persist(...)` **best-effort**\n+\n+即：\n+\n+1. fresh build 成功后尝试落盘\n+2. 落盘失败时吞掉异常\n+3. L1 和主返回值继续成功\n+\n+这样磁盘权限、损坏或偶发 I/O 异常都不会破坏主索引能力。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. TDD 过程\n+\n+### 4.1 先写失败测试\n+\n+本轮先新增四组测试：\n+\n+#### A. `test/core/cache/snapshotSerializer.test.js`\n+\n+要求：\n+\n+1. snapshot 序列化后还能正确 hydrate\n+2. `ProjectGraph` 行为不丢\n+3. `keywordMap` 重新变回 `Map`\n+4. `missingFiles/cycles` 继续与 graph 内部数组绑定\n+\n+#### B. `test/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore.test.js`\n+\n+要求：\n+\n+1. persist / restore 正常 round-trip\n+2. tracked file signature 变化时返回 `null` 并移除 stale entry\n+3. 单个 payload 损坏时只影响该 entry，不影响其它 entry\n+4. `index.json` 损坏后可自动恢复为空 store，并允许后续再次 persist\n+5. 超过磁盘配额时按 LRU 逐出旧 entry\n+\n+#### C. `test/client/services/indexClient.test.js`\n+\n+要求：\n+\n+1. fresh build 成功后会 write-through 到 `persistentCache`\n+2. 落盘失败不会影响主返回值，也不会破坏 L1 命中\n+\n+#### D. `test/extension.test.js`\n+\n+要求：\n+\n+1. 没有 `globalStorageUri` 时不启用磁盘缓存\n+2. 有 `globalStorageUri` 时，缓存目录必须组装到：\n+   - `<globalStorageUri.fsPath>\\project-snapshots`\n+\n+先跑聚焦测试，确认红灯原因准确：\n+\n+- `snapshotSerializer` 模块不存在\n+- `diskSnapshotStore` 模块不存在\n+- `indexClient` 尚未触发 write-through\n+- `createProjectSnapshotPersistentCache` 尚未存在\n+\n+### 4.2 再写最小实现\n+\n+本轮新增：\n+\n+1. `src/core/cache/snapshotSerializer.js`\n+2. `src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore.js`\n+\n+并修改：\n+\n+1. `src/worker/scanWorker.js`\n+2. `src/worker/workerPool.js`\n+3. `src/client/services/indexClient.js`\n+4. `src/extension.js`\n+\n+### 4.3 过程中额外修正的一处边界\n+\n+初版磁盘配额测试暴露了一个真实问题：\n+\n+> 单条 payload 本身就可能大于拍脑袋预估的阈值。\n+\n+因此本轮顺手补齐了更严格的配额语义：\n+\n+1. 优先逐出旧 entry\n+2. 如果只剩“本次新写入的 protected entry”且仍然超额，则直接放弃保留它\n+\n+从而保证：\n+\n+- `totalBytes` 不会长期大于预算上限\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. 实际代码改动\n+\n+## 5.1 `src/core/cache/snapshotSerializer.js`\n+\n+本轮正式把 worker 与 L2 共用的 DTO 边界收口为：\n+\n+1. `serializeProjectSnapshot(snapshot)`\n+2. `hydrateProjectSnapshot(snapshot)`\n+\n+这样后续无论是：\n+\n+- worker message\n+- disk payload\n+- 未来 server/client protocol\n+\n+都可以复用同一套 shape。\n+\n+## 5.2 `src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore.js`\n+\n+新增轻量 L2 store，支持：\n+\n+1. `persist({ snapshot, trackedFiles })`\n+2. `restore(rootFile)`\n+3. `listEntries()`\n+4. `getStats()`\n+\n+并具备以下特性：\n+\n+1. 原子写 payload\n+2. 原子写 index\n+3. tracked file signature 校验\n+4. stale entry 自动移除\n+5. payload 损坏隔离\n+6. index 损坏自动清空恢复\n+7. 磁盘字节预算 + LRU 逐出\n+\n+## 5.3 `src/client/services/indexClient.js`\n+\n+新增可选依赖：\n+\n+- `persistentCache`\n+\n+当 fresh build 成功并拿到 tracked file signatures 后：\n+\n+1. 先稳定写回 L1\n+2. 再 best-effort 调用 `persistentCache.persist(...)`\n+\n+这样即使磁盘缓存失败，也不会影响主快照结果。\n+\n+## 5.4 `src/extension.js`\n+\n+新增：\n+\n+- `createProjectSnapshotPersistentCache(...)`\n+\n+作用：\n+\n+1. 读取 `context.globalStorageUri`\n+2. 组装 `<storage>\\project-snapshots`\n+3. 创建 disk snapshot store\n+4. 若初始化失败，打印 warning 并回退到无 L2 模式\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 6. 真实样本验证\n+\n+本轮继续使用用户提供的真实工程：\n+\n+- 根文件：`D:\\temp\\LSDYNA\\2020-nissan-rogue-v3\\combine.key`\n+\n+验证方式：\n+\n+1. 使用真实工程构建 snapshot\n+2. 持久化到临时磁盘缓存目录\n+3. 再通过 `restore(rootFile)` 恢复\n+4. 核对文件数、关键字组数与 include tree 根子节点数\n+\n+这一轮验证的重点不再是冷/热速度，而是：\n+\n+- **真实工程在 L2 payload 里是否能完整 round-trip**\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 7. 验证结果\n+\n+执行：\n+\n+```powershell\n+npx mocha test\\core\\cache\\snapshotSerializer.test.js test\\core\\cache\\diskSnapshotStore.test.js test\\client\\services\\indexClient.test.js test\\extension.test.js --grep \"snapshotSerializer|createDiskSnapshotStore|persists successful fresh snapshots|ignores disk cache persistence failures|createProjectSnapshotPersistentCache\"\n+npm test\n+git --no-pager diff --check\n+```\n+\n+本轮提交前以上检查均通过。\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 8. 这一步的意义\n+\n+做到这里之后，项目第一次具备了真正的 L2 磁盘缓存基础设施：\n+\n+1. 快照有稳定的跨进程 / 跨介质 DTO\n+2. 扩展已经能把 fresh snapshot 持久化到 `globalStorageUri`\n+3. 配额、损坏、自愈、原子写这些“企业级稳健性”问题已经先被收口\n+\n+而下一轮就可以在这个基础上继续推进：\n+\n+1. `indexClient` 的 read-through 磁盘恢复\n+2. L1 miss 直接走 L2 restore\n+3. 扩展重启后的工程热恢复\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 9. 一句话结论\n+\n+**本轮完成了 Phase 4 的第一批基础设施：共享 snapshot serializer、受控的 L2 磁盘快照 store、`indexClient` write-through 落盘，以及基于 `globalStorageUri` 的安全接线，为下一轮 read-through 热恢复打下了稳定边界。**\n*** End Patch\n"
````

### ✅ `task`

**Reviewing unstaged diff**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "description": "Reviewing unstaged diff",
  "agent_type": "superpowers:code-reviewer",
  "name": "phase4-disk-cache-review",
  "prompt": "Review the current unstaged changes in D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna. Focus on the new Phase 4 disk-cache foundation batch. Context: the repo already has L1 in-memory snapshot caching, manifest metadata, worker-thread project indexing, watcher invalidation, and background refresh. The unstaged diff is expected to add a shared snapshotSerializer module, a lightweight JSON-based diskSnapshotStore under globalStorageUri with atomic writes, stale-signature validation, corruption recovery, and LRU/quota cleanup, integrate best-effort write-through persistence into indexClient, and wire extension activation to create the persistent cache only when globalStorageUri is available. Tests are expected for serializer round-trip, disk persist/restore, stale invalidation, corrupted payload/index recovery, quota eviction, indexClient persistence behavior, and extension storage path wiring.\n\nInspect the ACTUAL current unstaged diff only (not staged, not committed history). Report only substantive issues: correctness, reliability, race conditions, lifecycle problems, or regressions. Ignore style/trivia/nits. If no meaningful issues remain, say so clearly. Provide findings with file/line references and concise reasoning."
}
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>12 lines</summary>

```
I found 3 substantive issues in the current **unstaged** diff.

1. **Important — disk persistence is still on the critical path**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:205-208`  
   - `loadProjectSnapshot()` now `await`s `persistentCache.persist(...)` before resolving. A slow or stuck disk write will delay the first snapshot result and all concurrent waiters for that root, so the new persistence path is not actually “best-effort” from a caller/lifecycle perspective.

2. **Important — invalidation can race with the new awaited persist and return stale data**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:194-217`  
   - The generation/in-flight staleness check happens **before** the awaited disk persist. If a watcher invalidates the root during that await, this promise still resolves the old snapshot to its original caller, even though the cache was already invalidated and a rebuild may have started. That weakens the existing stale-in-flight protection.

3. **Important — extension wiring leaves the disk cache effectively unbounded in production**  
   - `src/extension.js:1084-1086`  
   - `src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore.js:66-83`  
   - Activation creates the persistent store with only `cacheDirectory`. The store’s default `maxCacheBytes` is `Infinity`, so the new quota/LRU cleanup is never actually enabled in real extension usage. Tests cover eviction, but production wiring does not.

I didn’t see other meaningful issues in the actual unstaged diff.
```

</details>

<details>

```
1. 'use strict';
2. 
3. const crypto = require('crypto');
4. const fs = require('fs');
5. const path = require('path');
6. 
7. const { hydrateProjectSnapshot, serializeProjectSnapshot } = require('./snapshotSerializer');
8. 
9. const INDEX_FILE_NAME = 'index.json';
10. const PAYLOAD_DIRECTORY_NAME = 'payloads';
11. const SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1;
12. 
13. function resolveRootFile(rootFile) {
14.     if (typeof rootFile !== 'string' || rootFile.trim() === '') {
15.         throw new TypeError('disk snapshot cache entries require a rootFile path');
16.     }
17.     return path.resolve(rootFile);
18. }
19. 
20. function getRootCacheKey(rootFile) {
21.     const resolvedRootFile = resolveRootFile(rootFile);
22.     return process.platform === 'win32'
23.         ? resolvedRootFile.toLowerCase()
24.         : resolvedRootFile;
25. }
26. 
27. function areFileSignaturesEqual(left, right) {
28.     return left
29.         && right
30.         && left.mtimeMs === right.mtimeMs
31.         && left.size === right.size;
32. }
33. 
34. async function readFileSignature(filePath) {
35.     const stat = await fs.promises.stat(filePath);
36.     return {
37.         mtimeMs: stat.mtimeMs,
38.         size: stat.size,
39.     };
40. }
41. 
42. function cloneEntry(entry) {
43.     if (!entry) return null;
44.     return {
45.         rootFile: entry.rootFile,
46.         payloadFileName: entry.payloadFileName,
47.         byteSize: entry.byteSize,
48.         lastAccessedAt: entry.lastAccessedAt,
49.     };
50. }
51. 
52. function getPayloadFileName(rootCacheKey) {
53.     return `${crypto.createHash('sha1').update(rootCacheKey).digest('hex')}.json`;
54. }
55. 
56. function normalizeTrackedFiles(trackedFiles = []) {
57.     return trackedFiles.map(trackedFile => ({
58.         filePath: resolveRootFile(trackedFile.filePath),
59.         signature: {
60.             mtimeMs: trackedFile.signature.mtimeMs,
61.             size: trackedFile.signature.size,
62.         },
63.     }));
64. }
65. 
66. function createDiskSnapshotStore({
67.     cacheDirectory,
68.     getFileSignature = readFileSignature,
69.     now = Date.now,
70.     maxCacheBytes = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY,
71.     schemaVersion = SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION,
72. } = {}) {
73.     if (typeof cacheDirectory !== 'string' || cacheDirectory.trim() === '') {
74.         throw new TypeError('createDiskSnapshotStore requires a cacheDirectory path');
75.     }
76.     if (typeof getFileSignature !== 'function') {
77.         throw new TypeError('createDiskSnapshotStore requires getFileSignature to be a function');
78.     }
79.     if (typeof now !== 'function') {
80.         throw new TypeError('createDiskSnapshotStore requires now to be a function');
81.     }
82.     if (typeof maxCacheBytes !== 'number' || Number.isNaN(maxCacheBytes) || maxCacheBytes <= 0) {
83.         throw new TypeError('createDiskSnapshotStore requires maxCacheBytes to be a positive number');
84.     }
85. 
86.     const resolvedCacheDirectory = path.resolve(cacheDirectory);
87.     const indexFilePath = path.join(resolvedCacheDirectory, INDEX_FILE_NAME);
88.     const payloadDirectoryPath = path.join(resolvedCacheDirectory, PAYLOAD_DIRECTORY_NAME);
89.     let state = null;
90.     let statePromise = null;
91.     let mutationChain = Promise.resolve();
92. 
93.     async function ensureDirectories() {
94.         await fs.promises.mkdir(payloadDirectoryPath, { recursive: true });
95.     }
96. 
97.     async function writeFileAtomically(targetPath, content) {
98.         const tempPath = `${targetPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(16).slice(2)}`;
99.         await fs.promises.writeFile(tempPath, content, 'utf8');
100.         await fs.promises.rename(tempPath, targetPath);
101.     }
102. 
103.     async function removeFileIfExists(filePath) {
104.         try {
105.             await fs.promises.rm(filePath, { force: true });
106.         } catch (error) {
107.             if (error.code !== 'ENOENT') throw error;
108.         }
109.     }
110.
```

</details>


### 💬 Copilot

I found 3 substantive issues in the current unstaged diff:

1. **Important — persistence is still on the request critical path**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:205-208`  
   - `loadProjectSnapshot()` now `await`s `persistentCache.persist(...)` before returning the fresh snapshot. If disk I/O is slow/hung, callers and coalesced waiters are delayed, so this is not truly best-effort from a lifecycle/perf standpoint.

2. **Important — invalidate/rebuild can race with the awaited persist and still return stale data**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:194-217`  
   - The generation/in-flight guard is checked **before** the awaited disk write. If a watcher invalidates the root during that await, this promise can still resolve the old snapshot to its original caller after invalidation/rebuild has already started.

3. **Important — quota/LRU cleanup is not actually enabled in normal extension wiring**  
   - `src/extension.js:1084-1086`  
   - `src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore.js:66-83`  
   - `createDiskSnapshotStore()` defaults `maxCacheBytes` to `Infinity`, and activation only passes `cacheDirectory`. So eviction is tested, but production usage is effectively unbounded unless another layer injects a limit.

I didn’t see other meaningful correctness/reliability issues 

### 💬 Copilot

I found 3 substantive issues in the current unstaged diff:

1. **Important — persistence is still on the request critical path**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:205-208`  
   - `loadProjectSnapshot()` now `await`s `persistentCache.persist(...)` before returning the fresh snapshot. If disk I/O is slow/hung, callers and coalesced waiters are delayed, so this is not truly best-effort from a lifecycle/perf standpoint.

2. **Important — invalidate/rebuild can race with the awaited persist and still return stale data**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:194-217`  
   - The generation/in-flight guard is checked **before** the awaited disk write. If a watcher invalidates the root during that await, this promise can still resolve the old snapshot to its original caller after invalidation/rebuild has already started.

3. **Important — quota/LRU cleanup is not actually enabled in normal extension wiring**  
   - `src/extension.js:1084-1086`  
   - `src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore.js:66-83`  
   - `createDiskSnapshotStore()` defaults `maxCacheBytes` to `Infinity`, and activation only passes `cacheDirectory`. So eviction is tested, but production usage is effectively unbounded unless another layer injects a limit.

I didn’t see other meaningful correctness/reliability issues in the unstaged diff.

"*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: D:\\Project\\vscode-lsdyna\\.github\\doc\\large_file_execution_record_phase4_disk_cache_foundation.md\n@@\n ## 3.5 write-through 失败必须静默降级，不能反向拖垮 fresh build\n \n 磁盘缓存只是 L2 加速层，不是主数据源。\n \n 所以本轮在 `indexClient` 里明确采用：\n \n - `persistentCache.persist(...)` **best-effort**\n \n 即：\n \n 1. fresh build 成功后尝试落盘\n-2. 落盘失败时吞掉异常\n-3. L1 和主返回值继续成功\n+2. 落盘通过 fire-and-forget 方式异步执行，不阻塞主返回值\n+3. 落盘失败时吞掉异常\n+4. L1 和主返回值继续成功\n \n 这样磁盘权限、损坏或偶发 I/O 异常都不会破坏主索引能力。\n+\n+## 3.6 生产接线必须默认带配额，不能把无限缓存只留在测试里\n+\n+为了避免“测试里验证了 LRU，但真实扩展接线却默认无限容量”的落差，\n+本轮在扩展侧给 `createProjectSnapshotPersistentCache(...)` 明确配置了默认配额：\n+\n+- **256 MB**\n+\n+这样生产环境从第一天开始就具备基础空间治理，而不是把上限控制留到未来再补。\n@@\n 作用：\n \n 1. 读取 `context.globalStorageUri`\n 2. 组装 `<storage>\\project-snapshots`\n-3. 创建 disk snapshot store\n-4. 若初始化失败，打印 warning 并回退到无 L2 模式\n+3. 以默认 256 MB 配额创建 disk snapshot store\n+4. 若初始化失败，打印 warning 并回退到无 L2 模式\n*** End Patch\n"
```

</details>

A completion notification will arrive as a new turn; no need to poll or redo its work.

Completed turns:
[Turn 0]
I found 3 substantive issues in the current unstaged diff:

1. **Important — persistence is still on the request critical path**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:205-208`  
   - `loadProjectSnapshot()` now `await`s `persistentCache.persist(...)` before returning the fresh snapshot. If disk I/O is slow/hung, callers and coalesced waiters are delayed, so this is not truly best-effort from a lifecycle/perf standpoint.

2. **Important — invalidate/rebuild can race with the awaited persist and still return stale data**  
   - `src/client/services/indexClient.js:194-217`  
   - The generation/in-flight guard is checked **before** the awaited disk write. If a watcher invalidates the root during that await, this promise can still resolve the old snapshot to its original caller after invalidation/rebuild has already started.

3. **Important — quota/LRU cleanup is not actually enabled in normal extension wiring**  
   - `src/extension.js:1084-1086`  
   - `src/core/cache/diskSnapshotStore.js:66-83`  
   - `createDiskSnapshotStore()` defaults `maxCacheBytes` to `Infinity`, and activation only passes `cacheDirectory`. So eviction is tested, but production usage is effectively unbounded unless another layer injects a limit.

I didn’t see other meaningful correctness/reliability issues in the unstaged diff.
```

</details>
